title: Padu Padan Coffee & Eatery
name: Sindhu Wahyu
text:
Padu Padan Coffee and Eatery - Duren Sawit

Akhirnya, saya jalan-jalan lagi ke Jakarta Timur untuk datang ke salah satu coffeeshop yang masih tetmasuk baru dan sudah masuk dalam wishlist untuk saya kunjungi. Padu Padan Coffee ini letaknya persis di pinggir jalan dan facade depannya seperti standar coffeeshop area JakTim. Namun saya suka konsep indoornya, interiornya homey dengan lambang gajah yang penuh arti filosofi bersahabat dengan manusia dan spesialnya adalah meja barnya yang tipe meja slowbar, di mana kita dapat duduk dan bercengkrama dengan baristanya. Untuk seating areanya ada di outdoor bagian depan, indoor dan semi outdoor di bagian belakang.
Untuk menu cenderung lengkap, saya pesan Magic dari perpaduan beans Brazilian dan Kintamani Bali, rasanya lite, creamy dan cenderung fruity. Saya suka rasa magicnya. Selain itu saya juga merasakan racikan manual brew mereka, ringan dan khas fruity manis. Double like dari saya untuk rasanya magic dan manual brewnya. Untuk makanan saya memesan nasi goreng, lumayan enak meski bukan favorit saya.
Overall, Padu Padan Coffee ini menjadi salah satu standar coffeeshop dengan sajian kopi enak di area JakTim.

PS : mereka mempunyai acara Omakase lho setiap weekend dengan 2 sesi.

title: Padu Padan Coffee & Eatery
name: Nakamura Yuki
text:
Kafe ini menghadirkan perpaduan sempurna antara kenyamanan, estetika, dan cita rasa. Desain interiornya dibuat dengan konsep modern minimalis yang menonjolkan nuansa hangat dan tenang, menciptakan suasana ideal untuk bekerja, bersantai, atau berbincang santai. Setiap detail — mulai dari pencahayaan lembut, kebersihan ruangan, hingga pilihan furnitur yang ergonomis — menunjukkan perhatian terhadap pengalaman pelanggan.

Menu yang ditawarkan juga beragam, mulai dari kopi berkualitas tinggi yang disajikan dengan teknik penyeduhan profesional hingga makanan ringan dan hidangan utama yang disajikan dengan presentasi menarik. Cita rasanya seimbang dan konsisten, menunjukkan standar kualitas yang terjaga.

Pelayanan menjadi nilai tambah tersendiri. Stafnya tanggap, sopan, dan memiliki pengetahuan yang baik tentang menu, sehingga mampu memberikan rekomendasi sesuai selera pengunjung.

Secara keseluruhan, kafe ini layak menjadi destinasi pilihan bagi siapa pun yang mencari tempat dengan suasana nyaman, pelayanan profesional, dan kualitas menu yang memuaskan.

title: Padu Padan Coffee & Eatery
name: Hana Fauziyah
text: Pertama kali nyobain konsep omakase tp bukan makanan, kopi! Yes, omakase kopi! Awalnya ragu krn punya riwayat asam lambung, tp surprisingly kopi2nya friendly di lambung! Tema omakase hr ini aromatik and the coffee speaks through it! Dikasih 2 jenis kopi, 1 bite& 1 special signature (omg ini enak paraah🥺). 2kopi pertama aromatik bgtt, tp bwt aku juaranya yg signature coffee: flower cake! Ini enaaaak bangettt!!! Unlike any other coffee I've ever tasted. Rasanya creamy, not too sweet, aromatic. Minumnya dipadanin sm earl gray infused cake and boom! Enaak bgttt. Tapi menu special ini cm bisa dinikmatin kalo ikutan omakase mereka (booking lwt ig nya @padupadancoffee). Lokasinya gak jauh dr keluar tol, pinggir jalan persis. Kudos jg bwt baristanya yg ngejelasin tiap kopi dgn ramaah..

Rekomendasi makanan buat yang mau nongkrong bareng temen, belinya pizza, sandwich atau kentang goreng bisa buat di sharing. title: Clay Coffee Space
name: Andi Mochammad Fadhil
text:
Merasa gerah sama coffee shop yang kecil? nongkrong disini solusinya, disediain tempat yang sangat luas dan anti gerah!

Parkiran gimana? luas juga sob!
Aman gak? aman banget ada banyak juru parkir yang standby menjaga coffee shop ini.

Coffee shop paling luas di skidraw mungkin meskipun secara letak gak di rawamangun banget tapi ini salah satu opsi yang gue pilih buat nongkrong sama temen-temen gue.

Setiap kesini selalu ada live musicnya, sayangnya gue gatau apakah terjadwal apa memang setiap hari, tapi ini poin plusnya sehingga bisa enjoy bareng temen-temen, dan yang punya suara bagus juga boleh langsung ikut nyanyi.

Rasa kopi susu enak, americano enak, pokoknya coffee shop ini udah gue nobatkan coffee shop paling pewe dan kopinya enak dan kalo rokok kalian habis langsung aja ke cashier karena mereka jual jd asupan teman kopinya tetep ada.

Cuma satu kurangnya, harganya sedikit mahal tapi semua terbayarkan dengan fasilitas yang ada disini dan yang pasti pemandangannya segar😂

title: Clay Coffee Space
name: Habibie Nugroho
text:
Clay Coffee Space: Surga Kecil Pecinta Kopi di Jatinegara Kaum

Clay Coffee Space bukan sekadar kedai kopi biasa. Dengan desain interior yang hangat dan nyaman, tempat ini menawarkan pengalaman bersantap kopi yang tak terlupakan. Setiap sudut ruangan terasa begitu intim dan cocok untuk berbagai aktivitas, mulai dari bekerja sendirian, bertemu teman, hingga sekadar bersantai menikmati buku kesukaan.

Salah satu daya tarik utama Clay Coffee Space adalah pilihan kopinya yang beragam. Mulai dari espresso klasik hingga berbagai macam kopi filter, semuanya disajikan dengan penuh perhatian dan menggunakan biji kopi berkualitas tinggi. Bagi yang ingin mencoba sesuatu yang berbeda, kedai ini juga menawarkan menu minuman non-kopi seperti teh, cokelat panas, dan berbagai macam jus segar.
Selain kopi, Clay Coffee Space juga menyajikan aneka pilihan makanan ringan dan pastry yang lezat. Mulai dari sandwich, salad, hingga kue-kue homemade, semuanya dibuat dengan bahan-bahan segar dan berkualitas. Kombinasi antara kopi yang nikmat dan makanan yang lezat membuat Clay Coffee Space menjadi tempat yang sempurna untuk menikmati waktu santai bersama teman atau keluarga.

Pelayanan di Clay Coffee Space juga patut diacungi jempol. Barista-barista yang ramah dan berpengalaman selalu siap membantu pelanggan dalam memilih kopi yang sesuai dengan selera. Mereka juga sangat berpengetahuan tentang kopi dan dengan senang hati berbagi informasi tentang proses pembuatan kopi.

Alasan untuk mengunjungi Clay Coffee Space:
* Kopi berkualitas: Pilihan kopi yang beragam dan disajikan dengan sempurna.
* Atmosfer yang nyaman: Desain interior yang hangat dan inviting.
* Makanan lezat: Aneka pilihan makanan ringan dan pastry yang menggugah selera.
* Pelayanan yang ramah: Barista-barista yang selalu siap membantu.
* Harga yang terjangkau: Harga yang ditawarkan cukup bersaing dengan kedai kopi lainnya.

Jika Anda mencari tempat yang nyaman untuk menikmati secangkir kopi sambil bersantai, Clay Coffee Space adalah pilihan yang tepat. Dengan suasana yang menyenangkan, kopi yang nikmat, dan makanan yang lezat, kedai kopi ini berhasil memikat hati para pencinta kopi di Jakarta Timur.

Tips:
* Datang lebih awal pada akhir pekan untuk menghindari antrian.
* Cobalah menu kopi spesial yang ditawarkan oleh kedai.
* Jangan lupa untuk mencoba makanan ringan dan pastry mereka.

title: Clay Coffee Space
name: Iknan Muhammad
text:
Selamat malam kak, mohon maaf sebelumnya, saya ingin menyampaikan beberapa keluhan kepada anda selaku owner Clay Coffespace.

Mohon SOP nya untuk lebih di tingkatkan lagi, Khususnya bagian koordinator lapangan mohon untuk lebih fokus dan teliti juga. Saya ada acara bukber di tanggal 16 dan sudah reservasi di tanggal 12, dan kita datang dari jam set 4 sore dan makanan datangnya lama di jam 7an dan sudah dingin. Ada beberapa karyawannya menurut saya sangat kurang ramah, saya memaklumi mungkin karena lelah dan posisi puasa dan banyak orderan, menurut saya untuk urutan dari pesanan para konsumen benar benar tidak berurutan khususnya pesanan kita yang sudah reservasi jauh" hari dan datang duluan di jam 4 sore dibandingkan customer  lainnya. Saya sangat kecewa karena kita datang lebih dulu tidak segera di layani namun yang baru datang  sudah diberikan sajian bahkan tepat waktu saat adzan berkumandang.
Saya dan teman" saya reservasi di tanggal 12 untuk bukber tanggal 16, karena sudah di acc maka saya menyatakan bahwa pihak kopi clay menyanggupi pesanan kami di hari itu dan kami sepakat untuk melangsungkan kegiatan bukber di clay coffe space tsb, bukankah yang reservasi di jauh" hari harusnya lebih di dulukan ya?

Makanan di pesan untuk disajikan sekitar jam 7 menjelang adzan isya, dan datanh telat"nya jam 8 lewat untuk bbrp menu makan dan minum yang belum tiba. Ada juga karyawan anda yang sangat tidak ramah, saya ulangi karyawan anda TIDAK RAMAH karena kami yang kecewa reservasi jauh" hari dan ada telat tiba makanan dan minuman hingga jam 8 bukannya minta maaf malah banting mangkok. Cukup sekali aja kesana dan kapok untuk pelayanannya, Terimakasiii 🙏🏻

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: Marcellino Indrawan
text:
An unassuming gem for matcha lovers.  Reasonably priced.

I love the deliberate process here: you pick your base (Matcha or Houjicha) and then choose how you want it prepared, whether as Usucha (pure with water), a Latte, or other variations. It’s great to see oat milk (extra charge) and low-fat options available for customisation.

The ambiance is quiet and understated, making it a perfect spot to pause. Service was excellent, and we were even surprised with a complimentary mini matcha soft serve, which was delicious.

The verdict on the drinks:
• Houjicha: Distinctly nutty with a smooth, cacao-like finish.
• Matcha: Fresh with deep umami notes.
High quality without the pretension.

Will definitely be coming back.

Such a comfy and cozy little gem! The atmosphere is super relaxing—perfect for chilling with a book, catching up with friends, or just enjoying some “me time.”

I tried their matcha cake and wow… it was so good! Rich in matcha flavor, not too sweet, and perfectly moist. You can really taste the quality.

If you're a matcha lover (or even just matcha-curious), this place is definitely worth a visit. Great vibes, great cake, great matcha = matcha made in heaven! 😌✨

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: Mr. Oktavianus
text: A hidden gem with friendly staffs and peaceful vibes and also suitable to work while enjoying good quality matcha. Ogura matcha cake was delicious, especially the ogura paste and matcha cream, not overly sweet but a bit too dry and need to be more fluffy. Matcha latte was thick and bold, a bit bitter sensation from matcha. It was pairing very well with the fresh milk. As for matcha espresso, it might not the best matcha espresso for me, but still good and worth to try.

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: Joycerlyn Chancellor
text:
Meiko matcha is really lovely, it has a very well balanced umami and bitterness with clean aftertaste. There’s a hint of fresh grassiness, nothing astringent. Color wise, it looks very green and portion is not too bad. It’s not too petite, not too much, just right for a regular size and the price isn’t too bad. You can also request for sweetness level or no sweetener at all. In my opinion, this is so much better than the popular matcha chain “FM”.

The place has a nice ambiance, very calm and WFC friendly. Plus there are more beverages and desserts, really loving the concept here.

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: Obed Timotius
text: I would rate their ambience 9/10! If you plan to sit around and open your laptop, do your stuff and enjoy your time! Seats were full but somehow it was not noisy at all. It was good taste-wise, nonetheless, there’s always room for improvement, though—but it was MORE than “just okay”! 👍🏻

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: The Duchess of Culinary
text:
Gincha Matcha House is located inside Metro Park Residence. There are indoor and outdoor area. They serve variant of matcha drinks, fruit tea, and coffee. For the matcha, Gincha provides Meiko (more savory, less bitter) and Haru (more bitter, less savory) that we could choose. Beside that, they also serve pastries and cakes.

When we came here on Sunday around 5 PM, it was so crowded. But after around 15 minutes, finally we got a seat. Since they close at 8 PM, I suggest you to come earlier, so that the cakes and pastries are still complete.

Here are what I tried :

Akai Ichigo with Meiko Matcha (IDR 38,000)
A cup of drink that consists of matcha, fresh milk, along with the strawberry puree and small bubble. I requested it without sugar. Since I chose Meiko Matcha, it doesn't bitter at all. Overall, this drink is quite good. The strawberry puree suits well with the matcha. I suggest you not to stir this drink at all, so that you can enjoy it to the max 😁

Matchamisu (IDR 25,000)
A soft dessert that consists of matcha cake, tiramisu, and matcha powder on the top. It tastes less sweet. Suitable to accompany the matcha drink.

title: Gincha Matcha House
name: Fannie Huang
text:
Matcha house yang satu ini beneran hidden gem. Lokasinya ada di dalam apartemen Metro Park Residence, lokasinya di ujung gitu.

Tempatnya buka mulai dari jam 8 pagi - 8 malam. Seating areanya ada indoor & outdoor yang nyaman buat WFC karena banyak stop kontak.

Buat menu mereka, ada beberapa jenis kue, mulai dari kue matcha sampai coklat. Selain itu mereka juga jual beberapa jenis croissant.

Pas aku datang pagi ini, lagi ada promo all day gitu. Jadi kalau pesen minuman matcha & croissant diskon 10% jam berapa aja. Buat payment di sini semuanya cashless.

Buat matcha mereka ada 2 macan dan ga ada additional charge. Pilihannya :

- Meiko, rasanya lebih gurih umami dan nggak gitu pahit. Katanya, ini favorit kebanyakan orang.

- Haru, yang ini lebih cenderung pahit ketimbang gurih.

Aku cobain minuman & makanan di sini buat take away. Aku cobain :

1. Iced Latte Regular (IDR 32k)

Jujur banget, sesudah cobain Matcha B*e, F**l M*tch*, terus matcha di sini, aku jadi makin ngerasa F**l M*tch* overrated banget banget banget.

Aku pilih iced latte yang haru karena aku suka green tea yang pahit. Gulanya aku pilih 25% ice normal.

Sesuai klaimnya, matchanya emang ceremonial grade. Rasa matchanya pekat dan cenderung pahit Meski 25% tapi ga kerasa manis.

Rasa matchanya tuh kerasa banget. Sama sekali nggak kerasa too milky. Meanwhile aku juga pesen iced matcha latte gula 25% di F**l M"tch* PIK, rasanya too milky 😭

Ini jauh lebih worth kalau mau ke matcha house affordable.

2. Double Matcha with Ogura Roll (IDR 35k)

Pas dimakan dingin, bolunya agak keras dan ini wajar. Tapi pas udah suhu ruang, bolunya jadi lebih lembut.

Krimnya beneran enak. Rasa matcha yang agak pahit kerasa, kacang merahnya manis gurih ❤️

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Erwin
text:
I was honestly surprised to find a café like this in Jakarta — a real urban jungle in the middle of all the concrete.

From the moment you step inside, it feels like entering a forest. There are so many trees and greenery that it almost feels like you’re dining in the middle of nature, not in a busy city. The atmosphere is very calming and refreshing, especially if you’re tired of Jakarta’s usual crowds and traffic.

They have both indoor and outdoor seating. The indoor area is air-conditioned and comfortable, while the outdoor area is equipped with fans, so it’s still pleasant and not too hot. What really stands out is that smoking is not allowed even in the outdoor area, which makes this place very friendly and safe for non-smokers. You can enjoy your food and have a proper conversation without worrying about cigarette smoke.

As for the food — to be honest, it’s good, but not something that completely blows you away. The taste is enjoyable and well-prepared, but not particularly special or memorable. I think the unique atmosphere and concept of the place play a big role in the overall experience, while the food itself is more on the “nice but not extraordinary” side.

Overall, this café is definitely worth visiting for the ambience, comfort, and non-smoking environment. A great spot to relax, chat, or just enjoy a peaceful break in the middle of Jakarta’s urban chaos.

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Lina J
text:
This gallery restaurant has a unique and vibrant ambience that feels like dining in a lush garden, with plenty of plants adding a fresh touch. The third-floor dining area is decorated with colorful tiles and ornaments, creating a lively and artistic atmosphere. For a quieter experience, there’s a room on the first floor with a long communal table, which many visitors use as a working space.

Food-wise, most dishes are rather ordinary, some even on the bland side. For example, the mie godhog could use more spices, and the rice bowls are nothing particularly special. Among the dishes I tried, the soto mie stood out as flavorful and enjoyable. Snacks, however, are good, from pisang goreng and singkong goreng to a matcha cheesecake that’s worth trying.

The restaurant also uses uniquely shaped bowls and plates, which look aesthetic but aren’t always practical as the unconventional shapes and textured bottoms can make eating a bit tricky and noisy. Outdoor seating adds to the garden-like charm, offering a shaded and comfortable spot for casual chitchat. Overall, it’s a visually delightful spot with some hits and misses on the food, and a calm, garden-inspired outdoor area that’s very appealing.

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Henny K
text:
This place is undeniably aesthetic. There are indoor and outdoor seating areas. For indoor most seating is at one long communal table. It might feel uncomfortable if you’re visiting alone or want to have private talks. The outdoor seating is more flexible and relaxed.

I tried the ceremonial 75. For the price it was pretty basic, I tasted it too grassy and not milky enough for a latte. The spaghetti aglio olio was also nothing special although I do appreciate that they paid attention to my request for extra garlic. BUT the real highlight was the matcha brownie, it was the best thing I had there.

For a place this curated and concept-driven, I expected a warmer and more attentive hospitality. Unfortunately, some of the female servers felt a bit distant and judgmental, idk if it's because came in wearing sportswear or what. The male staff were consistently welcoming and helpful. This isn’t about gender, but purely about the service experience I personally received. A place with such a strong aesthetic deserves thoughtful hospitality.

Verdict: This place is more about the vibe than the taste. Great for ambience, art, and aesthetics, but not recommended if you’re coming specifically for high-quality matcha or food.

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Ailisia Dharma
text:
I had such a nice time at Hakon Ethnic! I ordered the Ceremonial Grade Matcha No.90 (medium latte) and it was really good… smooth, creamy, a bit fruity and floral, no bitterness with a super clean finish. The Spaghetti Carbonara was also delicious, and the portion was huge.

The cafe itself is sooo pretty and spacious. They have lots of green plants, warm fairy lights, and the place is really big with three floors. The whole vibe feels cozy and refreshing.

It’s such a lovely cafe with good food, great matcha, and a beautiful atmosphere 🥺💓💓 Love it!!!

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Michelle Madeleine Indra
text: The place was very nice, very welcoming. All the staff were very warm and helped to create an enjoyable experience. The food was also great, more specifically the matcha brownies and the matcha drinks; the perfect place to have an afternoon snack with friends and family. However, what I loved most about it was the atmosphere and ambiance. Especially when it's raining, it truly feels like you're in the middle of a lush forest and I feel that having this environment surround you while drinking Hakon's matcha is an experience I would definitely recommend!

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Santa Yulian
text: If youre looking for a place with great matcha, a soothing environment, and a unique character, Hakon Ethnic is definitely worth a visitt. their matcha is a musttry. smooth, well balanced, and not overly sweet you can really taste the quality. It pairs perfectly with the peaceful ambiance of the place. I love how the place blends nature with a cozy, artistic touch, and every corner feels Instagram worthy without trying too hard hihi. It’s perfect for working, catching up with friends, or simply enjoying some quiet time. love it definitely coming back

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Karina Safira
text:
I came here with my friend because we’re both big fans of matcha. We love exploring matcha specialty cafes, and Hakon Ethnic looked interesting from the reviews.

The place has three floors — the first floor is quite small with a few tables, a matcha bar (just for preparation), cashier, workshop area, and toilet. There’s also an elevator to the upper dine-in floors, though I didn’t explore them much.

The staff were friendly, but the service felt a bit confusing. We were moved around to different tables several times without a clear reason, which made the experience a bit uncomfortable.

For the food, I had the Smoked Gyudon — it was good, tender, and flavorful, also tried the Matcha Espresso, which was fine but nothing special.. My friend had Chicken Butter Miso and Ceremonial Matcha No.75 (cold whisk), both quite nice. We also tried the Matcha Cheesecake, which was okay but nothing special.

Overall, the food and drinks were decent, but the service needs improvement. We spent around IDR 300,000 in total. Worth a try if you’re curious, but not a must-visit.

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: Angela Guo
text: Hakon has a beautiful ambiance, like stepping into a secret garden. 😍also offer main courses, and the matcha is authentic and delicious. The staff, especially Pak Aston, were very professional and friendly in providing service. You can even learn how to make handmade ceramics. I'll definitely come back again! Thank you~

title: HAKON ETHNIC
name: ocimum kareiflorum
text: Perfect ambience for intimate conversation! It’s highly recommended to make a reservation in advance if you want to be seated on the indoor area. We came for a light dessert, I’d say the matcha cheesecake is something that I will definitely buy again anytime sooner. It was a very perfect balance of sweetness, savory, and a hint of bitterness from the matcha

text: Hakon Ethnic serves some of the best matcha I’ve found in Jakarta.
