title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Len Ka
text: We ordered the pistachio matcha, thought it was too savory for me. The desert here is good, it combines everything I like (warabi mochi, kinako gelato and panna cotta), not overly sweet, the portion is perfect for sharing.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Laurens Surya
text: Good matcha, good food, good desserts, good ambiance. Wifi is fast too. Good for wfc during weekday. Really enjoyed my time here.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Sri Ratna Dewi
text:
The cafe ambience was very nice, there's an island bar at the center and we can see the bartender when they make the matcha and other beverages.

I also ordered Yuzu Cheesecake, the cookie part was too hard, I have difficulty to scoop it. Hopefully the cafe can consider to soften the cookie density.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Littlebeasty9901 “Littlebeasty9901”
text:
If you’re a matcha lover on the hunt for a unique twist, Natsuka is the spot to check out. I fell in love with the ambience the moment I stepped in—calm, cozy, and just the right kind of minimalist that lets you focus on the flavors in front of you.

Their Majicha matcha combo is such an interesting experience. The matcha is rich and velvety, and the hojicha layer? It has this subtle seaweed note—unexpected, but it works beautifully. Not overpowering at all, just enough to make your taste buds go, “Wait, what’s that?” in the best way. If you’re into exploring new flavor profiles, this one is soooo gwuuud.

Plus, everything is beautifully plated, and the vibes are immaculate. Definitely worth a visit if you’re into matcha with a twist.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Yasmine Z
text:
If you’re going by public transport, this cafe located in Urban Forest Cipete is highly accessible via MRT Cipete Raya.

Loved the ambience! Cozy and calm. I went to Natsuka not long after opening hours and there was no crowd. Random, but I really appreciated how thoughtful they were in giving us a box to store our bags :>

Tried their Uji Matcha and loved the presentation. The taste wasn’t too sweet, creamy, and not too strong, with a slightly bitter hint at the end.

Might come back to try another menu!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Claudia Suwuh
text: This matcha place is a peaceful, elegant escape. The room is calm and beautifully designed, perfect for unwinding. Every drink I tried tasted amazing—rich, smooth, and thoughtfully made. A must-visit for matcha lovers.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Ikke Nurhidayah
text:
Natsuka truly exceeded my expectations. I came here specifically to try their signature matcha, and it was absolutely worth it! The matcha had a perfect balance — rich, smooth, and just the right amount of bitterness. You can really taste the quality of the tea they use.

The ambiance of the place is cozy and minimal, perfect for a solo visit or catching up with friends. The staff were also super friendly and knowledgeable about the drinks.

Highly recommend their signature matcha if you’re a fan of authentic Japanese flavors. Will definitely be back to try other drinks and desserts!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Irving
text: The cutest café in South Jakarta is this Japanese gem called Natsuka. Serving specialty Matcha as well as great coffee mocktails with young innovative twist, it is a welcome addition to Jakarta’s cafe scene. Also, you shouldn’t miss the cheesecake, it uses Kiri cream cheese from France.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Tantika Bella Maizura
text: Pro tip sit at the matcha bar for the full experience though on weekends you only get a 2-hour slot. Had the Double Matcha with honey oat and cream and it was smooth earthy and slightly sweet in the best way. The Uji Matcha was deeper and bolder with a clean finish matcha lovers will appreciate. Also tried the warabi mochi with nutty roasted notes but the texture was a bit softer not super chewy.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: alfonsa sheila
text:
We come to try this matcha café located in urban forest and had such a lovely time! First up, we tried the flan cake—super creamy, perfectly smooth, and just the right level of sweetness. It melted in our mouths!

Then came the hojicha cheesecake, and wow—the balance between the roasted hojicha flavor and the rich, creamy cheese was spot on. You could really taste both elements without one overpowering the other.

And the one that we like the most is The Matcha Chezu, their seasonal drink. It's a matcha and cheese combo that might sound unusual, but trust us—it’s absolutely delightful. The deep umami of the matcha mixed with the slightly salty, creamy cheese foam just perfect.

The place itself is cozy and relaxing— and they will tell you hoe long you can stay. Definitely planning to come.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: 봄미레
text: was walking around Urban Forest and spot this cute cafe where matcha is their highlight menu. would love to hop on and try their matcha next time cuz I had my tummy full of caffeine already 😅

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: San San Muliawan
text:
Tried their Matcha Latte and Mont Blanc Coffee

It's inside the Urban Forest complex. The place and ambiance are very nice.

Serve a variety of drinks from coffee to matcha and some cheesecakes and cakes.

Matcha Latte : creamy, nutty, enough hint of bitterness. Freshly brewed.
Mont Blanc : unique blend of cold brew, sweetness from Sakura berry syrup, and hint of orange.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: zharen febriza
text: Best matcha in Town! I order Arashimaya Chizu Matcha and it's soo delicious! Combined of creamy and cheeze matcha 🩷The ambience is so cozy and very comfortable. Many thanks for all the staff who served us today : Ka Naila, Endah, Fajar, Epi, Tyo. Five stars indeed!!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Zezsa Putri Rainadia
text: everything is delicious! absolutely loved how the matcha tasted, i ordered 2 drinks and both were delicious <3 the foods were also very yummy, highly recommend the truffle fries and cheesecake (one of the best cheesecake i’ve ever had!) def will be returning.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Farhania Zuhdiar
text: We random scrolling cafe to meet up around cilandak area, we choose this cafe. Never expected the matcha is so MATCHAA. I mean it isn’t powdery and nature taste 😆

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: anne putri
text:
Such a charming new addition to Urban Forest Cipete! Natsuka Dessert Shop is not only adorable with its thoughtfully designed interior, but it also gives off a calming, zen-like vibe the moment you step in—thanks to the serene zen garden and minimalist aesthetic.

One of the coolest parts? The matcha-making station is right at the center, making the MatchaRista the star of the space—love that detail!

Highly recommend the Houjicha Cheesecake and Flan Cake—both are absolutely delicious and beautifully balanced. They also offer savory dishes for those who want something heartier.

Definitely a sweet spot worth visiting!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Wahyu Jatmiko
text:
Such a cozy and comfortable spot. I absolutely loved the ambiance here. The interior is beautifully designed with warm lighting, natural wooden elements, and calming greenery. The central table with the mini zen garden is a very nice touch because it adds to the relaxing atmosphere.

This is a perfect place to chill, work, or catch up with friends. The soft lighting and minimalist decor give it a very LoFi vibes feel. I could easily spend hours here. The staff were also very friendly and attentive.

I highly recommend this place if you are looking for a peaceful cafe with a great aesthetic and a soothing environment. I will definitely come back.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Brigitta Pandana
text: They just opened a few weeks ago. I saw it on instagram and wanted to come and to try it for myself. I really love the cheesecake, especially the yuzu one.  It’s not too sweet, it’s just nice. Also, tried the Majicha (matcha, hojicha, and brown sugar). Never had one in any other place before. I really love it so much.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Hera Hapsari
text:
We were curious to check this place out since it’s always packed, and people say it’s better than the one at the mall.
Turns out — we totally agree!
We ordered the iced double matcha, hot original matcha latte, and the tiramisu matcha — 100% approved!
Even the french fries were super good, especially with the truffle sauce.
Definitely coming back!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: widya noviani
text: Perfect spot to spend your time alone or with loved ones, chill and cozy ambience in the hearth of urban forest ✨

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Nasha
text:
I’m a matcha enthusiast so I been wanting to try the matcha here. Luckily there was no queue on a Friday afternoon with a few seats left at the matcha bar. However my experience turned sour when I was ignored and the person came after me got offered menu first. When I asked for the menu, the waiter was not even sorry for the mishap and even said, ok what do you want to order? Excuse me, I even got to read the menu yet??

Anyway I just ordered whatever I see first on the menu and left after I finished it. The classic matcha was nice, but they didnt serve the honey. I only knew after I paid that It’s a condiment? Oh well maybe won’t come back too soon.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Michael Wicaksono
text:
A must visit for matcha lovers. Very good matcha and nice ambiance.
Feedback for owner: Need to add some soundproofing since it’s too noisy for a matcha place.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Anisa
text: Double matcha latte was decent. The matcha itself definitely isn’t the bland, low quality kind, but the drink overall tasted quite sweet and milky. The matcha cheesecake has a relatively unique shape but it was dense and somewhat sandy. The taste leaned more towards the richness of the cheese than the matcha flavor.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Yusuf Siregar
text:
Great vibe and super attentive staff.

The seating at the middle of the establishment is perhaps the best cafe seat I've been in a while — you get to watch the "matcha barista" craft ceremonial-grade matchas, illuminated by a stream steady warm lighting all while observing from a comfortable semi-bar desk and chair.

For a self-proclaimed coffee-connoisseur like me, I would consider the latte I ordered as acceptable.

As for the ceremonial grade matcha — which I assume is their specialty — I am in no position to judge whether the taste is considered as top of the class since I'm still green to this whole different game of green tea (even though the 100K price tag is perplexingly expensive).

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Hanna Mulya
text:
Loved Natsuka! 🫶
Such a cozy and comfy vibe.
The matcha is seriously next level!! Wkwk never had anything like it before
Their matcha menu is super unique, way different from other places.
Staff were super nice too.
Definitely coming back.
Totally recommend!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Ika Meuthiah
text:
The matcha experience was great. The nutty taste of the matcha is top notch. They have various options for a good dessert. I personally love the flan pudding.
However, the sitting arrangement is too narrow. You can clearly hear conversations of the next table.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Ahmad Zaki
text: I ordered double matcha, it feels thick and very delicious. highly recommend it for you to try. the place is very comfortable, you can also request to sit at the bar table to see the process of making matcha.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Winda
text: Amazing Uji matcha latte. Place can get crowded but service is excellent. Love the matcha “bar” in the middle, great if you’re coming solo or want to chat with the barista. Price to quality ratio is definitely one of the best.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Winny Wisman
text: A nice comfy cafe with delicious drink especially the matcha, it has some umami and only slightly bitter

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Nida
text: If buying matcha was the reason I could never afford a house, then be it. At least I could say that I’ve lived a fulfilling life of exploring different flavors. Especially if the flavors are from Natsuka’s Sakura berry matcha

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Andrew Lie
text:
Very aesthetic cafe, calming ambience with zen garden here amd there.
Matcha latte and mont blanc are very recommended 👍🏼
Mont blanc : must try !
Perpaduan fruity cold brew dengan a hint of orange bitter and creamy-salty white layer on top, really giving fresh yet gentle sensation on tastebud ! 😍
Not to mention the cafe is surrounded by lush greeneries, giving very chill moment 😊😌 🙏🏼

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Aliyah Hadi
text:
First time I went on a weekday right before close order time, place was empty, service by the barista guy was great. He explained about the drinks and cafe, nice chat. Loved the matcha and pudding so I was sure I’d come back.

My second experience on a weekday  afternoon, was unpleasant. The girl staff who assigned seats didn’t ask me first how many people i bring, instead asked another person who came after me but somehow stood a little bit in front. The waiting line was messy and that person skipped the line but the staff who was supposed to be aware didn’t even care to confirm the waiting line. After several minutes she told me to wait until a seat is available or order for takeaway. We decided to wait.

However, because 20 minutes passed and I still haven’t gotten seated, me and my sister decided to take our orders home. Went out the doors to the “takeaway window”, a couple ordering in front of us. Once our turn came, the staff who was apparently the same ponytailed girl assigning seatings said takeaway orders are on hold and we can’t order! She saw us waiting inside and was the one suggesting takeaways because the dine-in was full yet I was denied ordering?

The situation inside was seemingly quite chaotic and the staffs couldn’t give appropriate assistances or solutions to waiting customers. Despite good matcha and pudding, the service was poor. Would not return for the “ambience” as it was unwelcoming, better order online.

*picture from my first visit.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Meg D
text: Trying their Natsuka matcha latte & uji matcha latte. Love their matcha so much! They have matcha bar with limited seats.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: She
text: Their matcha was good, but the cheese cake also recommended. Located @ Urban Forest, the new building inside the park, this matcha-cafe will be one of a good option to hangout.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Gratia Evelyn Margaret
text: I love all the drinks and desserts I ordered! Honorable mention for the winter berry matcha😍 My mom loved the cheese coffee and the yuzu matcha cake which was unusual of her haha. 100% recommend sitting at the bar because the vibe is just different…

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Muhammad Syarif
text: 1st timer try Dua Coffee, I'd say the cold brew which are mixed with apple juice and hints of natural sweets, are one of the best coffee-based sweet drinks I've ever tasted in Jakarta. While the banana bread/cake is also pleasing to me, especially with the melted butter on the top!

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: M. Nikhlah
text: Best place for meeting, and chit chat.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Arkan Tanriwa
text: Great place to work from, if a little dreary. Most of the place is nonsmoking but I was surprised to find an air conditioned section where smoking is allowed — a rarity these days.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: indra widhi susanti
text: It was Saturday night when I came so the place was packed. Great atmosphere to hangout and work. I like that the price has included the tax and service. Easy to count how much you spend 😂 Love the hot mocha and they offer enough variety of heavy food and sweets as well.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Rahma Julynda (July)
text: Cozy place , good coffee , friendly staff and price. Their classy cheesecake was the best

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Jacob Patty
text: Cozy place.. only too loud for my taste.. still recommended 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Nisa Suciaty
text: Came here on Sunday morning and tried its tasty cappuccino. Great experience!

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Cavin Siregar
text: I love the Petik Mangga menu. It was perfecto! Overall, i think this place is good to be a hangout place with friends. I think only the traffic make it less favorable in the afternoon (yes, it's Cipete street).

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Nabil Iswar Siregar
text: Cozy place, reasonable price, good service

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Arifatun Milah
text: Cozy place to have good cup of coffee. If you're lucky, you'll meet Raisa here cause this place runs by her brother

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Rifqi Rizqullah
text: I've been here a lot, and the cafe latte never dissapoints! Tried their cakes multiple times and multiple flavors yet I'm always ended up happy. Comfortable place and kind service. No wonder they're always having a full house most of the time of the week.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Sentanu Reksalegora
text: Cozy place. Nice coffee and affordable food. Had the black squid rice and it was nice, but the portion was a bit too small. Heads up, it is very crowded around the Cipete road and parking spot is very hard to find.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Joey The Wanderer
text: My favourite coffee joint since 2018!

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: ameermahskah sutoyo
text: Nice place to hang out and have fun with friends. But the food and drink are just okay. The playlist here is weird. You have queen at one point, and suddenly you're hearing high school musical songs.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Neysa Azzahra
text: I ordered the risol mayo. A little bit too oily, but otherwise quite delicious. The mayo flavor is unexpected, it's like mayo and other sauce that I don't recognize (perhaps ranch). But tastes good though.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Rivaldy Varianto
text: Cozy place and nice atmosphere for mini gathering. But to be honest, a bit disappointed with the coffee-based drink. We ordered ice coffee mocha, ice cappuccino, and ice chocolate but all the flavor is too bland. Did not have the chance to taste the food, and will do the next time. But for now, I will give three stars.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Rizki Riza
text:
The place is nice with proper parking space (not so big, but it's in Cipete Raya).
Just tried their signature kopi susu: Kopi Kedua. Taste good, better than most of kopi susu but not as good as its famous neighbour. But hey they got seats and tables inside which are really good things .
The ambience is nice, the crowds are cool. A good place to hang out at Cipete Raya.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: D.A
text: Love the ambiance in this cafe. The coffee selection is great and there are lots of food to try. Their nasi goreng tek tek was nice, especially with lots of pickles. One thing that can be improved is their tea selection. Would love to see more premium tea selection brewed in the cafe 👌🏻🌟. Thank you dua!

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Farah Soraya
text:
Not my first visit. They have lovely taste of food (especially pasta) and manual brew for coffee.
Quite crowded at 19:00 upwards, especially on weekend.
Cupping session per 2 weeks.

title: Dua Coffee @Cipete
name: Hansen Armando
text: Smoking area is cozy af. Try out their quesadillas . Satisfied guaranteed. Also the sold a low cost desserts but with awesome taste. For those who cant drink coffee they had a recommended thai tea latte. Go try it out.

title: Salty Salty
name: Antonia Ego
text:
Loved their salty cream — light, airy and fluffy.
Sad that they’ve changed their green tea from when I visited 2 years ago and I preferred their older version. The new version is still good too.

The Cheese Tart is surprisingly good for a shop that does not specialize in cheesecakes. If you like cheese and rich cakes — you may want to try ordering this.

The Tiramisu is not particularly my favorite, it’s rather dry and feels rough on your mouth. Given that Salty Salty does not specialize in cakes, there’s no reason to order the tiramisu.

title: Salty Salty
name: Mauro Martin
text: Tried the Salty Latte (coffee-based) and the Hikari Green Salty (matcha)—both are standouts. The Salty Latte is smooth and well-balanced, with a rich coffee base that pairs perfectly with the salty cream and burnt marshmallow on top. Creamy, comforting, and not overly sweet, with a subtle smoky note from the marshmallow that adds depth. The Hikari Green Salty (matcha) is just as impressive. The matcha tastes clean and earthy, complemented by the burnt marshmallow that adds a gentle sweetness and aroma. The salty cream ties everything together, creating a layered, indulgent flavor without being too heavy. Both executed really well. Definitely worth ordering.

title: Salty Salty
name: Juliana Hidayat
text: You should try the salty green. The combination of matcha and chessy cream. Perfect!! I trt the salty coffee too. The coffee goes well woth the cheese cream. The croissants are also good but yiu have to ask to heat up couse mine is not heated. I thought it a standard procedure if you dine in then the croissant will be heated up.

title: Salty Salty
name: Alvin
text:
Specialist for matcha,
Recommended the salty green Hikari menu.
The taste is light and the sweetness is slightly…

Place is comfortable and the service is fast.

title: Salty Salty
name: Aldo Widarta
text:
Located at busy area of PIK , its a hype coffee shop.

Tried Salty Latte and Salty Green , all the cream very good , taste salty but still got the balance , smooth texture - flufy but liquid , nice when it mix well with the matcha milk or coffee latte , it will taste on balance- the sweet , tasty , and bitter from the coffee or matcha will blend into nice taste.

The place was so nice , comfort , cold air cond , nice to have a chat there.

👍👍👍

title: Salty Salty
name: Angga Baresky
text: Visited Salty Salty at PIK and really enjoyed the experience. Their matcha is rich and authentic, with a pleasant bitterness that true matcha lovers will appreciate. The place itself has a cozy and relaxing vibe, perfect for hanging out or working. Prices are a bit on the higher side, but the quality makes it worth a try

title: Salty Salty
name: gello milo
text:
I really love this combination, it’s perfectly balanced and refreshing.

The cheese topping is creamy and slightly savory, pairing beautifully with the drinks. What I love most is that the drinks are not too sweet, so the flavors don’t feel overwhelming at all.

Rich yet light, smooth, and very enjoyable.

title: Salty Salty
name: Sabrina Nathaniel
text: Trying it out of hype! Matcha and choco were a good combination, for matcha I feel like there is a hint of nori but the cream cheese is super gooooood 😋🍵🍫🧀

title: Salty Salty
name: Patricia Kheng
text: came at 10am on a friday and there was no one !!! the beverages are soooooo good like i understand the hype?! the salty taste goes so well w all of the 3 flavours!!! their salted caramel chocolate pastry is very delicious too, a must try! their burnt cheesecake and tiramisu is just normal though

title: Salty Salty
name: jkus eats
text: Got here at a nice early Sunday morning where the queue’s nonexistent. The drinks were fabulous and so were the price tags. I couldn’t quite figure out in what way I should drink them but they were wonderful. The place was nice and cozy, with great atmosphere and social media supportive picture taking place.

title: Salty Salty
name: Nelly “Prudential” IG:fininsuranceplanner
text: Cute place with nice drinks and cakes. We ordered matcha latte mori, mochachino, salty choco. All good and creamy 🥰

title: Salty Salty
name: M *
text: This small place is quite crowded, and the ordering process is quite lengthy. The wait for your order is also quite long. Their signature salty latte is a bit too sweet, but the salty foam makes it a bit nicer once mixed together and nicely blended together with coffee. The shakerato is just okay, and it’s also a bit too sweet. The pastries are nothing special and can be skipped.

title: Salty Salty
name: Clarine
text:
Try their salty latte .. for me 9/10,
Matcha burn cheese cake 7/10.. I prefer original one as this is too sweet for me

title: Salty Salty
name: Listania Kristianti
text:
I like all the drinks. Very recommended: salty latte, salty green, tiramisu.
Matcha burnt cheese cake not too sweet, less cheese, very soft but the test not umami either.. worthed to try if you like cheese cake.
Strawberry latte is so so.
I would be come again! Very recommended.

title: Salty Salty
name: Vini Swasti
text: it wasnt packed when we went there, all the staff had nothing to do except for a cashier when we ordered but its unlucky they didnt even greet the visitor that come or when we leave the place either. Guess the coffee shop that way cheaper than this even treated the guest better. We ordered matcha latte and something with their 'salty' foam. They were just ok

title: Salty Salty
name: Dewi Rachmawati
text: ordered a variety of dishes here. flavors leaned a bit bold for my taste, but overall it was still an enjoyable meal, especially for sharing

title: Salty Salty
name: Christie Harris
text:
Drinks lived up to the hype however, if you prefer non-sweet drinks, definitely ask for less sugar. Kouign amman croissant’s texture and taste feels more like a bread (maybe I just got an old batch).

Place-wise, it got great ambience but it definitely could be cleaner and the servers could help with the table waiting.

title: Salty Salty
name: Angelin
text: I ordered the Salty Green (matcha) while my mother ordered the Salty Latte (with less sugar). We both enjoyed and were extremely satisfied with our orders. The cream on top is so smooth, salty, and gives that perfect balance with the bitterness of the coffee/tea. I also love the matcha basque cheesecake. It wasn't too dense, still light to eat and has intense matcha flavor. I don't recommend ordering the cheesecake together with salty drinks cause it will get too heavy. There are 2 floors but the place can get very crowded. The seats on the 1st floor are too close to each other. I highly recommend this place for delicious drinks and cakes, but not for chatting with your friends/family.

title: Salty Salty
name: Mr. Oktavianus
text:
My wife and I accidentally found this coffee shop after we had lunch nearby. Luckily, we got there just in time before it got super crowded.

We tried their signature lattes and flat croissant. Surprisingly, we really loved both of them! The best part was their salty cream - it was so good! It really went well with the flavor of the matcha and coffee. Even though it's a bit more expensive than other cafes, I have to say they are my favorite salted matcha and coffee. And the smashed/flat croissant was pretty satisfying too, crispy and not too sweet.

title: Salty Salty
name: Ellen Brigitta
text: Nice matcha drinks, different from regular matcha that we drink. They use cream cheese instead of regular milk. But it's unique and nice as a variation

title: Café Batavia
name: Amanda Yap
text: Highly recommended. Enjoyed my time here in Cafe Batavia - oldest cafe in Jakarta. It looks and feel like Eastern Europe cafe. Min spending of one main course and one drink for window seat, but we ordered more than that. Food is delicious too.

title: Café Batavia
name: Lidya
text: Visited Café Batavia in Kota Tua Jakarta — truly a legendary-level café. Most of the visitors were foreign travelers enjoying Indonesian delicacies, which really adds to the classic, historic vibe of the place. No need to even question the taste — it’s legit delicious. If you go, you definitely have to try their nasi goreng!

title: Café Batavia
name: M Y
text:
There were many tourists and foreigners, and the prices were a bit high.
However, all the dishes were delicious, and I think it’s a great place for people visiting Indonesia for the first time.
They offer both Indonesian food and Western dishes like pasta.
The atmosphere inside the restaurant is also very stylish.

title: Café Batavia
name: Rufaida Esti
text:
Cafe Batavia is a great spot with a very strategic location, right in the heart of Kota Tua. The atmosphere is warm and charming, with a classic colonial vibe that makes the place feel special and relaxing.

The food is delicious and well-prepared, offering a good variety that suits both local and international tastes. Everything is enjoyable, whether you’re coming for a full meal or just a casual hangout.

title: Café Batavia
name: Annastasya (Kei)
text:
We stopped by in the afternoon for some dessert, and it was wonderful!

First of all, the ambiance was perfect. You can experience an old Indonesian vibe inside the restaurant.

As for the food, I ordered the pandan latte, which was delicious! The bailey's cheesecake was also pretty good.

The service was good too.

title: Café Batavia
name: Ettore Palazzi
text:
This was the last place we visited before leaving Jakarta, and it was a very nice treat. The cafe is 196 years old and an institution for Jakarta.
Morabilia and pictures accross 3 centuries on the walls and everywhere you turn your eyes.
The food and service ar excellent.
A must visit.

title: Café Batavia
name: Habib
text: Beautiful café with very good black coffee. Delicious cakes and pastries. I am grateful for finding a prayer room and ablution just behind the stage. What I really didn't like is that the pictures of naked men and women in the toilets.

title: Café Batavia
name: Tiramisooo Tiramisooo
text:
Café Batavia is a must-visit when exploring Jakarta Old Town.
Once colonised by the Dutch, Jakarta Old Town has preserved its charming 17th century architecture and Café Batavia carries that same timeless vibe. ❤️

We stepped into a beautifully decorated space with floor-to-ceiling windows that let in the perfect dose of natural sunlight.

The menu is extensive and the food is so good. “This is the best Mie Goreng that I’ve tasted,” says me. The springy egg noodle is fried to perfection and not oily at all.

I’m still thinking of the Mie Goreng now. 🥹

#tiramisoooo #tramisoooojiaks #jiakjiakjiak #cafebatavia #jakarta #indonesiafood #miegoreng #jakartaoldtown

title: Café Batavia
name: Jenny Paz
text: Honestly, I don't understand how this place can be so famous. Maybe it's because of its prime location, but otherwise, I can't find any other explanation. The most bland food I've ever had in all of Indonesia. I ordered a tasteless salmon; it tasted like food for a sick person. The dish was described as a delicious mix of vegetables (zucchini, eggplant, carrot), but instead, I received a square of eggplant and a circle of zucchini. I've attached photos. If I hadn't ordered a side of rice, I would have ended up not only disappointed but also hungry. Not to mention, the staff looks at you like you're a poor devil, constantly reminding you that you have to order something or leave. When I went in, I fully intended to order a main course and dessert, but with such poor seasoning, I decided against the dessert.

title: Café Batavia
name: Gery Delano
text:
The Cafe can be visited for lunch or just to enjoy coffee. This restaurant serves a variety of Indonesian, Asian, and Western cuisine, including Indonesian dishes like nasi goreng (fried rice) and rujak crab (crab salad), as well as international dishes and desserts. Coffee, tea, cocktails, and mocktails are also popular.

Cafe Batavia is known for its classic atmosphere and strong vintage colonial decor, complete with period photographs and furniture, and occasional live music. Many visitors come not only for the food, but also for the historical atmosphere and views of Fatahillah Square.

title: Café Batavia
name: Grace Natalia
text: Loved my visit to Café Batavia! The ambiance is timeless — a beautiful colonial-style building filled with character and history. The food and drinks were excellent, with great presentation and rich flavors. Service was warm and professional, making the whole experience feel special. It’s the perfect place to enjoy a relaxed meal while soaking in the old-town charm of Kota Tua. Definitely a must-visit when in Jakarta.

title: Café Batavia
name: Evi Yuliati
text: "It's a must to stopped by" if you're in kota tua. The place is beautiful. Food is amazing. I had the best ox tongue and lychee tea there. Oxtail soup and latte is good as was. Very busy. Better make reservation beforehand to make

title: Café Batavia
name: Safyana Younas
text: Love it currently sat here after having finished our meal and waiting for the drinks! Everything was great and atmosphere was superb! My best meal so far in Indonesia. Service was absolutely fantastic, Apprillia was brilliant! Would definitely recommend for people to visit this place. Prices are reasonable and the music is lovely

title: Café Batavia
name: Amy Lim
text: We were staying in Glodok area. So it's a must to visit this cafe. The building were constructed in 1830s. We went there for breakfast. It was a hot day so we were greeted by friendly staff that show us the way to up stair & another staff join in to bring us to our seat by the window. Wow! This place brings me back to the 60s with soft oldies being played as background. Very little thing of this place is so vintage. Let's talk about the food. At first the menu were brough to us with all wording. The waiter realise that I need aomething with picture, he qiuckly brough an tab or ipad to me to show the dishes in picture. From there we ordered our meals & drinks. For us, meal were good but the drinks so so only. I remember seeing other order dessrt. So we go for Ogy Ice cream small with 7 scoop 😍 & also poffertjes. We really love this dessert. We sitted there nearly over 2 hours, just enjoying the surrounding & chill out & unwind. I would said this is the best place so far for my trip in Jakarta, just relaxing & enjoying my breakfast💕 Price is on the high side but you paid for the environment. Food is good, drinks - okay, desset - yes please.

title: Café Batavia
name: Vina Amira
text:
The place is beautiful and iconic it really captures the classic old Jakarta atmosphere with vintage decor, high ceilings, and warm lighting. It’s cozy yet elegant, perfect for relaxing or hanging out with family or friends.

I ordered Nasi Goreng Komplit, Mie Goreng Komplit, and Tuna Salad. The Nasi Goreng Komplit was flavorful and perfectly cooked, with generous toppings like chicken, egg, and satay. in addition Mie Goreng Komplit had that slightly smoky flavor and a nice mix of noodles, vegetables, and spices. i also ordered tuna salad, it was really fresh, and its dressing complemented the salad nicely.

The staff were friendly, and the service was good once we were seated. However, the waiting list was quite long, so it took a while to get a table. i think this is the only drawback.

Overall, Batavia Café is definitely worth visiting great food, a lovely ambiance, and an iconic place that truly represents Jakarta’s charm. i recommend this restaurant, especially for international tourists

title: Café Batavia
name: Fitri Rachmadini
text:
Café Batavia is such a unique restaurant with its authentic Dutch colonial building — it really feels like stepping back in time! The whole atmosphere gives you that nostalgic charm you can’t find anywhere else.

Their cakes are delicious and properly made — definitely not disappointing. The price is a bit on the higher side, but totally worth it for the food and the one-of-a-kind vibes. ☕🍰🇮🇩

title: Café Batavia
name: Оля Любарская
text: The food was really good, and for someone visiting from Europe, it felt very cheap. You can smoke on the first floor, but it didn’t smell smoky at all. We waited about 10 minutes for the food, though it was Monday and there weren’t many people. The desserts were also delicious and made right there. The staff were super friendly and spoke good English. We came back about an hour later after a walk, they called us a taxi and even walked us to the car.

title: Café Batavia
name: Selvano Eduard
text: Exceptional! No other words. Atmosphere was nice with a view on the square, the food was also amazing but the service was the best in my trip Indonesia so far. Especially Novriyan, who handled the service like a true gentleman. Thanks for the experience!

title: Café Batavia
name: Bobie
text: Great experience overall! The historic ambiance is charming, and the staff are friendly and speak English well. Loved every single dish we ordered—delicious and well-presented. Only downside: the ice cream portion was huge for just two people. Still, highly recommended!

title: Café Batavia
name: Don Nguyen
text:
**NOTE: We should have read the lowest ratings reviews on Google here before we went in. Those low ratings are Spot-on!

Went here with my friend who was showing me around Fatahillah, aka Old Town Batavia in Jakarta. We were craving Italian and this little cafe eatery was near and right in the center square of attractions. Greeted by 2 front desk hosts who seated us at our table with 10 minutes. Our server name was "Ryan", whom I had tipped 50k IDR/$3 because I used to work as a waiter throughout College. After Ryan had taken our order, I had requested from one of the waitresses there named "Yulia" that I would like to order a bottle of water, so I could take my medication. After watching Yulia walk past us at least 3 times in a span of 20 minutes, and reminding her within 10 minutes after my initial request, she still did not bring us a bottle of sparkling water which I had ordered/requested. After I had realized she did not want to get me the water in a timely manner, I went up to the other waitress at the counter who didn't do anything but just stand there and told her I had ordered a water 20 minutes ago and have not received it and stated to her that if they don't want to serve us, we can leave. She then stated we would have to pay for our dishes (which we did not eat yet) and I said if I don't have my water I can't enjoy my meal. After she gave me an attitude I said this is not going to work out for us and just go ahead and charge us for the 2 smoothies we had, and forget the 2 entrees. She then said that I would have to pay for the entrees since they were in the kitchen being prepared. Fair enough, please get me my water I said. After me requesting to speak to their Manager "Neni", my water was promptly delivered to my table. Needless to say, our meal together with my friend felt very unhospitable and uncomfortable. Not a single time did any of these 3 servers come by to ask us if we needed anything else. Not to mention, we were charged before our dishes even came out. Our dishes were decent, not the best Italian I've ever had, but not bad. But for 2 dishes, my total came out to 854,000 IDRupiah, or about $54 USD for two people. I don't know if Yulia charged us correctly because she never delivered to us an itemized receipt of all the charges. I only have the receipt given from the credit card transaction, which is attached here. I will not recommend any Apple or Google colleagues of mine from California to come to this lackluster restaurant when coming to Jakarta to tour and sightsee this beautiful city. The service here at this restaurant is seriously lacking, and with much attitude. They are all about Money, and not the Customer experience. AVOID. BTW, this Batavia Cafe restaurant had just 2 tables the whole time we were there eating including us, so there is no excuse for poor service we experienced. We normally tip everyone, but this experience didn't even warrant our business.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: lai echo
text:
Lucky to find a cafe in the weekend. Hidden coffee here. I have order 2 signature coffee. Both of them Enak.👍🏻 enjoy in here for spend a half days and provide a good space for us to chitchat . Thanks 🫰🏻 @turningpointcoffee
Follow my ig: echoinjkt_food

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Hakim Athalla
text: A bit cramped (and a little disorganized), but overall a comfy place. Their Magic is quite strong but hits just right. I wouldn't really recommend the place for long WFCs but if you're looking for a quick coffee spot it's not a bad pick.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Irene Junita
text:
Cozy place.
Helpful & friendly staff.
Unique and beautiful design.
Gonna go back here to see the view in noon time, to see another nuance. The environment is quite not noisy. Calm enough to study / doing some work.

As for food, it's delicious. Though big eater should order more here but it's worth to try.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Puspa Cahyono
text:
We went there on a weekday evening, and there's quite a crowd. The atmosphere is "clean"-beige and broken white with all sorts of whale paraphernalia. I guess the owner loves whales. There's even a corner dedicated to stuffed whale dolls on the second floor.
I had spaghetti with bacon, eggs and steak. The taste? Delicious! The drink's also good. It's a good place to hang out if you have the time.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Mr. Oktavianus
text: It is an artsy coffee shop in Serpong with unique decoration. I bet the owner is a fan of whale. They used LM espresso machine and roasted their own coffee beans. I noticed Mahlkonig there and ordered manual brew. However, their filtered coffee was burn and too bitter for me. As for cappuccino, it was milky and the body was quite weak.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Safira Larasati
text:
It was my first time to go to Ruko Golden 8, there were soooo many cute and edgy coffee shops here but this one was designed by Antony Liu so I chose to try it.

The vibe and ambiences were nice, it was obvious that it was pretty well-designed. The staffs were so friendly and held health protocol correctly. Kudos! Talk about menu, the beverages were just okay with affordable prices too. You should try their signature coffees, I recommend that than their blends menu. Verdict: 8,5/10

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Alexander AS
text: Nice place to have morning bfreakfast and a cup of coffee. The place has great ambience n unique decoration where a lot of bottles arranged around the cafe. Their concept designed by Antony Liu. Overall is good except the coffee. Hot cap feels a bit plain for me and the espresso tastes sharp.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Ivan Julian
text:
Black: tropical fruit note, medium body, bright acidity. Sweet aftertaste

White: sweet chocolate and a bit almond fragrance.

Overall nice place nice service.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: vHiemPlates
text:
Uniquely decored coffee shop
Owned and architected by Antony Liu
I supposedly think it’s an ocean themed in a Ghibli (Japanese) style
.
Food was great
Their Mr. Kim Burger is a must try
Once i also had Pasta
Mostly their food are blends of Asian and Western
.
Their one of a kind Savoury Lotus Chips should also be on your order 🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Erly Tunriallu
text:
Always happy to be back here.
The cafe is located in Gading Sepong area near to Pahoa school.

The place is easy to find with white paint and sign.
Spacious parking lot in complex.
Inside the cafe is spacious with 2nd floor and outdoor area.

Staffs are welcome, nice and friendly.
They are good in product knowledge of coffee ingredients.

Nice design and cozy ambience so comfortable for working, chatting and me time.

Coffee is good and perfect.
Price is affordable.

We highly recommend to find a good coffee here.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: aryo nugroho
text: A great, acrylic-wood theme coffee shop. I find this shop has extraordinary coffee. Went for capuccino and mocha for my partner, this is the first time that i tasted mocha that is not so sweet. The price is medium, around 30-45K, but for food it can went to 85K

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: hendra chaidrata
text: I might be several years late, but this place is awesome! The interior surely has features that actually easy to be missed but, really, really genius for those who can appreciate. As for the meal and coffee, i had chicken nanban and flat white. Nothing special about those two, but the flat white surely turned me into energizer bunny till 3 AM.. 😅

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Rafael Stefan
text:
friendly and nice environment.
A bit pricey but overall is okay.
Got outdoor and indoor, the spot is very strategic.
You may choose halal-non halal food (pork and bacon).

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Steven Santoso
text: Coffeeshop dari generasi awal per coffeeshop an. Good design but feels like cramped so many small decoration made it cramped.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Gisela Vonny
text:
Nice place. Good coffee.
New normal menu by scan barcode, pembayaran nya cashless.

Update (26 july)
- butterscotch frappuccino was good if u like sweet cofee
- black truffle spaghetti was good too, bit salty bcause it mix with salted egg. (but the meat over dry)
- the smoothie bowl is not my cup of tea 🙅‍♀️

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: David Christian
text:
I come to this place a couple of times, and they are all good experiences. Located within Gading Serpong, it's relatively hidden in a ruko off the main road. But what a pleasant surprise they have here. Ambience is relatively bright and excellent for either hanging out with your friends or working alone. This place is not very large, and tables are of smaller sizes, meaning this is more appropriate for 1-4 people at most. There's only one or two big tables at the back.

The baristas here are very warm and friendly and regularly interact with customers. It seems like some of the customers are already friends and familiar with the baristas (or perhaps owners), and even call them by their names. A big plus for a small coffee shop. Coffee is still very nicely done here. No complain at all. Parking is easy and abundant. Sometimes crowded, but you'll likely to always get a table even in the peak hour. Wholeheartedly recommended, especially for you guys around Tangerang, BSD, or West Jakarta.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Do Ryang Kim
text:
good place. good drink memu.
I like the smoothie bowl here the most, but today I ordered a burger and rice for lunch.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Jason Ganub
text: Nice cafe with Japanese vibes, good food, and very instagrammable. WiFi speed is only 10-15mbps which could be improved. I’d work from here if it was faster. Also, there are no western breakfast such as eggs or sausages. Mainly Asian lunch and small bites for breakfast or cereal.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Marcel Kumontoy
text:
Great ambiance, just wished the seat to be cozier. Great coffee, a lot to choose from. The TP Burger was nice, but it arrived a bit late to our table so the burger wasn't warm and the fries were not so crispy anymore. Caesar salad was ok, eventho the waiter get our order wrong (was asking for the pork bacon topping, but got none). The plus side was they were quick to apologize...
Suggested the Butterscotch frappuccino (if I'm not mistaken) by the waiter...it was spot on.
Definately will come back when have the chance.

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: 張瑋洋
text:
Just found out they moved from old location. The new place is really nice, comfy, and spacious!
Ordered the latte and grilled cheese sandwich. Latte was as usual,nothing really special. But the grilled cheese, on the other hand, was really great!
The cheese was perfectly melted to the sandwich, and the dipping provided was just nice to add a bit of sourness.

p.s. 20% cashback using go-pay

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Gabby Thanissia
text: Did not expect it to be THIS good! Hands down the best matcha specialty café I’ve been to so far. The matcha was incredibly smooth, and the vibe of the place was super cozy—with a really nice scent in the air too. Definitely coming back again!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: corinna hidayat
text: best matcha and cozy placee !! I’ll surely be back to try your other drinks.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Beurre Romance
text:
• Yuzu Cheesecake
• Flan Cake
• Majicha
• Natsuka Matcha Latte
• Chocolate hazelnut

Waited for about 30mins on a saturday. Place’s warm & cozy 🤎 Highlight was the service 🫶🏻 Went with my grandma. While queuing, one of the owners (i assume) offered her a seat, he was concerned that she wouldn’t be able to withstand the long wait. Thoughtful 🥹🤍

Fav of the bunch: majicha 🍵 blending in pronounced hojicha w/ matcha cream 🤎 would opt for less sugar if possible

Soft serve was really good too, intensely milky! However, it’s slightly too sweet for us 🍦

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: TTHP
text: Place is too crowded, so we choose to take away. Matcha taste great, but I’m not that picky with this drink

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Sonya
text: I guess the vibes would have been nice if it wasn’t so noisy (we went on a Saturday). Natsuka matcha latte was nice, but the matcha roll cake was pretty average.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Maudy Putri Anindita
text: The variation of matcha here is good, both for drinks and dessert menu. I ordered Sakura Berry Matcha, Arashiyama Chiizu Matcha, and Matcha Roll Cake. Everything is quite good, all the ingredients blends well, not too sweet not too bitter. The place looks nice and clean as well. Overall experience is good, would love to come back here and try out other menu

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Onigiri Design Studio
text: The food was great, i love the fusion and the matcha flavor.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: dani christina
text: I visited this place around noon on a weekday (at 2 PM). Surprisingly, it was quite busy, but  no need to wait. I ordered the Arashiyama Chizu Matcha and the original cheesecake. The drink had a dominant cheesy flavor, while the matcha taste was not very strong. You could also enjoy a crunchy crumb in the drink. The original cheesecake was slightly similar to a pie, but still delicious. The service was also great.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Dita Saraswati Giri
text:
A great “ceremonial” matcha place in South Jakarta. They have many variants of ceremonial matcha.

Menu I tried:
- Matcha Latte: love! just the right level of bitterness. Priced around 60-70k i forgot the exact price.
- Ice cream with matcha sauce: it’s a good pairing since the ice cream is sweet and the matcha sauce is bitter. Often other places made both sweet. Priced around 60-80k too.
- Ceremonial matcha is a bit more expensive starting from 100K.

The cafe vibe is great.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Sya'diah
text: Found another good matcha cafe in town. Tried their ceremonial usucha and latte, both tasted rich and smooth. The yuzu cake is also yummy. Perfect combination

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: abigail livia
text:
Nice place to hang out,love the ambience ❤️ They have several flavours for the cheesecake.. And my fav goes to Yuzu Cheesecake.. Love the fresh taste of yuzu inside the creamy cheesecake..
And Don't forget to try their matcha drinks! Sooo addictive

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: rifqa fadhila
text: If you’re into matcha, Natsuka’s a no-brainer🫵🏻😭. Their Double Matcha next level. Strong but smooth, creamy but still super chill smh. Already plotting my next visit!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Syafina Widya
text:
Went here with my friends on Friday afternoon 👀 not as crowded as I thought tapi tetep ada queue line. Which is fine cos I didnt have to wait so long and the service was good too!

Ordered double matcha, okonomiyaki, matcha latte dan strawberry cake. All good apalagi okonomiyaki nya, sukaaa. Strawberry jam di cake nya lumayan bitter but it was ok, didnt have much to complain. Price range was ok too, standar kafe Jakarta laaah.

Will visit here again buat kerja!

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Edho Pratama
text: A nice place to unwind calm, cozy, and perfect for a casual hangout. While this cafe is best known for its matcha specialties, their coffee selections are equally impressive. I tried the Arashiyama Chiizu Matcha and the Magic Latte; both had distinct, enjoyable flavors that stood out. One of the highlights is being able to watch the matcha being prepared and served, adding an authentic and engaging touch to the overall experience.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Ming Dao
text:
Came by in the afternoon and tried the Arashima Chiizu Matcha — honestly, it didn’t really stand out.

The baristas seemed quite focused on making drinks, which is great, but it felt like the customer experience took a bit of a backseat. Even sitting right at the big wooden table in the middle, I didn’t really feel that sense of connection or get a glimpse into what was happening behind the bar.

Overall, both the drink and the experience were just… okay. Nothing more, nothing less.

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Ifin___
text:
Double Matcha 4/5 (sweet at first, savory and bitter at the end)
Matcha Latte 4/5 (no sugar, a bit bitter and fresh)
Okonomiyaki 3.8/5 (pricey, the taste is okay)

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Marcel Spencer
text: Went here on sunday morning, the vibes of the place is very nice, and Zen. Their matcha is nice (i ordered natsuka matcha latte), and their yuzu cheesecake also great

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Neo Cirzel (GodBless)
text:
Very cozy and such a nice place to chill. After a hectic day, totally a great place to unwind. Definitely will come back. 💅❤️
The vibe and atmosphere is so calming ✨

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Rahel Ester
text: Nice matcha drinks, different from regular matcha that we drink, it's unique and nice as a variation. worth to try. The cafes is also comfy🤍

title: Natsuka Cafe
name: Fifi Utami
text:
Nice ambience. Their matcha and cheese cake so good!
For the price it’s quite normal cause it’s located in Urban Forest. Will come here again soon!

title: Salty Salty
name: Marco Pipin
text: The coffee was good, the croissant actually good if served while crisp and hot (reheat) but sadly not, my fav was the caramel donut? Its heavenly sweet.

title: Salty Salty
name: Clairine Chrestella
text:
I’ve been to Salty Salty twice, and here’s my take:

First visit, I tried the matcha drink—pretty standard, but the salty cream on top was a unique and memorable twist. On my second visit, I had the chocolate drink with salty cream and customized the sugar level to zero. It was perfect, not too sweet, and I’d recommend it.

The service, however, needs improvement. I came close to closing time, and they kept taking orders until they were already closed. A staff member told us to leave in an abrupt, unfriendly way, which felt poorly handled. Other customers seemed unhappy too.

Drinks are good, but the service could be better.

title: Salty Salty
name: Miradonna
text:
Salty Salty Cafe - Great for Early Birds!

I love that Salty Salty Cafe opens early—perfect for a morning coffee fix. Their Salty Latte is a must-try—rich, creamy, and just the right amount of salty. It’s definitely my favorite! The place has a relaxed vibe, and the staff are friendly. Great spot to kickstart your day!

title: Salty Salty
name: Angelina Setiawan
text:
The cafe was sooo aesthetic!
It was crowded on weekend so I decided to takeaway the drinks.

1. Salty Green (55k)
I asked for less sugar. The matcha was good, not too bitter. The cream was nice! It was soft, salty, slightly sweet!

2. Salty Latte (55k)
The coffee was nice, quite strong taste, the cream was nice!

3. Matchamisu (80k)
Tbh it was too pricey for the portion but the taste was nice. The matcha was quiet strong, the texture was nice.

4. Chocolate Basque Burnt Cheesecake (65k)
I love this one! Not too sweet, kinda salty from the cheese, the texture was quite firm, could taste both the choco and the cheese.

5. Lychee Earl Grey (80k)
Again a lil bit pricey but the earl grey was quite strong and fragrant! The lychee added a nice sweetness and fragrant to the cake. The spongecake was fluffy.

6. Salted Caramel Chocolate (38k)
A danish with sweet caramel and chocolate in the middle. There was also something similar to the almond paste between the layers of the pastry. This was my favorite tho it was a lil bit too sweet for me.

Overall everything was good!

title: Salty Salty
name: Charissa Patricia
text: Yummyyy food! Love the balance of sweet-savory of every dish. Would recommend the latte and the basque burnt matcha cheesecake.

title: Salty Salty
name: Riana Yuu
text:
One of my favourite kinds of drinks. Unique seasalt cream to top dark chocolate drink or lattes. You should really add some grilled marshmallows on it if you love chewy things! The burnt marshmallow flavor with the seasalt cream is soooo good 🥹 They also have some snacks to go.

I really recommend you to come early on the weekend because it can be very crowded around 2 PM till the evening. 2nd floor definitely can be your best choice to sit and chat while enjoying your drink to avoid some crowds and noise on the 1st floor when it's rush hour.

Weekdays might be less crowd if you're available to come on that time.

Good drink, but keep it moderate 😆

title: Salty Salty
name: Monica Febriana
text:
We randomly found this place. The interior design was very nice but a little bit too noisy when the coffee shop was full of customers. Luckily, we got an empty table during our visit to the coffee shop.
I ordered an orange americano and the coffee was good and refreshing.
The staff were very polite and friendly.

title: Salty Salty
name: Carrot gyuu
text: Great place for noon-coffee, loving the interior and the ambience. The drinks were so tasty and they got great coffee. The matcha drink was especially good as it got salty cream on it. Will come back for another order!

title: Salty Salty
name: Jeremy Kadir
text: ordered the salty matcha and their regular iced matcha latte, gotta say their matcha powder hits the right places, and their salty cream tastes just right, the saltiness mixed with the sweet bitterness of matcha just makes u smile, yea im lookin at u matcha lovers

title: Salty Salty
name: Aryanto
text: Great taste, but the place are a bit dirty when i come. Only after i seated that the staff cleans the table

title: Salty Salty
name: Global Exec CEO
text:
This place is a hidden gem for matcha and dessert lovers! The interior is minimalist and modern, with a soft Japanese aesthetic that creates a calming and clean vibe—perfect for chilling out with friends or family.

We ordered:
•	Matcha Sea Salt Cream: Beautifully layered, rich in matcha flavor with just the right hint of saltiness from the cream. The toasted marshmallow topping added a playful touch.
•	Salty Salty: A signature favorite—same great matcha base but even more intense flavor and texture.
•	Chocolate Sea Salt Cream (for my daughter): She loved it! It’s creamy, sweet, with a light salty kick that made it stand out from your usual chocolate drink.

We also checked out their Tiramisu Cake—looked tempting in the display. Will definitely come back to try more from their dessert lineup!

Service was friendly and fast, although the space is a bit tight during peak hours. Still, worth the visit for the quality of the drinks and the fun, Instagram-worthy presentation

title: Salty Salty
name: Gouw Margareta
text: ordered coffee latte with sea salt cream, tastes nice but a bit pricey. the place was nice too. they have various pastries 🥐

title: Salty Salty
name: Dwiriyo Suryo Sasmoko
text: I ordered iced americano and this is the first time I visited this cafe. For me, 38k IDR for a mediocre robusta blend is quite expensive. The taste is just ok, nothing special. I can taste the bitterness of robusta. I don’t know why they decided to use this kind of beans.

title: Salty Salty
name: Viola Andrea
text: My drinks weren't even full like everyone else. Too much salty cream bcs mine wasn't full. So many staff in one small place making the drink and doing cashier but none of them were wearing mask, it's unhygienic. Came on the weekend n really crowded. Just one toilet, plus there isn't any wastafel outside of the toilet just like normal cafe/restaurant. On the second floor only 1 AC which is not enough for cooling. The decoration on 2nd fl also has no effort. It was just like plain, has no effort

title: Salty Salty
name: Olivia
text: Visited at 8 pm, it was full and noisy, the music was loud, it was difficult to talk/work inside. They got plenty of plugs. Love their matcha. Not too sweet. Cream cheese on point, creamy, milky, and salty, just like the way they brand theirself. Mochi wasn’t to my liking. Overall like it!

title: Salty Salty
name: Wina Huang
text: i like their salty green: matcha with salty cream. Very flavorful but since the matcha is so thick, beware if you drink in the evening, it could make you stay awake for hours

title: Salty Salty
name: Tommy Mandagi
text: Went there two times, tried their salty latte and salty green. You have to try the SALTY GREEN! Very rich smooth salty sweet and creamy, the green tea was nice. Don't expect ceremonial matcha, this is a milky drink. The salte lattee was okay, not bad, but definitely have the salty green! Place was okay, but more seatings would be better.

title: Salty Salty
name: Lowilius Wiyono
text: Kinda went here due to the heavy rain yesterday. They are famous for their matchs latte with salty cream on top, along with marshmallow. They taste surprisingly clean with a mix of sweet and salty taste. Recommended for dates or company. They also let in pets!

title: Salty Salty
name: Isabel
text: LOVED THIS PLACE!!! 10/10. Lots of seating spots, nice staff, the food & drinks are amazing.

title: Salty Salty
name: ofscienceandmagic
text:
Love their signature salt matcha! So yummy, i cant help but compare cheese cream and the salt cream. Both are so similar.

Went 5pm friday. Not so crowded. Pretty chill place.

title: Café Batavia
name: Penny
text: Absolutely loved it here....like old school English 1950's kind of vibe. Lovely interior although the music was catchy and apt for atmosphere it was stuck on one song lol

title: Café Batavia
name: Berliana Sadikun
text: like eating in a royal place with elegant dutch buildings..and all the food is delicious especially the chili squid

title: Café Batavia
name: Leo Khan
text: Amazing experience. Tasty food. Excellent service. My server spoke great English and gave really good recommendations of food and drink selection. I went there Saturday night and had almost no one in the restaurant. Everyone was nice and polite. Great live music with free one song request for the band. I will definitely come back again next time I'm in Jakarta. Highly recommend!

title: Café Batavia
name: Anna Safa
text:
beautiful historical restaurant, a true fine brunch. it doesn’t taste authentic because the taste might caters more towards foreigners, it lacks seasoning and strong flavor that indonesia food holds. i got nasi goreng, ribs, and satay and others, it was okay, not the best. i also got strawberry juice, white wine and bintang beer.

for the price, a fine brunch price, i wouldn’t say this is worth it. but it’s a nice one time visit.

beautiful restaurant nevertheless, architecture and decor pieces is such a masterpiece. aircon is nice since kota tua is very hot. service is nice and helpful.

title: Café Batavia
name: Vishhal K Bhusari
text:
Appear polite in face but very rude ad core the staff at the entrance. Felt a cultural bias in treatment too.

The staff at Bar super nice, polite and humble (welcoming)

Came in with high expectations and disappointed

First experience in Jakarta of the cultural bias

title: Café Batavia
name: JoJo Grahm
text: Batavia is a café with a very vintage-style decoration, and the interior environment is extremely elegant. The location is right in Taman Fatahillah, surrounded by many attractions, making it perfect for a midday drink to escape the hot sun.

title: Café Batavia
name: Ianhung Lee
text: Instantly transport you back in time with dark wooden interior with colonial era touch on every corner. The establishment itself is elegant with a very attentive services. Food prices are on the higher side. There are plenty of seatings indoor but can be very occupied with tourists. Some area of seating can be loud with all the patrons chatting away while sipping tea.

title: Café Batavia
name: Akonya
text:
Absolutely LOVED the place. Amazing spot to visit in Jakarta. The special Nasi Goreng is perfect. Coffee is such a good quality.

Excellent service from the personnel, especially Lutfi.

title: Café Batavia
name: Teo
text:
A delightful dining experience!
The restaurant offers a cosy and inviting atmosphere that's perfect for a relaxing meal. The service was attentive and warm throughout our visit.
While the prices are on the higher side, the food was absolutely delicious and worth every bite. A great spot for those who value quality and a comfortable setting.

title: Café Batavia
name: Joonin
text: Love the nasi goreng special. Not much wait while we were there around 1.30pm on Friday. Very nice interior with a mix of colonial. Food was nice as well.

title: Café Batavia
name: Meaw Chew
text: Great atmosphere and excellent service from Novriyan. Best Nasi Campur in Jkt.

title: Café Batavia
name: Dhante Adhitama
text:
Highly recommended, definitely add it to your itinerary if you're in Jakarta. The dessert, main course, appetizers, and drinks are all delicious. 😍😍👍🏻👍🏻
It's recommended to make a reservation before visiting.

title: Café Batavia
name: Aubri Fadhila syifa
text:
The food very good 👍
Just the toilet still didn’t understand why they show some nudes pic there but so far pretty good , maybe I will back to eat nasi campur

title: Café Batavia
name: Inyoman Sanjaya
text: Historic cafe with legendary story, if you like history, come here and see how was legendary cafe, and then you can share the storyteller...

title: Café Batavia
name: Ancelikh
text:
You should definetly stop over here for at least a coffe and cake, if you not hungry.... Just for the atmosphere..... And the service.
Its a MUST Jakarta experience.

title: Café Batavia
name: Jason
text: Old school dining. Had the fried jackfruit, a donut made from yuzu, both were delicious.

title: Café Batavia
name: Charlie Swartsman
text: A beautiful historical restaurant, which therefore attracts a lot of tourists. This is also reflected in the prices. The food tastes good. The service was excellent and fast. The only disappointment was the bathroom cleanliness,  which fell short of what you'd expect from such a renowned restaurant.

title: Café Batavia
name: David Plaza
text: Beautiful restaurant to enjoy a meal or an Indonesian coffee. You’re immersed in the charm of the past, with a lovely view over Fatahillah Square.

title: Café Batavia
name: Palastri Shop
text:
The food is good, and the plating is excellent.
The atmosphere is pleasant, including the toilet.

The male staff are friendly and professional. They treated my partner from the Netherlands 🇳🇱 well and even asked for his opinion on the food compared to the 🇳🇱 Netherlands.

However, some of the female staff were not as accommodating. At first, they told us that only the bar area was available. But shortly after, six foreigners who arrived after me were given seats inside. Noticing that I had difficulty sitting at the bar, they moved us to a regular table. Later, when they saw we had ordered food 🍲, they moved us again—this time to the best location (inside with a view on the second floor).
(We changed locations three times.)

In the end, though, they thanked us, helped open the door, and treated us well. So, I still recommend visiting this place because most of the staff are friendly and welcoming. :)

title: Café Batavia
name: Marshelly Ariesya Allen
text: The vibes was so romantic, elegance, classic, colonial, but mixed it up into modern vibes. Recommend for romantic dinner, family dinner or just work from cafe 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Henry Rahardja
text:
Great selection of coffee, blended, and juiced drinks. Definitely love the moca latte with vanilla ice cream. The salad with pork bacon and poach egg is pretty good too.

Great place with tons of cute whale drawings and minimalist interior. Quite expensive but overall it's a good experience

title: Turning Point Coffee
name: Sisilia Deb
text: Great coffee shop that served good coffee. The place is cozy to hangout and have a chit chat with friends, or if you wanted to work with laptop it's a good place too.

title: Turning Point Coffee

title: Turning Point Coffee

Price worthy for the coffee & foods
They also sell coffee beans

Australian coffee with whale themed ghibli interior — what not to love at this place?

Many people complimented their main dish, but I’ve only tried their milk pie. It was good and balanced perfectly well to be paired with their strong magic.

Can imagine myself WFA-ing from the second floor of this establishment.

title: Turning Point Coffee

However the taste of the  later was VERY YUMMY (it's a local's touch I guess -- not savoury but sweet). If you make the price more considerate, definitely I'd come often

nice service. the hot choco was also delicious.
nothing to complaint.
good luck for the business :)

In picture: double matcha

I ordered the truffle pasta, it was good. Perhaps not the best I've tastes but it was okay. Not good enough I'm craving for it but I'm fine with it and is interested to taste their other dishes too next time.

Strawberry shortcake was nice, the cream wasn't sickening. But then again, I shared it with some other friends.

(Will definitely update with more reviews and photos when I get back there)

My favourites are long black and cappuccino (usually I request to be made magic).
Although their prices are a bit higher compared to other coffee shops, but it's definitely worth it. I give their coffee 7.5/10.
Shop's ambience was great as very clean well. They have cozy indoor seatings and the outdoor seatings was pleasant. During the pandemic, i'd love to sit outdoors.

text: taste so good for the matcha, coffee was okay. Havent tried the chocolate. the place a little bit too small, pet friendly i guess. Dont usually like marshmallows but this one taste good

title: Salty Salty
name: Anisa
text:
TL;DR – go for the drinks, SKIP the desserts. See below.

I'd say the Salty Green is worth trying but not worth queueing too long for. It's basically just a matcha latte with cream that's been slightly salted on top. The matcha is quite concentrated and I'd recommend going for less sugar as the cream is already quite sweet. It would've been better if there was a higher liquid to cream ratio though, as the cream took 1/3 of the cup's volume so you're not getting much too drink.

I would ABSOLUTELY NOT recommend getting the matcha mille crepe cake. I was shocked by how TOUGH it was; it's a cake, it's not supposed to be tough let alone so much so that I actually need to exert force to cut through it! It also tasted so bad that it's practically inedible. In short, it was like play-doh just before it dries and stiffens. I took one bite and left; total waste of money.

title: Salty Salty
name: Carmen Woo
text:
This cafe is not overrated as it served good beverages with attractive presentation! I had ordered both salty latte and orange americano, both i will highly recommend others to try!

Good aromatic beans with salty cream, best taste the cream and coffee separately instead mixing all up~ while for the other glass, citrus taste not too sweet with adequate soury pairing up with aromatic americano, refreshing and soothing!

They do have their signature salty matcha and salty choco which will be my next revisit option, so do their pastry and cakes 🤩

title: Salty Salty

text: Really love salty2, udah coba matcha nya for take away which I liked, and then tried the choco with half sugar and also really like it, I like when the cream covers the ice and I get to crunch on the ice covered with cream 🤣

title: Salty Salty
name: Edwin
text: Salty Salty—my go-to caffeine haven when I'm pulling office stints in Pantai Indah Kapuk. Interior game? A solid thumbs-up—clean, minimalist vibes that hit the spot. Coffee? Check. Pastries? No complaints here. Only gripe? The vaping zone needs a serious comfort upgrade. Front and center isn't doing it, especially when the PIK air is hotter than a summer fling. Let's dial up that vaping cool factor, shall we?

title: Salty Salty

I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a comfortable, clean and good interior design/apor to hangout. I think Salty Salty alao ambience made me feel right at home so make me and my friend wanna stay until the end of the day. I'll definitely be returning !

Best wishes for ongoing success, Salty Salty ! ❤️

title: Salty Salty
name: fel
text:
Loved the food, but unfortunately, really disliked the service.

At first, we wanted to order the salty chocolate and the salty latte. However, one of my family member mentioned that she cannot drink coffee hence we only ordered salty chocolate.

We haven’t paid so we decided to cancel the salty latte. I heard very well how the waitress mentioned it to one of the waiter to cancel and the waiter gave an annoyed remark.

Is this how a coffee shop supposed to be? We haven’t paid, and if we cancel it, we deserve the bad remark?

I put it here in hope that the service for this coffee shop can be improved. Thank you.

title: Salty Salty

we ordered matcha and chocolate and they are all delicious

title: Salty Salty

title: Café Batavia
name: Ian Kainama
text: Great service, one of the best restaurant I’ve came to visit! Definitely will come back. 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

title: Café Batavia
name: Sam W
text: Historical colonial style architecture. A must if you’re visiting Old Town Jakarta. Service was rather slow (we ordered one chicken and it took 30 minutes!) good cosy areas, well air-conditioned.

title: Café Batavia
name: Yuhui LIN
text: I have been to the Cafe Batavia for twice. It is the most beautiful cafe in North Jakarta!! It has 2 floors and there are tons of photos and paintings inside, making the space very cozy. I recommend you stay here for a lunch or dinner. The price is also affordable for foreigners

title: Café Batavia
name: Quinn'z Land
text: Really didn’t live up the hype. It looked nice and had its own unique vibe that fits the city, that was true but also everything good to it. Since this is becoming a trending spot now, you can’t exactly expect that there’s no wait. We were foreigners, didn’t know about the queue until we’re at their doorstep, so yeah, there’s a wait. Ours was about 30 mins so that’s okay. Now onto the food, none of them had a taste that’s easy to swallow for first-timers (us). We found it extremely hard to finish the meal despite its striking price. Food tasted strange, portions too large for women, coffee was so weird that we don’t even think we could form a solid opinion about it.

title: Café Batavia
name: Lyda Hak
text: This place is a bit a high-end café where they expect you to spend. The more you spend, the better location you will be offered for seating. However, all staff are friendly, speak good English n good to enjoy the atmosphere where you can learn the colonial history of this building. The coffee itself was just fine, not as wow as I'd expected for.

title: Café Batavia
name: Anatoly La Pere
text: Adventurous caesar salad - I get the boiled eggs but they were colder than the rest of the salad. Deep fried tofu was okay. Okay avocado drink.

title: Café Batavia
name: Tiara
text: I went here with my friends for the first time in my 30 years of life as an Indonesian. The vibe is odd in some way, I feel like I was a “kompeni” back in the colonial times, but i suppose it was their concept. Although I must say the atmosphere was great. The service was incredible, very polite, down to earth staff. Typical Indonesian smile all the time service. I love that about this place. Of course its somewhat pricey but with the portions and the service that you get, totally worth the price. Overall i would definitely comeback.

title: Café Batavia

title: Café Batavia

text:

Service is attentive and polite. Food options are somewhat typical of Jakarta: local menu with western options. The food we ordered was delicious and enjoyable. Nice beverage list too.

Highly recommended for ambience.

title: Café Batavia

title: Café Batavia
name: wisnhu wardhana
text:
Booked a table for 8 at 10.45am for brunch.

All the dishes were good.. I was billed 2mio which is very proper for the foods, service and the ambiance... no complaint...
but not for iced tea and mineral water.. 😂

For an average local who drinks iced tea in daily basis, 300k for 5 regular iced tea also 100k for 2 mineral waters is overpriced.
i spent 500k only for all 'regular' drinks. 😁

The seasoning and spiciness of most traditional dishes came a bit lighter than the original versions... which is okay because this resto has been a foreign tourists spot for many years so the foods have to be suitable for them.. still tasted good tho..

As a native batavian i need to ask the management to give more portion for traditional betawi dishes, desserts or snacks.. Also a bit nuance of "betawi" instead the entirely "colonial" atmosphere.

title: Café Batavia

text: Very pleasant environment in an historic building. Exceedingly, but tasty food is served.

They serve mostly local Indonesian cuisine but it was a bit “neutralized” compared to the authentic one, since we loved strong and savory dishes.
But the main attraction is always building which is well restored and well maintained for a more than 200 years old and quite significant building.

We ordered and the wait was not that long just 8-12 minutes I think. Food came I got my spaghetti bolognese. I think it needs a little improvement since not that flavorful. Tried a sate padang. Amazingly good. The drinks? It is definetly an 8/10. Tried the choco cake, forgot which one but it left me speechless. Price maybe need to lower a little since it was 800 thousand rupiah which is around 50$. I recommend it.

And i pay 70k-ish for it. 2) what's worst is the doughnut. Hard to chew. I am a big fan of doughnut so really disappointed. The place is small and not as aesthetic as the picture. Nothing special. Verdict, if u look for a place to hang out nearby. U can just come here to order their drink. But if u want have a good and satisfying meal. Definitely not the place.

Overall experience was alright, I'd give it a 6/10

Come here if you just want to have a drink and take Instagrammable photos. I'd advise y'all to stay away from the food.
