title: Café Batavia
name: Un P.
text:
Seems like sitting next to the window table there is the big thing for them because the staff inform the regulation if we want to sit next to the windows have to order one main course per person which is weird and the window view is not that wow (for me) blocked by the big tent next to it.

For the food and drinks , I can say its quite nice but this one thing that upset me when I order coffee and I am surprised that didn’t serve with fresh milk or creamer only with 3 types of sugar. So when I ask for a bit of fresh milk,  the staff want to charge additional for it which disappointed me so much. I never experience something like this before. For just black coffee small cup (price IDR 65k) I think they can do better than this.

And one more thing, smoking area is downstairs at the entrance and walk way to upstairs for non smoking area. I don’t like it at all. Imagine who don’t smoke and don’t like the smell have to walk through it to go up and after done the meal have to walk pass through it to go out. The smoke smell is all over my clothes and my hair.

title: Café Batavia
name: Calvin Lee
text: Had a wonderful dining experience at Café Batavia! The food was absolutely delicious and exquisitely prepared — especially the rice and beef, which were full of flavor. A special thanks to Fikar, who was incredibly friendly and interesting. His excellent service truly made the visit memorable. The restaurant’s ambiance is fantastic — charming, cozy, and perfect whether you’re dining solo or with friends. Highly recommend!

title: Café Batavia
name: Shuja Khan
text: Just like their logo ‘Exceptional historical dining experience’ it is indeed an exception historical dining experience because of the location but not just the location the building in which it’s located itself is historical. Ordered lontong and rendang, the food was good and spicy just as you would expect in Indonesia. Meat in the dishes was tender and juicy.

title: Café Batavia
name: Maria
text: A charming place where we enjoyed a tea and a cake, but also a drink unknown to us until now, a "beer" with cinnamon and cardamom. The superb building is located in the historical center of Jakarta, dating from the beginning of the 19th century, built by the Dutch, having several purposes until it became a restaurant. It has a bohemian air, the interior is splendid. Service, reception excellent! Thank you, we had a wonderful time!  💯👍👏

title: Café Batavia
name: Cuphead Boomerang
text: Lovely atmosphere, conveniently located right next to Fatahillah Square. It’s the perfect spot to unwind and relax after spending a few hours exploring the nearby museums.

title: Café Batavia
name: Alex Regionable
text:
Café Batavia, situated in the heart of Jakarta’s historic district, offers a charming escape into Indonesia’s rich cultural heritage. The café’s facade, featuring classic colonial architecture complete with wooden shuttered windows, is both inviting and impressive. Stepping inside, the ambiance is a seamless blend of traditional elegance and casual comfort, making it an ideal setting for both locals and tourists.

The interior exudes a warm, nostalgic feel with its polished wooden floors and an extensive collection of framed photographs that adorn the walls, each telling a piece of Jakarta’s storied past. The real highlight, however, is the authentic Indonesian cuisine that Café Batavia proudly serves. The menu is a vibrant array of local favorites, each dish prepared with care and presented with a homely touch.

From the moment I sat down, the friendly staff made me feel welcome, ensuring a pleasant dining experience. The food, rich in flavors and cooked to perfection, provides a delightful culinary journey through Indonesia’s diverse gastronomy.

Café Batavia is more than just a dining spot; it’s a cultural experience that offers a window into the soul of Jakarta through its food and atmosphere. It’s the perfect place for anyone looking to enjoy a casual meal in a uniquely historical setting.

Highly recommended for a taste of local cuisine in a place that feels like a home away from home!

title: Café Batavia
name: Yusri Yahya
text: It’s a nice spot to hang out while overlooking Fatahillah Square. Many people come here to cycle and enjoy other leisure activities. The food options are quite varied, though the prices are a bit steep. It’s fine for drinks and light snacks, or maybe some cake

title: Café Batavia
name: Okan Bostan
text:
I saw this place in a Youtube video when a travel blogger recommended it. Last year we did not take the chance to visit it but this year I definitely wanted to give it a try.
Even though there was no table available we managed to dine in by putting my name on a waitlist.
After approximately half an hour I got a call and we were able to order our meal.
The staff gave us probably the best table right in the corner of the restaurant by the window overlooking the big Kota Tua square with its famous and colourful bicycles.

The building but also the restaurant are completely kept in origin from the colonial style even though I do not approve any sort of colonialism.

The staff was very polite and friendly at all times and paid attention to detail.

The food was exceptionally good and tasty. You see how the chefs give their best not only with the taste but also with the presentation of the dishes.

I can recommend the corn fritters, the fried tempe and the chicken sate served with rice. My family had the nasi goreng which they liked very much, too.

Price wise this restaurant is on a higher level but the quality of the food justified it completely.

title: Café Batavia
name: comeupforair
text:
This place is a bit pricey. They charge 10% service fee but won’t even serve you a cup of water. Yes if you want a drink you’ll need to order, even no tap water.

I ordered the signature dish but not enough to fill me up. I ordered satay separately. The funny thing is I don’t think the waiters have enough attention on customers - I tried to have eye contact with them to let them know I want to talk to them (to order more) but I think many of them just ignored. I hope it’s not because I’m a foreign young girl

title: Café Batavia
name: Siti Masitho
text:
If I could give zero stars for service, I would.

We visited this café in Kota Tua because of its reputation. Unfortunately, the only thing “famous” here seems to be the attitude.

Despite plenty of empty tables in the main sunny room, we were seated elsewhere while guests who arrived after us were given better seats. Only after asking twice were we finally moved. Not a great start.

From then on, the service was consistently cold and condescending. When I ordered hot tea, a waitress felt the need to lecture me that it would “actually cost money here,” as if I had suggested otherwise. Later, they brought hot water instead of tea, insisted it was what I ordered, and handled the mistake with irritation rather than apology.

The food was forgettable, and the ambience couldn’t compensate for the hostile atmosphere. For a café that tries so hard to look upscale, the service feels cheap, arrogant, and unprofessional.

Management should seriously reconsider how their staff are trained. Basic hospitality, clear communication, and a respectful attitude toward guests are not optional in a place that claims to offer a premium experience.

We left before finishing our drinks. I won’t be returning, and I certainly won’t be recommending this place to anyone.

title: Café Batavia
name: Adi Pebriawan
text:
Stopped by Café Batavia for lunch after visiting the museum, and it was the perfect choice. The service was top-notch — friendly, efficient, and made us feel so welcome. The food was absolutely delicious, with a great variety and rich flavors that really hit the spot.

The atmosphere is unbeatable — classic colonial charm with elegant vintage décor, making the whole experience feel special and relaxing. It’s the kind of place where you want to linger and soak in the vibe.

Perfect for a post-museum lunch or a relaxing meal in Kota Tua. Highly recommended and definitely a must-visit in Jakarta!

title: Café Batavia
name: Sakshi Trivedi
text:
Cafe Batavia is a fantastic spot with a great mix of history, ambiance, and delicious food. The portions are generous, and the flavors are well-balanced, making it a satisfying dining experience.

What truly stands out is the exceptional staff—super friendly, attentive, and incredibly patient. They take the time to explain the menu, offer recommendations, and ensure you have everything you need without feeling rushed. It really adds to the overall experience.

The atmosphere is warm and inviting, perfect for a relaxed meal while soaking in the charm of the place. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in Jakarta!

title: Café Batavia
name: Riley Lucas
text: Came in for a quick refreshment. Got the coconut water, avocado shake and a mocktail. Service was good, cafe set up was nice with early colonial photos and furnishings tying in nicely with the theme. Cocktails were quite pricey for the location at around 180k - 200k rupia. Was nice to sit down and chill before further exploring the city.

title: Café Batavia
name: Lionel
text:
Ordered the famous nasi goreng, soto with beef offal, and stuffed squid with chicken.

Stunning venue with warm, attentive service. Dishes were well-prepared and flavourful — a lovely spot for a memorable meal.

title: Café Batavia
name: Mark Myerson
text: If there was a 6 star for atmosphere this place would get it! Gorgeous architecture with great bars and dining areas. We sadly missed Jazz Night which I’m sure would be brilliant. You can spend ages just wandering and looking at the interesting celebrity photographs. Food and beverages are excellent quality and reasonably priced.

title: Café Batavia
name: Musashi Ogasawara
text: Went here last week and the food was great. I didn’t have to wait for a long time, and when the staff saw I was taking a picture by myself they offered to take pictures—and they were nice ones. I definitely recommend when visiting Kota Tua.

title: Café Batavia
name: Ali Raza
text:
A historical restaurant but mostly over crowded and jam packed with visitors. You’ll need to wait for an hour or so to eat.
But yeah overall a good experience.
Taste is just average.

title: Café Batavia
name: Nicolas Pitarch
text:
Good environment, is beautiful see the colony atmosphere. I asked for a Soto Ayam and was really good. After the meal I asked a cappuccino and was horrible haha (really). The prices are expensive if you compare with another places, but still worthy it.
As you entry they give you a table, is mandatory ask for 1 meal and 1 drink.
Soto Ayam + Cappuccino = 181.500 RP

title: Café Batavia
name: Lis Horning
text:
Cafe Batavia – A Historic Café with Vintage Charm, Live Music, and Tourist Appeal

Cafe Batavia, located in Fatahillah Square, is housed in a beautifully preserved 1805 colonial building—the second oldest in the area. Since 1993, it has served as a café that blends history with a vintage, Instagram-worthy atmosphere.

The interior features old teak wood, colonial-style décor, and vintage photos. It’s cool and comfortable, with live music sometimes playing on the ground floor. There’s even a piano that guests are allowed to play—but only one song per person. Restrooms are located on the second floor.

That said, the place leans slightly toward being a tourist trap. The view is decent, typical of the Old Town area—not breathtaking, but atmospheric.

Ambience: ★★★★☆ (4.5/5) – Stylish vintage vibes, relaxing, and photogenic

Food: ★★★½☆ (3.5/5) – Taste is decent, but price is high for what you get

Service: ★★★★☆ (4.5/5) – Professional and friendly

Overall Rating: ★★★★☆ (4.2/5 or 85 out of 100)

Great for soaking up history with a coffee, but be prepared for tourist-level pricing.

title: Café Batavia
name: Andrey Janos
text:
Super average food and coffee, overpriced for sure … Nice classy building, interesting interior, come to take a picture and that’s it.
What I don’t really get is the design of the toilets (at least male), which must have been made by an interesting individual. Not sure how comfortable I would be sending my kiddos there …

name: Glen
text: Nice restaurant around Elang Laut area. It’s newly renovated and quite fancy. Had a main course and side dish, both were great however both were too salty for me. However still a good experience.

title: Brew And Else Coffee, PIK
name: Ketut Artha
text:
Cozy & Perfect for Coffee Lovers!

This coffee shop is an absolute gem! The ambiance is warm and inviting, making it a great place to relax or get some work done. The bean juice here is top-notch—rich, aromatic, and expertly brewed. Whether you prefer a classic espresso or something more adventurous, they’ve got it all.

The staff is friendly and knowledgeable, always ready to recommend the perfect cup. Plus, the attention to detail in both the drinks and the presentation makes the experience even better. Definitely a must-visit for any coffee lover!

Well this is the only recommended menu from me, the creme brulle is nice and not too sweet with thai tea ice cream, it's pretty and delicious!

2. Tuatara Beer
It's very light and crisp, it was delicious for me but sooo expensive.

3. Truffle fries
The fries cut is big so it's moist inside and not so crispy. But it's very plain, only some fries have some taste. I don't recommend it.

5. Carbonara
It's a good carbonara. It was okay..

6. Frittata
Well..it's an egg, with some salads. Can't say I'm dissapoonted, it's written on the menu, but I'm not expecting it to be so....plain.

7. Burger
It's so-so... The meat is dry, I'd rather have classic McD cheeseburger

text:
There’s no label/name outside the place, only a huge glass door displaying white & open space. I’ve passed the place a couple of times but never got the time to visit, so I decided to spare a few hours to do some assignment here.

First impression is how well-designed the place is. The outer part of the first floor is very white with marble tabletops, but as I got inside and arrived at the cashier/coffee bar it was black and gold, very 20s glam. Ottoman leather chairs and round tables can easily fit 6-8 people per table. There’s not much of a crowd (maybe because of the no label/name)

Their menu has enough variety but not too broad, coffee is pretty nice (I ordered iced latte) and the poutine is quite delicious even though it doesn’t really taste like poutine. Spent 98.000 IDR for those two and spent 3 hours there. They also have a second floor but I didn’t get to explore them. Their bathroom is so gorgeous!! They have lights on the mirror (like the ones at a salon/makeup studio) so makeup touch-up is on point 👌🏼

Overall, this place is surely going to be my new favorite.

With 2 slices of lemon and mint leaves inside the cup ofcourse there was only so little liquid that you can drink out of it.  When I asked for refill of hot water , they said you could not ask for more hot water because it is not tea.  My advise is you put it in a bigger cup or allow guests to ask for a refill.  Jangan terlalu kaku n sombong since you guys are still new. It doesnt cost you that much anyway.
Thank you.

title: Café Batavia
name: Johnson Badawi
text: Such a lovely cafe in Old Town! The vibe was vintage and posh—perhaps remnants of its colonial past. But the food was nothing colonial. They serve one of the most interesting local cuisines—from a local sweet and spicy salad to a coated shrimp that were all delicious. Surprisingly, the price was not too bad. Inside the shop, you’ll find the most creative interior. Definitely would come back.

title: Café Batavia
name: Maria Go
text:
Can be considered as a "posh" restaurant in the area. You can definitely feel welcomed the moment you step foot even if you are just dining solo. Staff are very accommodating and will recommend all the right food fit for the table.
I am super thankful for the girl server who took my order because she recommended me a good dish that is fit for just a solo diner because apparently I was picking out huge servings the first time 😅 She also recommended a good and light iced coffee which I liked!
Food was superb huhu the fried rice was super delicious! 🥹 They have different food options from local cuisine to international ones. They also have a bar food drinks.
Cant recommend them enough!

title: Café Batavia

text:
Historical restaurant with lower than average but expensive food.

The place was the 2nd most historical building, inside was well preserved. If you would like to visit, recommend to make reservations in upstairs seats near the window.

The food is really disappointing, iga bakar had a bad smell to it, soto was cold.

Overpriced as well.

title: Café Batavia
name: Justin De klerk
text:
Batavia Cafe in Jakarta is a delightful gem that takes you on a nostalgic journey the moment you step inside. The vintage decor creates a unique atmosphere, offering a walk back in time that is both charming and enchanting. The cakes at Batavia Cafe are simply delicious, each bite a testament to the bakery’s commitment to quality and flavor. Whether you choose a classic chocolate cake or a more adventurous flavor, you won’t be disappointed.

The staff at Batavia Cafe are fantastic, providing friendly and attentive service that makes you feel right at home. Their warm smiles and willingness to go the extra mile ensure a pleasant dining experience. One of the most unique features of this cafe is the gents’ mirror urinal, a quirky addition that adds to the cafe’s distinctive character.

Overall, Batavia Cafe is a must-visit spot in Jakarta. Its combination of delicious cakes, fantastic service, and vintage vibe make it a perfect place to relax and enjoy a slice of history. Whether you’re a local or a tourist, this cafe offers a memorable experience that will leave you wanting to return again and again.​⬤

title: Café Batavia

text: It is a really nice restaurant. The staff are very helpful and polite.  The food is yummy, and the cocktail is very special . At night will have live music 🎶 😆😆🥰

text: What a good atmosphere, it was so lively, the live performance was incredibly amazing. Staff was great and helpful. Their cempedak was so tasty. The mocktail I chose wasn't really my taste 😅

title: Café Batavia
name: Omar Mohamed Abdelhady
text: One of the best places to visit in Jakarta. We went in the afternoon it wasn’t crowded, the drinks are decent, the service is great and the decoration is beautiful. We had a special experience in this place and we would definitely be back if we are ever in Jakarta again.

title: Café Batavia
name: Shaheen Shahmirzadi
text:
Cafe Batavia in Jakarta is a true gem that combines historic charm with a vibrant, lively atmosphere. From the moment you step inside, you’re transported back in time, surrounded by elegant colonial-era decor that exudes a unique old-world charm. But what really makes this place stand out are the people and the incredible vibes.

The staff at Cafe Batavia are some of the friendliest you’ll meet. They’re warm, welcoming, and go out of their way to make sure you’re comfortable and enjoying your time. Whether you’re a first-time visitor or a regular, they treat you like an old friend, which adds to the overall experience.

The live music is another highlight. During my visit, the music set the perfect tone for the evening, creating a lively yet relaxed ambiance. The band was fantastic, playing a great mix of tunes that had everyone tapping their feet and soaking in the good vibes. It’s the kind of place where the music enhances the atmosphere rather than overpowering it, making it easy to enjoy conversation or simply sit back and listen.

The combination of excellent service, great live music, and the unique setting makes Cafe Batavia a must-visit when in Jakarta. Whether you’re there for a meal, a drink, or just to enjoy the ambiance, you’re in for a memorable experience. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a slice of Jakarta’s rich history with a side of fantastic vibes!

title: Café Batavia
name: Ha Nguyen
text: Overall good experience with good food. But it’s a bit pricey

title: Café Batavia
name: 재현
text: awesome exotic interior for foreign travelers and taste of menus .i enjoyed a lots.

title: Café Batavia
name: Janna Garcia
text:
The first time i went here with my boyfriend was last January holiday. We decided to try it to have late lunch. As we entered, a group was going out because they didnt get a table. So we thought, "do we need a reservation?" So we asked, and because it's just the two of us, there's an available table. We had a very good view of kota tua from the second floor. We ordered their recommendations and we really liked their food. We wanted to have desserts but there's nothing on the menu so we asked again. Surprisingly, their dessert menu is at the back of their photo frame displays. The one nearest us was Princess Diana. Our server, Lutfi, was really cheerful and helpful as he patiently answered our questions and served us the whole time we were there.

Definitely going back here again. Food, ambience, and service are all great. If you're a big group, i suggest reserving a table in advance :)

title: Café Batavia

text:
We having a our dinner here, but even we spent 800,000 rupiah, 4 of us still feel hungry. Haha.

First they will ask you which floor are you prefer, so we request 2nd floor and window table, but has been told their rules on weekend is need to order 1 main course and 1 beverage for each person, no share.

Actually I don't mind, but my table is at the corner, I couldn't see outside, and all the dishes just normal, even the drink, nothing special.

Maybe next time I have to choose stage floor, can enjoy the band performance with my coffee and bakery.

title: Café Batavia

Great food and vibes here! We spent a couple of hours just enjoying the food and drinks while soaking in the heritage of what it feels like being a colonizer (extremely rare experience!) and being amongst them (a lot of European tourists).

The ground floor is air-conditioned with smoking allowed, while the floor upstairs is a no smoking space. From what we understood you'll have to book a table ahead if you'd prefer a table upstairs. We walked-in and settled for a table downstairs. However if you're looking for a nice photo by the window, upstairs would be the best choice.

Food was great. Service was good as well.

text: Despite being rather small, they do prepare good coffee. There's also a large selection of choices for coffee, which you can discuss with their BAErista. title: KINA
name: Robert Silaen
text:
​Nestled in the bustling area of Senopati, Kina emerges as a charming oasis with its unique concept. Adopting an old residential building, this cafe has been successfully redesigned with a modern touch, all while preserving its classic warmth and ambiance.
​Upon entering Kina, you'll be greeted by an incredibly familiar atmosphere, as if you're visiting a friend's home. The interior blends vintage elements with contemporary design, creating a space that is both comfortable and aesthetic. The modern and spacious bar area is a focal point, ready to serve a wide range of beverages, from coffee and tea to a diverse selection of alcoholic drinks. For those needing more privacy, Kina also provides a VIP meeting room perfect for business gatherings or private events.
​The food menu at Kina is highly diverse, featuring both local and international dishes. You can enjoy authentic classic Indonesian cuisine or try an international dish with an interesting twist. The quality of each dish is well-maintained, presented with beautiful plating and a flavor that delights the palate.
​Overall, Kina is the perfect place for various occasions. Whether it's for simply enjoying a coffee in the afternoon, having lunch with friends, or holding an important meeting, Kina offers an unforgettable experience. The blend of a cozy residential concept, delicious food, and a pleasant atmosphere makes Kina a must-visit destination for cafe enthusiasts in Jakarta.

title: KINA
name: Adinda Sheilla Alamanda
text:
I decided to visit KINA Senopati for Iftar, The reservation system here "Chope" is easy to use, efficient, and incredibly convenient. Booking a table was quick. They have a WhatsApp contact that’s responsive and helpful. I reached out for a few questions after booking, and I got quick, friendly replies.

The Asian dishes are great. The flavors are bold, full of spices. I had the “Nasi Goreng Saikoro” and the “Sundubu”. However, the Western dishes were a bit underwhelming. I tried the Sandwich and Pasta while it was fine, felt a little average compared to the Asian food.

As for the coffee, it was absolutely amazing. They offer a wide variety of coffee drinks, and every one I tried "The Kopi Susu Aren" and "The Roasted Peanut Latte" was smooth, rich, and perfectly brewed.  It’s clear they take their coffee seriously. Definitely will come again for their coffee!

title: KINA
name: Daryll Santillan
text:
Searched for a nice coffee shop to work at because I’m staying in the area and this was such a good find!

Highly rated and discoverable on social media but not all hype - I get why they’re dubbed as one of the best coffee shops by Chope.

Good selection of food with reasonable for Senopati prices, warm and quick service, and ambiance perfect for small meetings and co-working. Didn’t notice the time fly ba so stayed for almost two meals to finish work.

I had the roasted peanut latte, guacamole salad, and club house sandwich. Roasted peanut latte was really good, creamy texture with the right coffee kick. Guacamole salad with chicken was very smoky and flavorful, I just wish they could match the greens with a better dressing cos it was overbearing already. Clubhouse was good - packed, nothing special but curly fries was freshly cooked. Also had panna cotta which was very silky and not too sweet. Best paired with the iced americano.

Overall great experience. Point of improvement: their smoking area seemed full and at some point smoke was seeping into the dining area but wasn’t too bothersome for me. 👌🏼

The staff is always friendly, the toilet is clean, and they even provide pads and cotton buds in the bathroom.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: One Will
text:
The coffee at Common Grounds is the perfect way to start your day. Every cup is brewed to perfection, offering a rich and smooth taste that shows they really know their beans. Whether you like a strong espresso or a creamy latte, the quality is always top-notch and consistent.

​The service here truly stands out and makes you feel welcome. The staff members are friendly, energetic, and very helpful when you are trying to decide what to order. Even when the shop is crowded, they work quickly to make sure you aren't waiting too long for your caffeine fix.

​One of the biggest highlights is their dessert menu, specifically the Banana Nutella cake. This is hands-down the best cake they offer. It is incredibly moist and the combination of fresh banana and rich Nutella is a match made in heaven. You absolutely cannot leave without trying a slice.

​For those who need a spot to relax, the shop includes a very comfortable smoking area. It is well-ventilated and designed so that guests can enjoy their break without feeling isolated from the cool vibe of the cafe. It’s a great feature that makes the shop inclusive for everyone.

​Lastly, the location is incredibly strategic. It is situated right in the heart of a busy area, making it easy to find and the perfect spot to meet up with friends or colleagues. Because it is so central, it serves as a convenient landmark and a great home base for anyone spending the day in the city.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Anthony Steven
text:
A refined caffeine experience right in the heart of the city. ☕🌆 At Common Grounds Citywalk, I tried both the Hot Americano and Cappuccino — each cup offered a perfect harmony of roast, bean, and brew. Full-bodied yet smooth, with a finish that stays just long enough.

When to come? Weekdays at lunch can be packed — come earlier or after 2PM for better seating.

Vibe? Cozy yet urban, with a roastery’s soul and a café’s charm.

If you take your coffee seriously (but still want to sip in style), this is your place.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: David Christian
text:
Talking about the quality of F&B (coffee and meals) they offer here, I am very impressed. Went here twice, and we ordered hot cappuccino and flat white. Coffee is marvellous. I love the distinctive, deep, and rich taste of their coffee, which makes it stand out from most other coffee shops. We also ordered a maple glazed bacon for breakfast for 75k - consists of scrambled eggs, garlic baguette, and the bacon. It was really really good. F&B-wise, this place scores 5/5.

For the ambience, you need to remember this is located inside a mall. It's dominated with bright white accent without any exposure to natural light through windows. Not a huge fan of this kind of setting, much prefer the vibrant atmosphere of its Neo Soho outlet.

This is a really popular breakfast spot since it opens early at 8, and is located near an elite commercial area. So expect some crowd even early in the morning. That's why I don't find this place to be an appealing one to move my working spot to. This could be more appropriate for lunch meetings or quick chit chats. Ambience-wise, 3/5.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Yanly Riky
text:
My experience at Commongrounds Coffee Roasters in Citywalk Sudirman left me thoroughly impressed. The coffee here is truly exceptional, boasting a bold and high-quality taste that sets it apart from other coffee chains in the area.

In addition to their fantastic coffee selection, I also had the pleasure of trying their fried chicken waffle—a delightful surprise that perfectly balanced fragrant waffles with crispy, slightly sweet fried chicken. While not a common meal choice in Indonesia, it’s definitely worth a try!

However, the only drawback was the limited space and packed atmosphere. The place felt a bit cramped. Despite this, the outstanding coffee and unique menu offerings make Commongrounds Coffee Roasters a must-visit for any coffee lover looking for something special.

text:
Came with girlfriends so it's 5 of us.

1. Sat on the table where we found out that there was a table with a better spot, right beside window, we actually made the short vlog for instagram, and when we proposed to waiter to move there, he did not allow us because it was "reserved", but turned out we were there until evening, not even one guest coming to that table. 😊

2. It is not allowed to bring our own tumbler. Helooo, in nowadays where people are aware of health and hydration, also to reduce plastic use, and you still use the old rules? Come on, I brought tumbler, not packaged drinks whatsoever.

3. I was offered vallet parking but I refused, so I parked by myself, and turned out I had to pay the same price as vallet. Seriously? Also the price was 20k per flat, it is so expensive.

For the bill we had to pay for 5 of us, tumbler forbidden, expensive parking, lie about the "reserved table", then that was the first and the last time I went there, and will not recommend this venue to any of my relatives.

name: Maria Hany
text:
they have indoor and outdoor area. the outdoor is designed like garden with pebbles. I do think it will be better if they have large fan for better circulation and table could be more sturdy or that the pebbles is styled in the side.

other than that it was a great place and good food with live music and great service as well

title: KINA
name: Antonius Arya Hadiputra
text: The only positive experience was the warm greeting from the front desk upon arrival. Unfortunately, the waitstaff seemed distracted, spending more time on their phones than attending to customers. Our order took far too long to arrive and was served cold. The food and drinks are overpriced, making it a place people might visit just for beverages rather than a full meal. To top it off, there wasn’t even a simple thank you as we left.

title: KINA
name: Megia Tamsil
text: Server was so friendly and responsive, cozy, prayer room available at 2nd floor. Tried sea salt caramel latte, so creamy and legit as for side dishes I ordered fried calamari . It was good. Reasonable price. WFC friendly. Valet service 20K

title: KINA
name: Nadya Hafiyya
text: great ambience, food, and service! definitely will come back againn

title: KINA
name: Aeysha
text: Definitely my favorite south based go-to place! Loved the ambience with a great outdoor area. Always had a pleasant time here! Thank you to Mas Akbar :)

title: KINA
name: Dave Packers R.
text:
A good place to meet with friends, the place has good ambience.
It was a satisfying burger and the coffee also good.
The staff are friendly and helpful.
The price is reasonable.
Last Friday nite they had live music at 8pm.

title: KINA
name: Nida
text:
One of the prettiest coffee shops I’ve been to in jakarta. Ample of parking space too if you bring your car . Their food selections were very interesting and definitely not like other cafes I’ve been too. Loved that they served from classic Indonesian comfort meals to peculiar tastes like chapaguri. Highly quick and attentive service too

The sundubu I ordered was the right portion and taste. For drinks I got the turmeric and tamarind jamu, also another banget

title: KINA
name: Sisilia Mariestha
text:
Overall a bad experience. The waiter messed up my order, food came cold and took more than 20mins to be served.
The waiters also seemed to be inattentive, making it a bit frustrating to have to call them.

But props to the front desk lady for being accommodating, she did great.

title: KINA
name: Teresa Tridiva
text: kina has always been my go to spot with friends on weekends. beyond the warm ambience, the service is outstanding (special thanks to mas akbar for the hospitality and sharp memory). will surely return for the tahu cabe garam and the cozy vibe!!!

title: KINA
name: shafira fitriar
text: Cozy place, the furniture has taste match with the light and retro vibes. This is my fav husband's coffee to go cafe. Overall love it.

title: KINA
name: deri slyrova
text:
Space-wise is good. You can choose the indoor (with air cond) semi outdoor (with no air cond). Also there is a smoking room with air cond.
The seat made from solid wood, can be tough if you were sitting for long time.
For pricing, is lil bit expensive, why i said this- compared to place in the area, price is same, but the portion is so small.
They serve long black in glass for liquor for 40k. Also the spaghetti serving in soup plate. Which very small.
Came here at Saturday night, very crowded, and they serve live music. If you want to sit in the backyard, you get charge 100k per pax. Either you will end up pay more coz the portion is small, imo.

title: KINA
name: Annisa Diana
text: Vintage themed cafe with many open areas available. There is a praying room for moslem at second floor. For office meeting you can book/reserve through the staffs. They open from 9 am to midnight. I went here until 9 pm, unfortunately you can not order coffee after 8 pm.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Tri Alexander
text:
Good place to hang out with friends and family. Was there in uncrowded time between lunch time and tea time. The indoor upstairs was kind of damp and hot. Air con was not doing its job properly.

Many food stalls to choose with a friendly face and services.

Place was quite clean but need to remind you that outdoor means sharing with mosquitos, some flies and many cute felines… only be kind to the feline, canine and other pets please… did I mention you can bring your pets here?

Thank you

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: aL RD
text:
A cool place for hang out, wfc, chitchat ot just relax. Like food courr concept, there are some tenants.
• Taste: 4/5, varied for all tenants, mostly there are dlicious.
• Menu: 4/5 , a varied tenants (steak, beer, coffee, chinese food, cake, hotdog, many more)
• Ambience: 4/5, 2 level outdoor & indoor area,
• Price: moderate
• Location: around neighbourhood
• Services: very friendly staff
• Parking lot: 2.5/5, no official parking lot, only in the roadside
• Overall: 3.9/5

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Fre Tob
text: Another great place to visit, and another option for ppl to find a nice place to hang out. It’s a one place that has many store choices so ppl can sit and eat/drink in anywhere they like. There’re indoor and outside area, which outdoor area more spacious. The main cafe there is Sejiwa kopi from Bandung. And other tenants that I like is Nasgero. Will definitely come again to try other tenants.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Gideon
text:
Good place to chill!

Visited this place on a Saturday Afternoon.

Parking is limited and Valet Parking only for parking around the place.

There are some tenants options
1st floor
- Sejiwa Coffee
- Lucy Steak and Buns
— Bespoke Bikeshop
— Lee Cooper Retail

2nd floor
- Beer Mart
- Food court with
— Rawon Bar
— T1 chinese style takeaway
— Cosmic Dog
— Nasgero
— Beermart
- Barbershop

- Food are okay, price relatively okay for this kind of area 80-120k/ dishes. Definitely a semi fancy place to chill.

- Staffs are nice, but given the plenty outdoor seating with no staffs around making it feel weird spending 80-120k for such dishes.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Randy
text: a good place to hangout. they have beer and coffee tenant and some good food like nasgero and rawon bar. easy to car park by valet service. motorcyle will cost you 5K IDR, if you want, you can park in Blok M Square and walk to this for cheaper motorcycle park.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: arief taufik
text: Nice, cozy, with very calm ambience. Not many tenants, but it is just enough option to choose for the f&b

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Larasati Putri
text:
Flat iron steak is a MUST TRY! very smokey, tender, but still have meaty texture, it’s just perfect. I recommend house gravy for the sauce. Steak menu exclude the vegetable side dish, so you have to order separately for it. Portion pretty small if you’re very hungry haha

A little complain for the service, they are not very friendly :(

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Shirleen Sheila
text: Nice place to hangout with friends, but when rain is fall needs to move. Love the coffee rum from sejiwa coffee and the nasgero

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Pitta Sekar Wangi
text:
Love the tenants! We had buns from Lucy Steak Buns for late lunch and a Padiluwih lager to pair from Lucy Beer Mart.
Lot of standing fan around outdoor area, thank you very much you are thoughtful, Lucy!

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: V
text:
Finally ventured to this trending compound in the city, primarily drawn by the curiosity to explore the dessert shop called The Moody Baker that had been the talk of the town. Little did I know that the compound itself would be a delightful discovery. The design is an artful blend of modern aesthetics, seamlessly integrating comfortable indoor spaces with inviting outdoor areas.

As I navigated through the compound, I uncovered a world of culinary delights and experiences. From a captivating beer spot to a cozy coffee shop, a variety of food stalls ( NASGERO is one of my go to if I was in the city), and even a photobox that's an essential for any Gen Z hotspot – it's a well-rounded haven that caters to diverse tastes and preferences.

The Moody Baker didn't disappoint either. Matilda and Rhum Tartmisu is a must try. However, what truly surprised me was the compound's overall charm. It's not just a destination for satisfying one's sweet tooth, but an immersive experience that invites visitors to indulge in the flavors of life.

This place has something to enchant everyone.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Karinka Febritta Anindyasari
text: The place is pretty crowder. Perfect place to meet up with friends or colleagues. There’s no escape if you are sitting outdoor. The food is just okay, i tried rawon bar and sejiwa drinks.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Stefanus Stefanus
text:
Parking slots need to be VALET but the valet staffs are not doing their job.

I came on Wednesday 23/10/24, 13.45
First ever VALET SERVICE that i need to go to the staff to handover the key and pay the rate while they are playing their phone. They don’t even care to move the safety cone, so i need to do everything on my own.

Worse case is THEY DON’T care to guide you while you are parking! They only came for getting the money!

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Rina
text: Nice places, nice ambiance, greenery, but not have a great food and beverages yet, will try for more

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Dan Borzutzky
text: Beautiful place, but STOP SERVING ALL COFFEE IN PLASTIC CUPS!!! Your default option should always be glass cups... Take care of the planet please! 🌴♻️

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Saif
text: Great place to hang out, a lot of options to choose whether it's food or drinks.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Marcia Marsheilla
text: Super content! We tried Lucy steak and buns, went there with friends and they felt the same. Great ambience, will definitely come back and try other restaurants

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Prima Wijaya
text: Cool place to hang out and dine, various food options available. Must try: Han Noodle, Nasgero and Nasi Kandar.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Edwin Indera
text:
Delicacy!
Second floor is quite comfortable for iftar break, the special menu is on point. Tasty, and the price is fair.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Liesna Subianto
text: Nice place to hang out with friends. Lots of food around 😊

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Ruby Riyadi
text:
Comments:
The food and drink: The food was delicious, the chicken fried was crunchy. The drink is so-so.
The environment: The restaurant is not spacious, sometimes faced a long queue, in peak a hour.
The service: the service was so-so.
The price: affordable price
Overall experience: it thinks the restaurant is worth to try for curry food lovers.
Advice for reader: I recommend you buy nasi biryani kandar with chicken fried. It decided to back!. However, don’t ride the car to the restaurant because a parking lot is limited and the parking fares is quite pricey.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Rahul Chawra
text: It's a great great place to begin your day. Some fabulous all day breakfast as well as several other main course choices. But then what they truly do really well is not the food but the coffee. With a roastery located right here, be assured that you'll get some amazing coffee at any point in time. I like discussing the origins as well as the brewing methods with the baristas who are pretty well-versed in what they do. If I'm staying around Sudirman, this is my usual choice when it comes to starting my day. Good job guys

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Sapiderman Lumpat
text:
Good coffee and delicious food.
Taste : 4.5/5 doesn't disappoint
Service : 4/5 fast and friendly
Seating : 3/5 standard. Inside and outside seating
Price : 3/5 not bad. Worth it.

Recommended : Try the burrito bowl! Steak and eggs or the Grilled Chicken Pesto. Really Good!

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Josh H
text:
Go to this coffee spot for first time.

Taste vs price? Were okay but nothing special.
Park space? Quite big and nice.
Services? Nice & responsive.

Recommended? Yes for brunch and wait for something else.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Marya Budianta
text: The food is delicious, although it is given in small portions. The salmon is well cooked and I love the sauce. The atmosphere is very nice, I like the design of the room, tables and walls. I would suggest that they lower the price of the apple juice, it's quite small compared to the size advertised in the menu. Or they might increase the serving size and keep the same price.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: me mivanch
text: It's one of my favorite place. Good food, good coffee.  Open early, I think around 8 am. The price quite high.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: indrawan designworks
text: At first I guess they only serve coffee and light meals, in fact they serve various kind of food,from mexican,western,and asian. The taste and plating are very good, unfortunately they lack of staff for serving the customer. So be ready waiting for being served quite long. You should try having some meals when visiting citywalk, and having a different experience within.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Nanang Setiawan
text:
Meetup point. Coffe & sandwich is great. Separated private space is avalaible.
Will be much better if the music volume turn down a bit.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Kenny Kurniawan
text:
Today i ordered their mocha lotus cake and their special beverage pistachio matcha latte.
The cake is quite big, and taste good. The pistachio matcha latte also nice.

The place also okay for hang out or chilling, the staff was friendly. Overall, okay to try, even personally, the price is quite pricey.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: The Traveling Cow
text: Food is delicious, coffee is great. Menu is extensive with a lot of option, mostly breakfast menu but also main dishes. Price is decent. They also serve non-halal menu if you have concern on that.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Pantou
text: Tried their magnum collab dessert. The presentation is sooo pretty! Tasted good too!

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Jason Budiarto
text: Always at their best. Been eating so many times and always satisfied with their dishes. Also you should try their coffee because it is so aussie. They also always consistent with their servings and food quality. Very recommended to come here with your family and friends!! 👍🏻👍🏻

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Irving
text:
An established Indonesian coffee chain with Melbourne style food and drinks. The venue is well-lit and not too noisy, making it a perfect place for meetings.
The food is good, definitely above the average cafe food in Jakarta. Although it is a bit pricey for cafe food, you definitely get what you pay for.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Jocelyn Wang
text: Love it so much. From coffee to salad to dessert. Will definitely visit again when I m in Jakarta.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Fy EAD
text:
typical of coffee shop.
Hello and greeting!
Sending appreciation and best regards,
been joining the team of Common Grounds Group about 9 months.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Rindu A. Prihadi
text:
Common grounds has always been my go-to cafe for breakfast-lunch. Foods including the pastry are bestt.

Anw its my first time in ciwalk sudirman, love the ambience, music, staffs are nice, place is cleannn.

My 2 favs: caesar salad & almond croissant.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Rocky Joseph Pesik
text: Good place to have coffee. I like their manual brew, and they have around 6-7 selection of coffees to choose. Food is good too

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Victor Lin
text: The main branch of Common Grounds Coffee Shops. Their coffee is never disappointing. One of the best coffee shops and roasters in Jakarta. Home of champions.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Indira Jauzā
text: Pleasant experience. Food selections are great ranging from Indonesian to Western food. awesome service, staffs are helpful & lovely.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Keisha Glori
text:
Food was good but the price was really expensive for the portion and type of cafe. Enjoyed my experience there with really friendly waiters.

The coffee is roasted well and I love the varieties of choices they have

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Irma Maulida
text: One of my fav cafes in jakarta. Their coffee is unbeatable. Order burrito bowl and latte this time 💜

title: KINA
name: Lutfia Maulidina
text: cute and the staff are nice. suitable for hang out

title: KINA
name: Hans Jonathan
text: This cafe is tucked away in the back alley of Senopati. The green vegetation and wooden interior invited you to slow down. Came here for an event, ordered Wagyudon for brunch. Their beef rice bowl was just okay, it was a brunch package paired with lychee tea. I also tried their latte, which is good. I imagine this is an okay option for WFC if you are in the area. Their @kopikina is also a coffee bean distributor for a lot of the coffee shops in Jakarta, so do inquire about it to the owner if you are into coffee beans.

title: KINA
name: Sarita Anggun Kinanti
text: The place is nice, demure, with plugs on every table. I ordered salted caramel latte and the taste was so good. Overall a good place for WFC

title: KINA
name: Thias
text: Here for workmates breakfasting. Choosing breakfasting menu and it's ready before adzan. The portion was quite big for sharing. And loved the salted latte.

title: KINA
name: Ibnu Mulyanto (IBeNimages)
text:
Good coffee.. plenty of beans selection for V60.. ordered Mandailing V60, and it was server nice and smooth.

nice ambience with vintage vibe… semi outdoor place is available, but with smoking space we chose not to sit there.

spacious parking space

title: KINA
name: Nizar Ilhamy
text:
The place is quite cozy, indoor smoking area is vintage, which is cool.
The food and coffee is 9/10.
Repeat order-able.

title: KINA
name: patricia oktaviani
text:
Not only Coffee the serve main Food also,the Food portions were generous,, the taste was also reasonable for the Foods,, the staffs were friendly and helpfull,, the place ambiance was Good,, Will definitely recommend this planet to friends, that love Coffee but also want to  "makan berat" ,, love it

Must try: The gyu don wagyu worth to Try,, 5/5

Crispy mushroom : It tasted like they used truffle only I guess, pretty Good

title: KINA
name: Darell Mahardhika
text:
Delicious coffee at this window shop coffee hidden behind the busy road. Staff are super friendly!

Busy with locals this is the best coffee by far across several blocks. Flat white on the footpath. just what I needed on a chilly Jakarta Night in January.

Friendly folk, not the too cool variety you find in some places. Delicious coffee. Good job Kina!

definitely 9/10 ☕️✨

title: KINA
name: Zabrina Sulatiasto
text: very lively place with good foods and drinks🖤🖤🖤

title: KINA
name: Jonathan Wirawan
text: The place is nice and cozy.  For the food and drink itself , I ordered their egg benedict and iced latte when I came here last week.  The egg benedict is really good because the yolks on the poached eggs are still runny , the english muffin is toasted perfectly and the hollandaise sauce are delicious too.

title: KINA
name: Nadya
text:
Visited on Saturday. The place is easy to find, with spacious parking space. The interior is also spacious, clean, and nicely decorated (mostly brown-ish tone with wood furnitures). They have co-working space  2nd floor, and outdoor seating for those who want to enjoy some fresh air or those who want to smoke. Love the place, eventho when we visited, there's a problem with the electricity so the fan (for outdoor seating) doesnt work.

The staffs are nice and friendly. They also serve a lot of beverages varieties, e.g. coffee based, yogurt, fruit/tea based, jamu, and they even serve alcohol. Tried their manual brew (they have wide range of single origin choices you can choose, from flores bajawa to papua moanemani natural!) and their "The Pink Studio, 1911" (blended coconut water with strawberry and almond) and they both taste greeeeaat!
The price is reasonable, the taste itself is worth the price. Would come back here to try another drink and their food!

title: KINA
name: Ferrani Nafy
text: best place to hangout, love the ambience, food and beverages! also the staff kak daniel is nice & humble ☺️

title: KINA
name: Janatun Naimah
text:
good place for chill with friends, good
food, good ambience 👏🏻

title: KINA
name: Leonardo dosduo
text: A nice and vintage Coffee Shop, they provide outdoor area and two indoor smoking room, the outdoor are can be quite Hot on sunny day. Nice and cozy place, quite pricey (up 40k for a cup of Coffee), parking space quite limited, but they provide valet service.

title: KINA
name: Vilia Ciputra
text:
Their Berry Late with real crushed Berry was pretty unique and refreshing. A new cafe in town, the design and concept were pretty homey. They have classic stereo set and gramophone, which is amazing to me.

Sitting spaces were spacious and you could choose between indoor and outdoor. Coworking package is available, too.

title: KINA
name: Geraldine Luisa
text: Ambiencenya ok bgt bgt bgt buat wfc, ngobrol & duduk lama”. Very cozy!! And homey bgt ❤️ food was great with generous portion. Would def come back. Thanks mas danin

title: KINA
name: Puspapelanggi
text: I went to have a lunch with my collegues. I guess because it was friday afternoon. The chef is praying maybe, and it took quite a long time to wait our meals. But it taste so good, I ordered a double steak (meat and chicken), the portion is too big for me. I loved their earl grey gula aren milk tea, and their signature coffee but I forgot the name.

title: KINA
name: Monica Decianti
text: Came on an iftar time, crowded but managed to have a table.

title: KINA
name: Almira Zada
text: Cozy place to hangout! They have indoor and outdoor area. The indoor interior offers old vibes design. Great service and good variations of foods and drinks. The price is acceptable

title: KINA
name: Muhammad Syarif
text: A cafe and eatery that is so homey, I'd say the concept is simple but so clever. It feels if you are familiar with interior design books by TeNeues or Pageone, Kina really gives you that! Shout-out for the very friendly and knowledgeable server and baristas! I just tried their latte, it was nice and almond with turmeric mik, pretty unique one.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Adelia Dinda Sani
text: The place is really nice, I love its cool, relaxing ambience and its strategic position being not too far from the city center. The restaurant is quite spacious, and it's perfect for a group hang out or a casual dinner for a good me-time after office hour or even during weekend.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Gilbert Suriawidjaja
text: Great food and surroundings. The steaks and soup was great, and the surrounding has lots of other foods and pkaces to visit before or after the meal here. Recommended for people living nearby blok M or blok B, or tourists in general. Worth it.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Febriando Irsal
text:
We tried cosmic dog, and it was ok. Also had sejiwa's ice americano, its not freshly made.

The place is quite nice for afternoon hangout, night time is also good with the outdoor lighting.

The best place to sit is the outdoor area, keep in mind there's no roof in the outdoor area.

Not many parking space but there is valet service.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Anastasia Yuanita (Leon)
text: A good place to hangout with lots of food and beverages options. Many seats available for groups. Good ambience.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Takeshi Kiri
text:
Large, cute, affordable, mostly small food joints with a convenience mart that sells beer that is not cheap (Rp 110k for Budweiser), a bike repair shop, and a barber shop focused on men. Crowded with a young early college aged crowd who study, hang out, eat, and socialize.

Ps. Very local scene, staff at the shops and stalls don't speak English so I do not recommend foreigners come here unless you're with an Indonesian speaking friend.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Diptyandhito Agung
text:
Went to Sejiwa, coffee tasted preety good.
Such a busy place, gotta wait for 15-20 minutes to get the table if you're lucky. But this is a good place to have a conversation till midnight while most of cafe in Jakarta close at 10-11 PM

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Aryo Indarto
text: Great food selection, tried the Rawon, Nasi Goreng and dessert selection. The big downside of this place is it’s not smoking free zone. It’s bad to eat while in the smokey environment. Unless this policy change , won’t come back here again.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Nuska Rianggono
text: Good place for either having have heavy meal,light meal or just having a cup of coffee

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Sha
text: Nice atmosphere but can offer more exciting tenants. It was a very limited choice and rather boring

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Dwi Aji Sentosa (Aji)
text:
Food : I ate nasgero - nasi goreng bang gero at the 2nd floor. The fried rice is unique, it used Briyani rice. So it's not a common Indonesian friend rice.

The taste is quite unique, not to strong but not to light. Just enough. But i like it.
The portion is also enough for me.

Place : This Nasgero is one of the stall in LCC. For Muslim, please be aware, because i think there are some non halal stalls in LCC. But the place is Quite nice to hangout with your friends. The second floor is closed at 10pm.

Service : just good. Nothing special.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Chris Chandra (foodisable)
text: Nice greeny nook for a good bite and relax with friends

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: GyooJin Choi
text: Good place to enjoy Jinro soju. It is perfect.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Nuala Rex
text: I came here because I wanted to try eating the nasi kandar here.  For me, the condition of the place was not comfortable because the room was small and closed.  I ordered chicken nasi kandar. For me, the nasi kandar was not tasty, the rice at that time was undercooked.  The chicken is delicious and the skin is crispy.  The Apollo cuttlefish was a bit fishy.  I'm a bit disappointed because seeing the ad on Tik Tok is very tempting

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Annisa Nugrahany
text:
I went to LCC on saturday afternoon. It was super crowded and we almost didn’t get table. I ate basque cheesecake from The Moody Baker. Delicious to my liking. Creamy and not too sweet.
The place is suited for hangout. It’s surrounded by trees which makes it pretty comfy. They have many food options like coffee, dessert, main course (nasi goreng, steak, burger, etc). They also got barbershop, photobox, and other stuff you can do while hangout. I would say LCC is like M Bloc Space but fancier.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Galih Pratama
text: Actually the vibes it's nice. Comfy & homey. The coffee that was Great even though a bit pricey. Second floor there's nasi goreng it's delish. The ambience it's South jakartans

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Groza Subhakty
text: Another new place near the hype of  Gunawarman. I tried the  HK Stir Fried Beef Noodles from T1 , not bad!  and quick served..character for stir fried cooking ofc. Nasgero tasted very ordinary...nothing special!   Located on the second floor. The dining area is food court style, warm and windy from the big fan installed. Not good.   They should add more air conditioning for more comfort.  Well it is new..just opened few days ago. Three and a half stars is more acurate.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Febs Febs
text: One of the nicest coffee shop in the neighborhood. Not only they have coffee shop but also some few restaurant. Don’t be fooled with the photos on internet, the tribun is actually quiet small, but still comfortable.

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Dhita Ms.
text: For real Imma back for their pasta and Nasgero!

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Aulia Octav
text: Nice food from warpas, good ambience, awesome people here!!

title: LCC - Lucy Curated Compound Adityawarman
name: Shella Huliselan
text:
This hainan chicken rice by Nagata at LCC is super delicious...the chicken, the rice are the best...will back again for sure..
You must try their fish skin too, 1 will be never enough...

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Hasnasgf
text:
Visited because of the Christmas decoration hype 🎄
Came by public transport around late lunchtime. Parking is limited and paid. Waited about 15 minutes before being seated.

Most guests came picture-ready, usually in groups of friends or family.

Food: Tried Homemade Bolognese, Creamy Carbonara, and Iced Tea. Decent for the price but nothing stood out. Portions weren’t filling for me. Tableware differed from the menu photos (food was served in plastic bowls instead of glass/melamine).

Service: Food came quickly despite the crowd. Staff were attentive and pleasant.

Ambience: Crowded but still enjoyable. Lots of people taking photos and videos. Comfortable for chatting with friends. Toilet was clean and well-equipped.

Overall: Best if you come for the vibe, okay if you come for the food.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Lisandra WP
text:
Oni House Jakarta has really cute and charming decorations. We visited during Valentine’s season, and the themed decor was adorable every little detail felt cutesy and well thought out, which made the experience fun and visually pleasing.

Food wise, everything was pretty average. The prices were reasonable, but nothing really stood out in terms of taste. The desserts, in particular, weren’t great for me still okay, but not something I’d come back for specifically.

Overall, I’d rate it around 3.5/5. Oni House is interesting and enjoyable for the ambience and decoration, but if you’re looking for a place mainly for good food, this might not be the top choice. It’s more about the experience than the meals.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Haryanti Ari
text:
I've been wanting to go to this cafe for a long time, since 2017. The decor always changes depending on the event, like Christmas, Halloween, Valentine's Day, but I don't know about Eid 😁
Even Christmas has different decor every year. I forgot who it was with, but I was greeted warmly and even got a discount 🫶😁The palm sugar and hokkaido coffees are quite similar in taste. I haven't tried the menu yet, but I'll come back next time with friends and family.
I happened to pass by yesterday and immediately turned around to go to Onni House.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Clarissa Graciela Nahak
text: Tried their christmas special, food took forever to be served esp the Rosemary Roast Chicken (1hr+ waiting time), I don't think they were prepared to serve the christmas menu 🥲 the beef stroganoff was alright, but the chicken was very dry. Would recommend coming just for the atmosphere and lovely decor not for the food.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Mariana Mariana
text:
Just found this cafe last minute before our wed anniv. Good place , comfortable, love the ambience. A lot of people take a photo. Because the decoration is amazing.
Suitable for dinner, hang out and all event tho. For food just vanilla.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Monica Decianti
text:
Complimentary americano or cafe latte for each order of cake.
Mini lava malted cake with vanilla ice cream is a must

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Grisel Ginting
text: Still Christmas vibes even tho it's jannn🎄 Irish Baileys Cake 👍🏼best to come before or after Christmas :)

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Ian
text:
What I love most about this place is its ambiance. The playlist, decorations, and furniture are superb! They use wooden tables and chairs, with cute touches from the giant teddy bear dolls.

I only ordered Earl Grey Chocolate, which was refreshing. It's a tea, but also a chocolate drink.

The staff are helpful and friendly. They gave recommendations and demonstrated product knowledge when I asked.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Dessy Sundari
text: I visited this place in early December 2025. I love the Christmas vibe, the foods and how warm the staff were.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: P S D
text:
Invited to attend an engagement procession at this coffee shop / restaurant.

The place itself got a beautiful interior and a cozy vibes.

Unfortunately the foods that we ordered tasted below our expectations, my dori fish tasted flat but the soup is too sour, others main dishes taste too salty, maybe cause they make it in a huge batches.

The best part is the finger foods, great taste and in a good portion

Service was attentive and they got a good team.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Enggi Dewanti
text: Love the ambience, the meal sets are amazing. And the christmas decor is 10/10.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Catharina Yuma
text: Having my first experience at Onni House and ordering spaghetti carbonara + Irish Baileys. Both are just PERFECT!!! not too sweet, but still great for a sweet-tooth. The service was nice, they will greet you and explain things nicely. great ambience to work as well. Great job Onni House!

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Silmi H
text:
The Swedish meatball tastes sooo good in my opinion 🥹

Tried the tiramisu alsoo so goood 😍 (they give free coffee!)

I also love the ambience of this resto. Surely will back!!

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Franky Saputra
text: Food taste good , abudance portion. Service and atmosphere really good. We'll come back !

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Chrisna Mafs
text:
good ambience, nice ladies staffs, food recommendations ; beef sambal matah fried rice, gulai salmon rice set, creamy carbonara. and don’t forget to try walnut butter cake for the dessert (not too sweet).
clean area and cozy place for meet up with friends. they also have private room which can accommodate 12-15 people.

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Dhian Megarani
text:
I really love the christmas decor at Onni House Jakarta, it’s beautiful. Even though the waiting time can be a bit long, it’s absolutely worth it once the food arrives. The dishes are delicious, especially the crispy dory in batter with the spicy and sour sauce, which was my favorite. The tiramisu was also amazing and a perfect way to end the meal.
Thank you, Onni House, for such a lovely dining experience!

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Arline
text:
Finally we can visit and enjoy tea time at this thematic Onni House at Tanjung Duren area.

Recommended :
Fresh Ice lemon tea 8/10
Irish baileys cake 8/10
Ice coffee latte 8/10

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Arief
text:
Onni House located at Jl.Tanjung Duren Utara V - West Jakarta
Parking is limited but there will be attendant will assist to get park.
Dining area is spacious ,  clean and neat ( available smoking area )
Except restaurant they also florist...nice vibes
( late review post )

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Nadia Ayu
text: Ordered steak & frittes, miso caesar salad and salmon fried rice. The portion was decent and enough for 1 person eventho in the menu looks big. Salad was good but the egg kinda overcooked. Steak was cooked perfectly as we like and melt in your mouth ✨ nasi goreng was a bit oily and the portion quite big. Overall good for first try 👍🏻

title: ONNI House Jakarta
name: Marco Lie
text: Nice place. Good ambiance. Foods are so so. The only one taste good only pasta creamy carbonara. Opor baked rice and javanese fried rice it's pretty bad. And the coffee, even with extra shot, just so so. Iced chocolate nice.

title: KINA
name: Riri Astarina
text: My type of place. Ambience is super nice. I can work there but at 8 PM they started to turn the lamp off lol. The waiter is able to explain — knowledgeable. I ordered Wagyu Don where the rice is exchangeable to Shirataki. Perfect! Then Ice Japanese Coffee. They said the beans are Cups of Excellence which this is very nice. The taste is bitter but floral. Recommended. Last one, the Banoffee. It was the bomb. My favorite! 🖤🖤🖤 they also have photo booth which is super cute. Definitely will come back. Thanks, Kina. Can we kiss forever? 😆

For my spaghetti aglio olio, the portion was a bit small, but perfect for my liking. I felt that the tables were too crowded at the area where I sat, so it's a bit uncomfortable. food is good, place was OK, unfortunately i got a seat in front of the man's toilet that are not really comfy🥲 the toilet are always closes and opens and it's quite uncomfy to look at inside of the toilets while eating, try to change the door to automatic door that keeps it closed pls

Unfortunately the meal that we ordered just so so:
Wagyu Chapaguri
Spicy Korean noodle, with wagyu slices, porto mushroom dan pakchoy

Rosemary duck confit
slow cooked duck, grated with rosemary served with baby potatoes, asparagus and mixed peppers
(took a lot of energy to chew the duck)

For my drink, I ordered hot chocolate.

We got complimentary desserts: espresso panna cotta dan banoffee. I love espresso panna cotta a lot! This one totally recommended sekali.

Health protocol applied here, loves the ambiance, totally worth the visit.

Loved the calm yet refined ambiance — perfect for brunch dates, slow dinners, or even quiet meetings. It’s one of those places that makes you feel good just by being there. Highly recommended!

name: Abdul Aziz
text: Coffee is okay for the price level. But the barista made me wait 30 minutes for a cup of manual filter coffee.

title: Common Grounds Coffee and Roastery CS
name: Ivonne X
text:
I recently visited this restaurant, and unfortunately, my experience left much to be desired. I have specific dietary restrictions due to an autoimmune condition, so I was extra cautious with my order.

When I asked whether the mashed potatoes contained dairy, one staff member initially dismissed my concern with a “it’s just mashed potato, no milk” comment. I wasn’t convinced, as many mashed potatoes typically include cream. After insisting, another employee went to check and confirmed that cream was indeed used. This inconsistency in staff knowledge was disappointing.

I ordered a chicken salad and mineral water, intending to eat a small sweet potato I have brought for my important carbs- a necessary part of my diet since I cannot consume most grains, shaded potatoes, dairy, or soy—due to my autoimmune. However, an employee informed me that bringing outside food wasn’t allowed. I understand the rationale behind restaurant policies, but enforcing a strict “no outside food” rule without considering individual special case dietary needs can be quite counterproductive. The staff’s inflexible approach led me to cancel my order entirely which was both disappointing and a loss for both parties. If there is a gluten free or grain free carb available there I would definitely order, in which they don’t.

I hope the management considers training their staff better on ingredients and offering more flexibility to accommodate special dietary needs. A little empathy and creative problem-solving can go a long way. It is just a small sweet potato for an autoimmune survivor to go along with their main salad dish.

We came here after finding out that the barista is an Indonesian Barista Champion and so our expectations were set quite high. We ordered their cappuccinos and single origin coffees from Ethiopia and Panama, these hit the spot for our caffeine rush and were the best we’ve had.

They also have a full breakfast menu, we highly recommend the huevos rendang and breakfast chicken burritos. Don’t skip on the waffles either, their sweet concoctions were made to perfection.

This was a highlight of our trip in Jakarta, would highly recommend all around!
