text: Hype & vibe matcha stall packed with queue as the place is small with limited 8 tables and seats. Worth waiting to try for its matcha jelly (the best). My picks are the matcha & hojicha gelato and matcha soft serve anmitsu.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: david async
text:
The location is on the LG floor, I went there with my friend, and we bought

1. uji match soft serve story. I love the sweetness, it's not punchy. The Dango is also good, it's chewy.  The red beans are also nice
This dish consists of 3 layers
- The first layer is Matcha Ice Cream a little bit bitter but not that punchy
- The second is something like jelly, I'm not sure if it is coconut or else with sweet thick milk,
- and the last layer is something like granola or grain, crunchy.

In my opinion, this dish has a good texture

2. Genmai Matcha Chizu, there's a cheesy layer and a matcha layer. I order with a low sugar level, The sweetness is just right

3. Hanami dango matcha story, this dish is ordered by my friend, and she said the dish is a little bit bitter. The concept is quite similar to the soft serve story with 3 layers of dish

4. Hanami Dango Chizu, this drink is also ordered by my friend, the taste is just right, and the dango is the same as the matcha story dish

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Balidronelady
text: Over crowded in the small store, better to take away the menu. Staffs friendly but long que, mana tahan nunggu. The matcha is okay not really special but the portion is quite generous w that price.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Sara Maynard
text:
I love the service here and if you like matcha that is not too sweet this is the place

Service is amazing.

Tried the mochi and ice cream,definitely recommend it. Unfortunately the dine in place has a small space

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Shafira Putri
text:
I dont really like matcha but this place is pretty good. Their menu is either matcha flavored or sakura flavored. They look very pretty and taste great. Their matcha is super vibrant so I think they use ceremonial grade matcha.

I ordered one beverage, the pure matcha with no milk and the mille crepe. Both looked pretty and taste good.

The place is very small though. But highlight of my visit here is the server’s hospitality!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Lowilius Wiyono
text: The matcha was great. I ordered their harumi matcha soft serve. They taste nicely with flavorful topping

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: One Spoon
text:
Ordered their Uji Matcha Soft Serve.

Surprised by how good it was.
Very flavorsome and interesting bowl with many items with different textures. Absolutely loved it.

Tried also a bite of their layered matcha crepes cake. Very good too, not overly sweet.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Felicia MB
text:
Always be my favorite matcha dessert place!!
- All matcha-based dessert have strong matcha flavor, not sweet and very light.
- Sakura mile crepe was almost tasteless. Hope they will add another little hint of sakura taste
- Sakura tea was a little bit sweet, but still good to accompany the dessert.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Yuki Sabrina
text:
this matcha would be the best of all in this mall .. umami and the notes are all balanced with the Harumi Cold Whisk. Even if the ice melts into the matcha, the taste and notes are still going very strong.

the service was very accomodating too .. might dine in one day

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Kevin Bryan
text: Great matcha crepe, fulfilling for two. They don’t have your usual matcha latte, it’s more of a float that they have here. The ice cream are nice too, although the hojicha is a bit too bitter.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Evie L
text:
A heaven place for matcha lovers and not sweet tooth. Its matcha soft serve is ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐, The combination of rich matcha ice cream, smooth white cream, and crunchy crumbles creates a perfect balance of flavor and texture. It’s a delight in every bite, with the different elements playing off each other in your mouth.

Their mochi is also worth mentioning—it doesn’t disappoint with its soft, chewy texture and subtle flavor.

The only downside? The place is quite narrow, and it tends to get crowded, so you’ll want to enjoy your treat quickly as others are likely waiting for their turn.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Vania Tjandradjaja
text: Good matcha, quite sweet, pretty busy, would love it better if it taste a bit more umami. But overall good enough 😊

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Prita Amelia
text:
Located in West Mall on the LG Floor. Has a limited space because this outlet is narrow.
It's highly recommended for matcha lovers. There are many various matcha menu in this place.
If you don't like matcha, you can try to order sakura menu instead of matcha.
In thic pict, I ordered Anmitsu Uji Matcha and Shiratama Sakurato Bowl

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Benny (Bandiger)
text:
Uji Match located in lowest floor of Grand Indonesia Mall.
As their name match is the main ingredient here for ice cream and drinks.
But they also have Sakura flavor here.
The ice cream is not sweet but have a little bitter green tea.
Most of the menu is below 60K.
The place is small with Japanese decoration.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Marcellino Adryan
text:
Uji Matcha, a haven for matcha enthusiasts, offers a delightful assortment of matcha-based desserts, from ice cream to dango and various beverages. Not only does it showcase the classic matcha, but it also features intriguing variations like hojicha and sakura.

The sweet yet slightly bitter flavor of the ice cream, complemented by the 3-color dango that leans towards subtle sweetness, creates a harmonious blend. The dango, while individually slightly bland, perfectly balances the rich sweetness of the ice cream.

Beyond the delectable treats, the employees' exemplary service truly enhances the experience. Their warmth and attentiveness add to the visit's enjoyment.

What truly sets Uji Matcha apart is the interactive fortune minigame offered post-purchase. It's a unique touch that engages customers, allowing them to participate in a fun activity—shaking a tube to retrieve a numbered stick, determining a corresponding fortune from a safe box. This thoughtful addition truly makes the visit memorable, adding an extra layer of enjoyment to the already delightful treats. 🍵🍨🥢🎉

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Lara Wiratno
text:
Finally got the chance to visit and I must tell you that I'm very happy with my experience!!

Their service is excellent, since It wad my first time the waiter was kind to explain to me in detail what they serve and all their menus,

I ordered the most recommended one, but i forgot what it's called, i think the balance of sweetness and bitternes is perfect. It has 4 layers (vanilla pudding, cereal, cream, matcha ice cream) and with Motchi & red bean as its topping.

For its price, I think it's very worth it!!

Cons: The seat is very limited

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Annisa Putri
text: Best matcha house in town! I am a really matcha fan and i have been strolling all over jakarta and Uji matcha is the best matcha house with best matcha quality from Japan. My repeat order is the 2nd pict..

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Tere - (heytheresia)
text:
One of my favorites for matcha dessert. The place is quite small and not busy but the desserts and cakes are nice

I love their matcha soft serve, quite strong matcha flavor, not so creamy and milky (which I like) served with mochi and red bean, they also have hojicha soft serve, a little to sweet for my palate but the hojicha latte was really good and not to sweet

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Ardra Arlizar
text: For me Uji Matcha hands down is still the best matcha in Jkt - even on par with whatever matcha I tried in Jpn. Enak bgt, gelato and sodt serve-nya!!!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Vanya Adisti
text:
Shiratama Machalato Bowl was amazing!😭🫶 I really love their Matcha Gelato and Sakura Gelato. The combination between the gelato, red beans, jellies, and those little dangos is so delicious and good for the soul.

I also had to try their Ichigo Daifuku and I loved it as well!!

The service was excellent as well, the staffs were very welcoming.

Coming back here isn’t a want, but a need🥹

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Hacker
text:
This place is really good! The service are very nice. The cashier and waitress were kind and friendly. The only bad thing is the waitlist and cramped space.

As for the food, the matcha are quite similar to the ones in Japan so you can say it's pretty authentic.

The crepe and roll cake was soft and fluffy. I can still taste the hint of bitterness from the matcha. Everything was full of cream but it felt light on the stomach and not too sweet so you don't feel sick or heavy eating it

Everything is delicious but if me and my friends had to choose one, we'll choose the roll cake.

For the drink, I ordered Pure Uji Matcha Drink that doesn't have any sugar at all. It taste bitter and I like it!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Wiewin Masli
text: Highly recommend this spot for the best matcha in town and excellent service. Seating is limited, so weekdays are ideal for dine-in. Savor their signature Float Matcha Latte with Matcha Gelato—and *P.S.* don't forget to try the unique omikuji (Japanese fortune-telling stick) experience! 🍵🍦🌿

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Joycerlyn Chancellor
text: a very great place for anyone who loves everything matcha. Most people come here for the ice cream/gelato, but i think the mille crepe is also great. The matcha is very prominent and the crepe is not too sweet or overpowered by the cream. Tons of elderlies come here for desserts. It’d be great to have a bigger space for this place, but overall it’s very nice.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: xyz 48
text: Their new harumi matcha milessss above other, it's like on their own league. Definitely recommend y'all to try it, 11/10

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Kiki Nada
text: Yummy matcha servings and very friendly and lovely waitress! 🩵 Say hi to the team from April 4!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Jessica Sienny
text:
Best matcha desserts in town! All matcha based dessert have a strong matcha flavour, not too milky or sweet. The place itself kinda small and narrow, but has a nice Japanese vibes interior.

The dessert presentation was so pretty too, highly recommend for matcha lovers 🍃

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Puteri Rengganis
text: a very cozy place to read peacefully with calming instrumental music. ordered their specialty Ice Cream Matcha Latte (normal sugar) and it tasted great! the matcha itself was bittersweet with perfect amount of bitterness 👍🏻 service was exceptional, definitely will be back again. thank you!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Nabilah Rahmagitha
text: Uji Matcha, with the tagline "The First Matcha Cafe in Indonesia," offers the Genmaicha Story. The matcha soft serve is really good and comes in a generous portion. The mochi is soft and delicious.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Jane Wen
text: Soft Serve Matcha with toppings and crushed waffles is one of the best <3 The server was friendly, making it a pleasant experience to dine there.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Tanukichi
text:
I frequent this Japanese dessert place several times a month. The only thing they could improve would be the selection of their menu, getting bored of ordering the same thing over and over again. They have a banner displaying summer menus but none were available so I have to give them 3 star on food due to this limited selection issue. The only cake they have is crepes cake...like cmon we all know crepe cake is far from a cake. It is layers of crepes filled with cream. Service was always good. Space is very limited I had to wait 30 minutes standing for a table during my last visit.

Food 3/5
Service 5/5
Ambiance 4/5

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Ivana D K
text: Since they are opening in GI, they are always full. The place was pretty cute but small and crowded, so we can only choose to take away. They have new series which is sakura series, you should try their hanami dango sakura soft serve and their sakura chizu.

title: Matchaman
name: Yi Ling
text:
The place was packed with locals even on a weekday noon. I tried the 🍵 Signature Matchaman Latte (Iced), which uses a unique house milk/oat milk blend and a hint of vanilla.

It’s creamy and well-prepared, though you might want to ask for less sugar. While it might not be the place for "matcha purists" who want to taste the tea on its own, it’s a fantastic spot for innovative and delicious matcha beverages. Leaning towards the creamier side. ​The drink features cold-whisked ceremonial grade matcha with their signature house milk and oat milk blend, sweetened with a hint of vanilla.I'd definitely want to come back to try their other innovative beverages.

Personally, I don’t think this is a "high-end premium matcha purist" spot if you enjoy tasting matcha strictly on its own, but they make a very innovative and good matcha-based beverages!

title: Matchaman
name: Stefan Rafales
text:
A sleek and cozy matcha spot in the heart of South Jakarta, Matchaman seeks to satisfy the increasing trend and growing matcha popularity. For those who enjoy matcha, the extensive drinks menu + sweet accompaniments like dorayaki has something for everyone to try.

Came on a weekday morning and tried their Strawberry Matcha Latte and it was tasty and refreshing - not too sweet, and properly balanced. I’m no expert in matcha but I enjoyed the drink. The queue wasn’t too long but you can use the time in queue to see the matcha drinks being made on the counter.

Overall ambience and vibe was a balance of cozy and lively - good for a quick drink with friends, although very limited seating available. The staff was professional, and provided a proper matcha experience, although the wait for the drinks can be long depending on how busy the place gets. Will be sure to pay a visit again when I am in the area.

title: Matchaman
name: Yora
text:
Finally got to try Matchman!

I intentionally came during Ramadhan, right before iftar time, to avoid the queue and the usual crowd and it was the perfect timing.

I ordered their signature Ice Matchaman Latte, cold whisked ceremonial grade matcha with their signature milk, sweetened with a hint of vanilla (I chose normal sugar and ice). The nutty flavor is quite dominant. It tastes smooth, well-balanced, and not overly sweet.

Since it wasn’t crowded, we could comfortably dine in and enjoy the atmosphere. A great spot for those who prefer a nutty and earthy matcha profile!

title: Matchaman
name: Ghivan Christine
text:
I tried their signature matcha and it was really good. Not too sweet, just pure matcha with a hint of milk and cream.

The service was also great. When I reached there, a line was formed, but it turned out pretty fast.

The ambience and vibes are totally on point. However, since it went viral, all the seats are occupied.

💰 Signature Matcha Latte +/- Rp 63.000

title: Matchaman
name: Piter Halim
text:
One of the great matcha cafés in Jakarta!
I’ve been to quite a few, but this one definitely stands out as one of the best.

We tried their signature Matchman Latte, and it tasted just right — perfectly balanced, not overly sweet like so many others out there. The Quattro Cheese Dorayaki was another winner, rich and flavorful with four kinds of cheese blending beautifully together.

It’s not cheap, that’s for sure — but you’re getting real, high-quality matcha here. How could I even complain? 😜

Great taste, cozy vibe, and a proper matcha experience — totally worth a visit for any matcha lover.

title: Matchaman
name: Rahadiany Rachmawaty
text:
Kind staffs, very delicious drink.
The place is little, but not crowded with visitors.  We still can hang out comfortably.

For matcha i prefer with ice.
Favorite, matchaman latte and strawberry matcha latte, so fresh and balance.

I tried also their dirty latte.

title: Matchaman
name: Dev Anggraini
text:
I am not a big fan of matcha; more a coffee drinker. Tried their hot pure matcha (no sugar) surprisingly loved my order. It was warm and tasted like ‘pure-real matcha’.

The cafe was packed on weekend so it was uncomfortable to spend your time too long especially if you are an introvert like me.

Good service. The queue was moving fast and order served quickly.

title: Matchaman
name: Lyn Nolan
text: I can say this is one of the best Matcha I’ve tried in Jakarta. Very grassy, earthy and nutty. Pistachio matcha a bit sweet as they don’t have no sugar option, but still tasted good.

title: Matchaman
name: Salony Widjaja
text: The matcha beverages hs no grassy taste nor smell. Matcha white chocolate is quite fragrant and not too sweet. The peach matcha is recommended too if you like peach. Dirty matcha is bitter. Maybe suitable to those who enjoy strong bitter taste frm matcha & coffee.  Though queue is long but fast service.

title: Matchaman
name: Maharani
text:
A cozy minimalistic matcha shop in Blok M. The atmosphere is calm although it can get crowded at times. But everything was orderly, starting from the ordering till we get our food & drinks.
Went on a friday afternoon, so the queue was decent. Only a few people in front of us and can get a table easily.
As for the drink, I specifically went there to get this drink which is the matcha latte with chocolate mousse. The drink has no additional sugar which is a good thing. The matcha flavour was quite intense, and the milk wasn’t overpowering. The chocolate mousse just gives an extra layer of flavour & creaminess to it. A really good combo of flavours.
I also got the valhrona chocolate dorayaki (which i forgot to take photo). The dorayaki was nice & soft and the chocolate filling was not too sweet. It also goes well with the drink that I got.

title: Matchaman
name: OMR AL-ABBASI
text: My experience at matchaman was excellent in every detail. The atmosphere is elegant and relaxing, and the decor reflects great taste that gives the place a classy touch. The service was outstanding from arrival to departure, and the staff were polite and professional. Cleanliness is top-notch, both in the seating area and restrooms, and the prices are reasonable for the quality offered. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a calm and high-quality experience. I’ll definitely visit again.

title: Matchaman
name: Hera Hapsari
text:
I come here because out of all the places that sell matcha, Matchaman is definitely one of the best in South Jakarta.
We always ordered matchaman latte iced less sugar.
The shop is nice, the AC is cold, but sometimes people walk in and sit down right away without ordering… so if you come alone, you can end up with no seat left.

Hopefully visitors can be more mindful — even though there’s no waitress at the front, they already set the flow so you’re supposed to place your order first.

title: Matchaman
name: Rahadianto Putra
text: The hyped Matcha trend in Jakarta! Even though the price is quite high, but everyone is still super enthusiastic of this trend. I ordered the signature one. The taste of the Matcha is very bold and thick, and if you order the less-sugar one, you won't be disappointed.

title: Matchaman
name: mardhiana koesumawati
text: Trying out the latest viral hangout spot in Blok M 😃 Matchaman. They offer a variety of matcha-based drinks. I can’t drink milk or dairy products, so I ordered the Matcha Peach Soda. The refreshing soda combined with peach slices and the rich flavor of matcha was perfect for quenching my thirst after walking around Blok M 😃

title: Matchaman
name: niclo
text: one of the best matchas ive tried in jakarta, very strong matcha taste and very smooth. i recommend you to order the matchaman latte, the normal matcha latte is very weak in taste compared to the matchaman latte. even though theres a 10k difference in price, i think its worth it.

title: Matchaman
name: Benedicta Nathania Gadiza Ferdianita
text: Matchaman has always been my go-to matcha place since it opened, it is a shame that they currently only have 2 branches in Blok M and Pacific Place but I did hear that they’re going to open a branch in PIM 5 so super excited for that! I hope Matchaman continues to be consistent with their products and services because it is one of the only matcha places that I can come back to for a decent cup of matcha. (I always order the Oat Matcha Latte with no sugar!)Other than the oat matcha latte, I would also recommend their signature and the coconut matcha!

title: Matchaman
name: Evelyn L
text:
Jumping on the matcha craze in Jakarta! 🍵This place is said to be one of the most talked-about matcha spots in town. Besides the usual usucha and matcha latte, they offer a variety of matcha-based drinks like dirty matcha, coconut matcha, and many more plus dorayaki to pair with your drinks.

We started with a few recommendations from our friends: the Signature Matchaman, Strawberry Matcha Latte, and Honey Seasalt Cream Matcha Latte. The matcha had a rich umami flavor that stood out, though I personally would’ve liked it a bit creamier. The Strawberry Matcha Latte was a pleasant surprise. The sweetness of the strawberries really complemented nicely and uniquely with the matcha.

Just a heads-up: we went on a weekend afternoon and had to queue for about 20 minutes just to place our order. After that, it took another 30–40 minutes to get our drinks. Seating is limited, so expect to wait and stand a bit. This place seems to be consistently busy!

title: Matchaman
name: Sabrina Nathaniel
text: As a huge matcha fan, I love their matcha in this place! I tried three menu items: matchaman latte, matcha latte, and matcha jasmine latte. The matchaman latte was my fave (& their signature), but the other two were good too. I'd love to come back here 🌱🍵

title: Matchaman
name: Hidianto Lusman
text: Nice place with good vibe and aircon.. perfect for hot day… Ice matchaman latte is very nice, not too sweet even though i ordered normal sugar level, matcha taste not too strong. Creme brulee dorayaki is nice. Perfect match with the latte

title: Matchaman
name: I C
text:
Place quite packed. Waited 30mins++.
Orderd their most famous menu and the standard matcha latte.
Taste ok, in indonesian term "LUMAYAN".
But i think its quite expensive, feels like another matcha rip-off.
I dont think i will be coming back.
Once is enough for me.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Karel Devino
text:
The place is so tiny, you can find it in front of Bread Talk. On weekend, there would be a waiting list so prepare yourself.

Matcha Bowl
I like the sakura dan green tea mixed ice cream. They complement each other. The green tea jelly is also good. The red bean is soooo good! however, the mochi tastes plain and no filling. I give 4 out of 5 for the bowl

Refill Hot Green Tea
OMG, I think its my first time drinking a tea that taste “gurih”. I really like it a lot besides the fact that we can refill the tea twice. I really recommend this tea after you taste other sweet menus. I give 5 out of 5 for this.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: miasperanza
text: Great taste! And not too sweet! Dessert cafe, they serve beverages & dessert all using matcha & sakura as based. A bit expensive though, but I think it's worth the price (more or less IDR 50K/beverage or dessert).

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: The Duchess of Culinary
text:
Uji Matcha is specialized in variant of drinks and desserts that are made with matcha. Matcha fan is definitely cannot miss this one.

This place with Japanese style decorations isn't that big. There are only three small tables and bar where we could dine in. During the pandemic, there is distance sign on each table and chair.

I tried Pure Uji Matcha Drink (IDR 39,000) where I requested it with less ice and sugar. This is the pure matcha drink made by uji matcha powder from Kyoto. It tastes less sweet and not bitter at all. A refreshing cold drink with matcha taste that isn't that strong.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Kharisma Tamimi
text: Good place for people who like matcha. The matcha roll cake is very soft and not too sweet, yet has complete package of taste such as creamy from the cream, bitterness from matcha powder, sweetness from redbean paste, acidity from strawberry sorbet and chopped fresh strawberry fruits. And you'll also get the good combination of texture from soft roll cake with crunchy chopped strawberry fruits. You should eat the cake together with all of the condiments to enjoy the whole excitement.  My favorite menu is matcha soft ice cream that has simple balance taste of matcha and milk

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Jasmine Feivel
text: A great taste of matcha (a bit bitter and soft) with crumbs and whip cream is just perfect for dessert! However the vanilla ice cream is too sweet for me.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Rania Ruth
text: Tried this matcha dessert and tasted so good! Red bean balances the bitterness of the matcha, the soft chewy mochi and some crunchy waffle. A perfect combo of flavor and texture.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Nisrina Nurfitri
text: I must say Uji Matcha is the best matcha dessert café in town! As a self-declared matcha lover, I'm really picky when it comes to matcha-related stuff. I don't like it when the matcha tastes too sweet or too artificial, but you will not get any of that here. I bought Azuki Matcha Latte, Shiratama Matchalato Bowl with both matcha and sakura gelato, and Uji Matcha Soft Serve Story. I admit the sakura gelato is a bit too sweet for me, but both the matcha gelato and soft serve are perfect. The color of the matcha is vibrant and the matcha itself tastes so rich yet not too sweet. Some people might find it a bit pricy, but does it worth the price? It sure does! The service is also superb! Definitely worth a visit!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: wan r
text: It is so good. Hopefully, they can add a plain matcha latte option without the soft serve, and also hot drinks to the menu.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Milo
text: tried their signature harumi cold whisk, it was soooo goood, the nuttiness 😋🤌🏻, long line, short wait — and the friendliest, most helpful cashier ever 🤏🏻🤏🏻

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Neta
text: The matcha and sakura soft ice cream tasted really good, you’re going to love this! The prices where affordable as well.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Wendy Hardynata
text: Their desserts never dissapointing. The desserts have unique taste. Have come here multiple times and for sure will come back anytime soon. The only minus point for this place is just too small.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Dyni Hayami
text:
If you miss something about matcha and other Japanese style dessert, Uji Matcha is the best place to have those kind of desserts in Jakarta.

You can enjoy matcha ice cream, sweets dango and kind of anmitsu in a good price. Hot drink is also available here including Hojicha.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Jane
text: My most favorite place for matcha-based desserts, and MRT made it easier to drop by after office hours. Love matcha jelly so much

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: K. W.
text: Top tier customer service - that explains the patient groups waiting for their turn. Served good matcha before it was cool, at extremely reasonable prices.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Eunike Elisaveta
text: Small place in Grand Indonesia, that you can enjoy dessert like in Japan. They served any kind of matcha and sakura flavor.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Hillary Victoria
text: Not the best matcha but still ok.. sugar added to red bean is too much (can even taste the granules).. portion is too small for the price point

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Art
text: Nice ambiance with a soft Japanese tune playing in the background. Very busy on Sunday evening with a long wait to sit down but you can order to go easily. Ideally, they offer free water for those waiting to get in.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Zlata GERGELOVA
text: One of the best matcha ice lattes I have ever had in my life. I am matcha lover and this place is simply delish. The seating space is quite narrow.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Esha Putra
text:
One of the best place to enjoy matcha dessert. Their desserts are one of the best in the town. If you fan of a real matcha, you should try their desserts.

Located in LG of West Mall.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Katie Sanderson
text: We ordered the Crepe roll  and it was delicious. In fact, I may be a bit addicted to these crepe cakes/ rolls haven't had a bad one yet!

title: Matchaman
name: Nabila Batris
text: matcha nya the bestt one in jakarta. I recommend getting the matchaman latte its so good definutely worth a try!

title: Matchaman
name: sandra anda
text:
Matchaman strikes an impressive balance between traditional matcha techniques and creative flavor innovation. Every drink and snack I tried was carefully executed and delicious, from the bold dirty matcha to the indulgent truffle dorayaki.
Considering the premium ingredients and craftsmanship, the prices are more than fair.
If you’re in the mood to treat yourself or simply obsessed with matcha, Matchaman is absolutely worth the visit.

title: Matchaman
name: Xiao Lim
text:
First of all, I tried the signature matcha latte and it was really good. Not too sweet, just pure matcha with a hint of creaminess from the milk.

Secondly, service was great, there was a long queue and it turned out quite fast.

Lastly, ambience and vibes are on point. A great meeting point to hang out/gather. However because it was viral, all the available seats are occupied with the crowds.

title: Matchaman
name: Rian Farhan
text:
With ceremonial grade matcha straight from the Mountain of Uji, Japan, Matchaman guarantees good quality ingredients. I tried the OG Matcha Latte, it’s smooth, grassy and slightly bitter, not the best I’ve had, but still alright.  I might come back to try other menu options.
The service was fast, clean, and friendly, making the experience smooth. Seating is available both indoors and in the front outdoor area, so there are options depending on your preference.

I think Matchaman has a great concept, delivering premium matcha, but there’s room to enhance the taste and explore more menu. Thank you!

title: Matchaman
name: Joni F.
text: Worth trying their Matcha selections ranging from their Signature Latte as well other variations. Your drink is freshly brewed and made to get the best out of the matcha.

title: Matchaman
name: Adi Gunawan Rachman
text:
Finally got the chance to visit Matchaman! Last time I came, the place was way too packed and the queue was insane. Luckily, this time there was no long line and I even got a seat inside.

I ordered their signature Matchaman Latte and a Double Choco Dorayaki. The matcha latte really impressed me. At Rp 63,000 per cup, it tasted like it was made with premium matcha. I asked for less sugar, and it turned out perfect because I could still enjoy that slightly bitter, earthy matcha note.

The dorayaki was honestly the highlight. The chocolate, both in the batter and the filling, was rich and not overly sweet, with a nice hint of bitterness. The dorayaki itself was super soft and fluffy. So satisfying!

Definitely planning to come back again.

title: Matchaman
name: Ummu Pertiwi
text:
Came here at the beginning of 2025, kind of forgot what we ordered, but i think it was their signature matchaman latte.
A bit of a funny story, when we asked the staff, what flavor components of choice they had (nutty, umami, vegetal, etc), she was confused and she answered they only used 1 kind of ceremonial matcha grade. Then, we just ordered the latte with an open mind. to our great surprise, the latte had really good balance between the nuttiness, umaminess, vegetal, and bitterness, with sweetness from the oatmilk. Maybe will come back when they hypes dies down

title: Matchaman
name: Wulan
text: One of the best matcha I've ever taste. I ordered Honey Sea Salt Cream Matcha, perfect balance between creamy&sweet+the bitter of matcha. Worth the price and hype.. Will come again for sure

title: Matchaman
name: gayatri mia
text: Suka banget! The sweetness is just right, and it’s not one of those super milky matcha lattes, this one really tastes like matcha 😍😍 It's totally worth it, even though the strawberry matcha latte costs 72k, the dorayaki is super soft and fluffy, and the filling is generous without being overwhelming at all

title: Matchaman
name: Dessy Febrina
text:
One of the nicest I've tried. Creamy and does not taste like grass; my husband, who generally dislikes matcha, may tolerate it on the matchaman latte.

Furthermore, the dorayaki brulee is superb, please give it try!

The main drawback is the huge queue and limited seating. The order arrives quickly; perhaps the staff has grown accustomed to it.

title: Matchaman
name: mei.
text:
goes to one of my wishlists, matchaman in long weekend seems a bad decision but that's why i was in jakarta 👀✨

to experience one of my fav. matcha latte (63k) at first i was like, "hmm.." but it turns out really great too! 😍🫶🏻 (it was worth the wait though, even you must have to be patient and waiting for almost an hour) 👏🏻😭

great service and great lighting (it was all green to matches with matcha) 🍵💚

title: Matchaman
name: Crystaline Claudia
text:
Insane queue especially during weekend. Had to wait for at least 1 hour for our drinks. Obviously no space to seat when it is super crowded. Drinks are okay. Loved the pistachio matcha latte. Signature is okay. But all the staffs are very quick witted and helpful even it is crowded.
Maybe will be back when the matcha hype has calmed down a bit 😅

title: Matchaman
name: Gea Wisesa
text:
The most crowded matcha store in Blok M‼️

Located near Haka Dimsum & MRT station, you can easily find these shop. Matchaman has greeny vibes like a matcha.

I have to wait +- 30 mins until my order finish. I ordered their Signature Matcha Latte. Its a cold whisk ceremonial grade matcha with fresh milk. Their matcha taste nutty and not too creamy. They still has bitter of matcha but not too much because I actually don't like bitter taste✨

Unfortunately, they only have 1 type of matcha. But they have variety matcha combination on their menu. One menu that I want to try is Matcha Jasmine Latte.

Overall its worth the hype, you have to try it🍵

*EDITED*
I've tried Matcha Jasmine Latte and I love it. The matcha is not that intense but still there. The spin-off menu of matcha at Matchaman is worth to try.

title: Matchaman
name: Dawam Prabowo
text:
This place is one of the newest matcha cafes in town, located in the Blok M area. I went here on a Wednesday afternoon, and it was quite packed despite it being in the middle of the week, probably because it has been viral on social media lately. Nonetheless, I only needed to wait for around 10 minutes to get my drink, as the staff were quite efficient in making the orders.

I tried their Matchaman Latte (63k) and I would say that the matcha they serve is pretty authentic with a slight bitterness and umami taste. It was, however, not among the best matcha latte when compared to the ones I had before because it's missing some boldness to its flavor.

The place itself is pretty small, with a U-shaped counter area dominating most of the space. There are a few seatings at the back, but each is positioned really close to one another and is always full considering how crowded the establishment is. So, not a really good place if you want to have a relaxing chat with friends.

title: Matchaman
name: Agista Jung
text:
Came here while waiting for someone

I didn’t know since when this cafe is here but it has been almost 4-5 months I didn’t go to Blok M. I gave it a try and wanted to compare this place to another matcha place close to my work place. Well, I had their signature matcha which cost me around 65K. I could say that their claim for using ceremonial grade matcha wasn’t just a buff, they really do. Its matcha powder tastes quite strong but still creamy. If you asked whether I love it or not? I think so.

They also had an open view space so you can observe the barista serves their matcha. Compared to another matcha place tho, I think I’d still prefer the other matcha place because they also had various range of snacks to accompany matcha-time.

Is it worth it to queue for long and get its matcha for a high price? I don’t think so. The queue itself goes quite speedy since the cashier works fast. But for that price, ummm not yet. They only accept cashless payment and they also sells dorayaki.

title: Matchaman
name: Jamal Fathoni
text: Went here on weekday before lunch and there's no queue at all. The ambience is super cozy. Their signature matchaman latte is a must-try, really creamy, but you can still taste their matcha & just a hint of sweetness which is perfect!

title: Matchaman
name: Hans Jonathan
text:
Really good and proper speciality matcha drinks, using proper ceremonial grade matcha.
Ordered these items:
- Iced Matchaman Latte
- Iced Strawberry Matcha Latte
- Creme Brulee Dorayaki
There is nothing amiss!
The green tea used had proper bitter and umami properties and the milk smooths it out.
They stand out in the crowded Blok M food scene with proper hyper focused matcha excellence and beautiful striking interior.

title: Matchaman
name: Nabila Fathia
text: too expensive. plus they also reject taking my order of matcha jasmine latte with no sugar even though the ingredient was optional simply because they don’t recommend it without sugar. i think it’s a bit rude to underestimate a customer’s sensitivity for sugar. man, i’m truly okay with having my matcha bitter. just because you don’t like it, doesn’t mean i can’t like it.

title: Matchaman
name: priscilla claudia
text: One of good quality matcha with affordable price in town! Prefer the matcha with water only but the latte was decent too

title: Matchaman
name: Zerbin Siew
text:
Queued here for 40 mins to get this hyped matcha.

The matcha taste was very strong, it depends on personal liking. For me, it's special as you don't usually get such matcha with strong taste. But whether if it is worth the wait, I would say no. Maybe will come back again when the hype fades.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Yi Ling
text:
A local recommended this matcha spot, warning me that the lunch queues are legendary. Luckily, we arrived a few hours before closing and barely had to wait.

Here is what I tried:
🍵 ​KOEN
I paired this with fresh milk and found the flavor profile bright with a nice savory umami undertone. While I requested a little sugar, I think I’ll dial it down next time to let the matcha shine even more. That said, the sweetness did help balance the tea so it wasn't too astringent. It’s a very steady spot for matcha enthusiasts who have a specific flavor preference; for me, this one leaned towards the nutty side.

🍡 ​Warabi Mochi
This was lovely! It has a slightly firmer texture while remaining soft and elastic. It’s a classic jelly-like Japanese confection that melts in your mouth. The kinako powder adds a nice nuttiness and sweetness, and the accompanying matcha ganache was rich with a bold, "grassy" profile. ​The space itself is also beautiful and bright!!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Swastika Nohara
text:
One cup of matcha costs around Rp77.000 up to more than Rp150.000. With this price range, it’s hard to accept that they didn’t make my order the way I placed it.

The matcha is rather bland, lack of that umami taste that usually present in a proper serving of matcha. The place is nice, it gives a clean ambience.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Rizki Larasati
text:
The toilet and wifi is out of service when I visited there 😢

the matcha wassss gooooddd, pricey but worth it

We order akemi normal sugar : ok, creamier taste
Stawberry matcha : for me is too sweet
Sora less sugar : slightly bitter but I like it!

We also order the dessert jade velvet and cheesecake just ok

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ivy Carolyne
text:
I recently tried Uki Matcha, and while the experience was interesting, I found it a bit on the pricey side. The ceremonial grade matcha starts at around 60k, which feels quite expensive compared to other places. For a one-time treat or to experience something different, it’s still okay, but not somewhere I’d visit regularly.

As for the desserts, they didn’t really meet my expectations. Based on what many people say, I was hoping for something more special, but the taste was just average and not as impressive. Personally, I think there are still plenty of matcha spots out there that serve better flavors at more affordable prices.

Another thing to note is that because of the long waiting list, you’re only allowed to stay for about 1–2 hours. It makes sense given the crowd, but it does make the visit feel a little rushed.

On the positive side, the café has a nice and minimalist ambiance. The staff were polite, and the presentation of the drinks was neat and well-prepared. Overall, Uki Matcha is worth a try if you’re curious, but for me it’s not a must-return spot.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Tatiana Imarasha
text:
One of the best matcha in town. Quite pricey for a cup of matcha, especially since I requested less ice, so you can see how little I got, but it’s really good.  👍🏻

The place isn’t very big, so it gets crowded and there’s usually a queue on weekends.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Siti Alifiah Kusumawardani (Fhia)
text: I must say they serve one of the best matchas in town. What I love most is how well they describe the flavor of each matcha base, so if you’re not quite sure which one you prefer, you can simply read the description and pick your brew. Last time I picked the Seiu and it was exactly what I wanted. Next time I’ll probably try the Sana or Nagomi since they were out of stock last time 🍃🍃

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: livjunkie
text: This is the first time i had to wait in line for a drink in Indonesia ever. I picked Nagomi Matcha Latte with oatmilk, it was honestly amazing. Rich with umami, creamy texture and no bitterness at all. High price perhaps due to its location & branding because i have tried matcha lattes similar to Nagomi with lower price. Decided to bring Tenka Matcha Powder home (its the only one thats left). Overall as a matcha lover, they deliver as promised. The cafe looks worn out and the floor was dirty but appreciate the staffs that are capable to give out their best still!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: RS
text:
Uki Matcha is absolutely exceptional. Their pastries are beautifully made — light, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. And the matcha? Just perfect. Smooth, rich, and not too sweet, exactly how real matcha should taste.
Everything feels thoughtfully crafted, and it’s the kind of place you’ll want to return to again and again. One of the best matcha and pastry experiences

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: niclo
text: kalo nanya enak si enak ya….but its so expensive and honestly i think there are better places where you can get matcha of the same quality. tapi regardless, i think its definitely worth trying at least once bc its rare that you can pick from many different ceremonial grade matchas with different taste profiles. tapi in terms of harga its MAHAL BANGET, 1 gelas bisa 80rb lebih which is um… dan jujur menurut gw kalo lu bukan matcha connoisseur ato matcha fan parah, you cant taste the difference anyway. wld come here maybe once or twice a year to try the different matchas maybe

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: C A.H
text:
The only downside is the lack of space compared to the amount of crowd. Come here early. I arrived a little after they opened and it was already crowded. I went with myself so I was able to grab a seat, but shared with another party at one table. I had to wait a little over 20 minutes to get everything, though - and mind you... this is not even 1 hour yet after they opened.

The matcha was amazing - I got their seasonal ceremonial grade. Pricey but worth it. Their desserts are also delicious. This is one of the top matcha places for me (no wonder why it's always crowded).

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: PangPang Arabica
text: matcha with a variety of authentic flavor choices. I really like the Montblanc Usucha. matcha with a fresh orange taste, blend the taste.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ummu Pertiwi
text:
Came back here after a year and it's still as crowded. It took us around half an hour to get a table.
Last time i ordered kaze usucha. This time, we ordered kaze latte fresh milk with their cheesecake. A little note that i have particular taste when it comes to matcha. I love ones with dominating nutty flavor components. And this quite satisfied my palate although, honestly, ive had better ones with better price points. Their cheese cake is quite good but the matcha sauce was too sweet.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ming Dao
text:
I visited Uki Matcha and had to wait almost two hours just to get a seat, with a 60–90 minute dining limit once inside.

As for the drinks, nothing really stood out, they were fine but not particularly memorable. I’d highly recommend the waffles; they were the highlight of the visit. On the other hand, the matcha mochi didn’t work for me, the flavors didn’t quite come together.

Overall, it’s worth a try if you have the patience

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Laras Auspisia
text: One of the best matcha cafes in Jakarta! The flavors are quite varied, and the mochi waffle was decent lafff

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: aprillia dwi
text: I tried their signature beverage, the tenka one. I had it with normal sugar, but it wasnt that sweet which is good. They served well and fast. I love the white themed interior and the vibes, fit perfectly

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: adya antyahita
text:
I’m not really into matcha, but this is my second time coming here to keep someone company who is a matcha fan.
The first time, I ordered the “Koga Hojicha.” It tasted kinda like 90% dark chocolate—definitely not sweet, really rich and deep, even the color was chocolatey brown.
This time, I tried the Orange Hojicha. Perfect for someone like me who’s not a matcha person. It still had a bit of that matcha bitterness, but the orange made it feel fresh and light.
The Original Basque Cheesecake? So good. I’d pick this over the mochi I had on my first visit, no doubt.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Sonya
text:
Love when my food is pretty!! 💚
Ordered the akemi latte with no sugar — would recommend if you’re into nutty, less bitter matcha. The jade velvet had a subtle sourness from the fruits and wasn’t too sweet, though I do wish the matcha flavor was stronger. Also tried the pain suisse au matcha and it was too sweet for my liking, but the pastry itself was quite nice.

We had to queue for a while, and it was very crowded and noisy inside. Most of the tables were quite small, so comfort wasn’t really a strong point. Also, everything’s pretty expensive, unfortunately.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ela Yudhanira
text: The place is so crowded and we need to wait to get seated. There are different variants of matcha, from the cheapest one to the most expensive ceremonial matcha. They also have several matcha desserts. Overall, matcha latte is good. A bit bitter and savory matcha combined with milk, in a good composition. It’s all balanced well. Matcha blended well into the water as well. There’s no powdery taste. Cheesecake is good. The matcha liquid topping is a bit too bitter for me. The rice cake is better. The sweetness and the bitterness are balanced in a good way. The price is quite expensive for the desserts. It is located in the middle of SCBD Park.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Nadhira Haifa
text:
This matcha shop was viral months ago and now that the hype is a bit down, so let’s give it a try.
Ordered a ceremonial matcha Akemi with additional fresh milk no sugar, it has a strong taste of roasted nutty with a slightest taste of bitterness. I love how it taste, but it’s kinda overpriced for almost cost me 100k.
I came on the weekend for takeaway and it took me more than one hour of waiting in a queue to get inside the shop, order the matcha and finally get the matcha.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: T S
text: *PLEASE BE NICE with food driver! The desserts was great as well the matcha latte (i tried Kaze), the service towards customers was fine but to the food delivery driver..? I was sitting enjoying my meals and beside me there's an elder grab driver asking for details to the barista make sure his customers order (he said it was his first time taking matcha order) but i heard everything in high tone and some attitude coming from one of the barista.  Please improve your service equally towards everyone.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Devi Santika
text: excellence service from Mba Fitri, and really good matcha

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Adam Godwin
text: Authentic japanese dinein experience. Come quickly as they are now serving their sakura menu. Their sakura soft serve ice cream is great.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Puja Kyryliuk
text: A favorite place for matcha Lovers hehe the service is very friendly and the place is cute, it's just minimalist... so far okay

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Sean Axel
text: I really like the ambient, so secluded. The matcha Soft Served is very nice! Recomended for matcha seekers!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Erni H
text: Delicious various of matcha, stunning decor places but not large spaces, the staff is helpful! Thank you

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: tamago telor
text: Space is quite limited but have a good variety of matcha selections and great explanations for the choices 🤭

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Naomi Pertiwi
text: Nice little place to enjoy various matcha desserts. I hope this business will grow prosper and big. We loved it.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Aussie Mohammad
text: Best matcha in GI, love the place, but is so crowded on weekends

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: NADSENSE II
text: Superb services. All the staffs are so friendly, they patiently explained all menu tonthe customers. Food are great, it taste so expensive and pleasing at the same time. It would be lovely if the could expand the place to have more dine-in customers!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Vallery Sumardy
text: For matcha lovers, this is the perfect spot with excellent desserts and service, though seating is a bit tight.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: dinda rahmah
text:
I opted for the soft-serve matcha, and it was a delightful experience. The flavor was spot-on, striking a perfect balance between the bitterness and sweetness that true matcha enthusiasts crave. The addition of fresh fruits like strawberry and lychee, along with a scoop of red bean, added a delightful touch of sweetness and texture. Hidden beneath the soft-serve, a layer of matcha jelly provided an unexpected and refreshing surprise.

Overall, Uji Matcha is a must-visit for anyone with a love for matcha. The quality of the matcha is evident, and the presentation is both visually appealing and thoughtfully executed. Though, the place is small so you might need to wait in the waiting list before :)

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Rina Agustin
text: First time try this matcha, and well i like it

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Elisabeth Simaibang
text: my fav matcha spot in town so far! i love the umaminess, nuttiness, and just the perfect balance of sugar (if you’re opt for a bit sweet matcha) for their normal serving. love it.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Meivy Soesilo
text:
i love japanese snacks, and drinks ❤️
i like the decoration so pretty 😄

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Zuraini Dzulkapli
text: Love it!

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Johnathan Lee Yin Yen
text: Desserts are really yummy, I do admire the interior design. The attention of details are quite pleasing.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Genevieve M
text: Lucuu, harumi matcha is really nice

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Renn
text: The most authentic Matcha in Jakarta and one of the best. I ordered Soft Serve Matcha Ice Cream and Ichigo Matcha. Both tasted great, and the best part was the Ogura (Red Bean). I really loved the mochi texture as well; it was very chewy. The taste was almost identical to what I ate back in Japan. Overall, I recommend this place for those who miss Matcha Ice Cream from Japan! And also the service is very friendly

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Farah Amelia
text: Loved their Shiratama Bowl Mix, ordered the matcha & hojicha gelato. Especially pleased by the hojicha, the smoky flavor is good for balancing matcha. Surprised by how lovely the waitress is! Also great product knowledge btw. The ambience is wonderful but the place is incredibly small, it’s hard to get in/out to. We’re lucky that when we arrived there’s an empty table. Would recommend when it’s not crowded! Might get annoyed if it’s too cramped.

title: Uji Matcha Grand Indonesia
name: Narisya Lucky
text: Service is really good here. The food is very delicious and the waiting staffs give excellent service. Recommended

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Olivia
text:
Tried out the viral matcha, not because i’m a matcha girl, but apparently, my friend is 👯‍♀️

I ordered the number two (forget the name) & my friend ordered akemi, and we also ordered the cheesecake. We had to wait >1 hour for our drinks. My drink came first, and i didn’t like it. It was bland, bitter, and not creamy, but please note that i don’t like matcha in general. Their matcha was so STRONG i even got GERD after i drank it 🥲. My friend’s Akemi tasted good. Sweet & fragrant, i love it! Great for one who doesn’t like matcha.

The cheesecake was goood. Especially the matcha sauce. We believe they mixed the sauce with white chocolate? Its soooo nice! Overall good for matcha lovers!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Matthew De Jano
text: UKI Matcha SCBD offers a truly new and different matcha drinking experience! The service is very friendly and helpful, making the visit even more enjoyable. It's a bit on the pricey side, but highly recommended for matcha lovers looking for a new sensation."

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Utari
text: Tried the Kaze with oat milk latte at Uki Matcha and it was a calm lil breeze in the middle of the crowd. Surprisingly, the queue moved fast (bless!), and the staff were super nice and informative, love that. The space gives a fresh vibe, and although the matcha wasn’t mind-blowingly umami, it still hit that comfy, mellow spot. It’s definitely a chill stop if you’re around and curious about their menu. Would I go back? Yeah, if I’m in the area. Overall, vibes were right, experience was smooth. 5/5 for the full package, not just the cup.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Puspa Almas
text: Tried their Ikigai latte iced no sugar, my 1st ceremonial grade! Surprised with the taste, just wow--also the price tho. The ambience is quite nice n clean. The place isnt that spacious. I came on Sat around 10.10 am and had to queue for 15 mins to order and another 40mins to get my matcha done. Visited as a tourist while in jkt so its not really a problem for me to wait for a cup. I asked for take away but then as im waiting there's a seat so just lucky for me eh?😅

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: The Duchess of Culinary
text:
Uki Matcha is a matcha specialist place where they serve various matcha drinks and desserts. We came here on a Friday afternoon, and it was so so crowded. After ordering and paying, the waiting time was about 20 minutes, so that we strolled along SCBD Park before going back to Uki Matcha.

They provide four types of ceremonial matcha powder with different origins, characteristics, tastes, and definitely the prices. For the matcha latte itself, the price starts from IDR 60,000.

I tried Iced Sora Matcha Latte with Oat Milk (IDR 78,000). This is the second cheapest matcha choice from Uki Matcha. And this is probably the most expensive matcha latte that I have purchased so far 😅
The character of Sora is mild creamy, mild umami with a hint of a sweet aftertaste. Well, maybe because it's mild, so I didn't find any special taste in this matcha, as I have another with a better and strong nutty character that suits my taste 🙏🏻😅

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Rafidha Najib
text: I have been around and always find myself lining back to UKI. Akemi and Ren are my two fav matcha types they have out of 5 ot 6 Ceremonial grade matcha they have. Whenever I'm in Jakarta, I would always make the time to visit.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Janice Lin
text:
Came on a Friday afternoon directly after lunch, and the place was very crowded. There was no line and we could place our order immediately, but we had to wait 20 minutes for our takeouts.

The place was minimalistic and interior was mostly white, a lot like a lab. We could watch the staff whisking our order in real time and it was surprisingly therapeutic. We ordered an Akemi matcha (usucha/straight tea style, unsweetened and without milk), a basque cheesecake with tenka ganache, and a genmaicha mochi danish.

The Akemi matcha tasted clean and strong with no bitterness or grassiness at all. The closer we got to finishing the cup, the stronger the floral aroma became.

We had the desserts about 20 minutes after the purchase. The genmaicha mochi danish actually did not taste like the genmaicha I know at all, and it tasted simply like ordinary matcha. However, the still warm and gooey center teams up with the crunchy pastry so well.

The basque cheesecake was also tasty, but unfortunately the ganache have thickened in texture so we could not pour it over the cake.

I think we should return for dine-in someday near the payday to have it properly.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: christi hayunindita
text: One of the "it" matcha places in jakarta. Their matcha and desserts selections are great. I particularly love their burnt cheesecake with extra matcha ganache. It is quite upscale and pricey but the place was always crowded especially during lunch on workdays. You would be lucky to get seats during this time. One thing that is not cool is their waiting time for drinks. On their crowded time i have experienced 1 hour queue for drinks because they have the same line of queue for every order; in store, online, and take away. Maybe they can work on a better system for this.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Mw Mi
text:
Basque cheesecake with matcha sauce bolehhh deh coba
The cheesecake melting in the mouth + sweetness matcha sauce complement the overall taste...

Matcha drink is good.. Hojicha with strawberry has an interesting refreshing taste

Utk overall dining experience, tempatnya nyaman but for the long time queue? We are not sure it's worth it.

Maybe they need to develop a better system to queue, because when we're there it's quite questionable for takeaway/ dine-in.. the takeaway  takes a long time to enter inside even more faster us to dine in and sit.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Vera Nathalia
text:
F&B:
- they serve matcha drinks and desserts.
- they also sell matcha kit and powder.
- been here twice and during which I had Kaze iced latte, Airin iced latte, basque cheesecake with matcha and genmacha danish
- all f&b are high quality

Service:
- a bit slow for matcha drinks, but totally worth the wait

Atmosphere:
- minimalistic design with white surrounding which I personally like cos it looks clean

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Shirleen Sheila
text: The matcha was so rich! Worth the price, I think. But the queue was so long—better to go on weekdays during office hours if you want to try it without queuing too long.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Kenny Oscar
text:
For matcha enthusiast, this is the place to go. Trying and finding out the place for each type of matcha is a must.

I would suggest to order the matcha without milk to taste the pure taste of each matcha.

The staff is knowledgable of the taste amd flavour profile of the matcha.

Really recommend this place for people who really cares matcha. If you're just trying out, I recommend the basic one is enough to fill your taste buds.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Marcello Suryadi
text:
Beautiful setting inside of SCBD park. The white interior is calming and invites you in to sit down and relax while sipping on a cup of your choice.

We ordered the Tenka (cheapest one) and Ikigai (the most expensive one) to conpare its taste. We also ordered their Mochi.

The Tenka was amazing, it’s different from the regular matcha you can get out there. The guide (yes they have a guide in the corner) state that it’s supposed to be bitter but combined with the milk it compliments it really well.

The Ikigai however is a-bit disappointing. I think it’s a bit watered down due to the ice? It’s supposed to be aromatic but I can’t seem to taste the difference, it’s almost odorless while tasting. I prefer Tenka far more to this item.

The Mochi was alright, it’s pretty good paired with the sweet Matcha sauce. When I am in the area, I’ll come back for the Tenka, really good Matcha.

By the way when parking in SCBD, if you don’t use an electric car prepare to fork 100K. Or you can park in Lot 17 and walk a bit.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Miranti Vidyasari
text: Spot the new matcha bar in SCBD park, it has a sleek and clean design inside. Order out Matcha Latte with a selection of Kaze Matcha types. Upon first sipping, it has nutty and roasted feels, blending with the milky flavor along the way. One of the choices to have great matcha in the area.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: anne putri
text:
UKI Matcha is hands down the best matcha spot in town. I’ve tried a handful of matcha places across Jakarta, but none have delivered the consistency, depth, and character like UKI. It’s been almost a year since I started coming here—and somehow, they just keep getting better.

Their top-tier menu item, Ikigai, is priced at 166K, and yes—it’s worth every sip. It’s a super matcha latte with a bold, earthy aftertaste that doesn’t linger too long, just enough to remind you how excellent it is. The crowd favorite, Kaze Matcha Latte, is also a standout—bold yet incredibly smooth and easy to drink.

Don’t even get me started on their desserts. The matcha basque cheesecake and matcha jade velvet cake? Absolute perfection. They complement the drinks so well, it’s honestly hard to stop at just one bite.

Skip the rest—UKI Matcha is where your matcha journey should begin (and end).

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Stephen Santosa
text:
A nice decent premium matcha cafe in SCBD area

Visiting June 23, 2024, the store is still full and queue is long in the weekend. For dine-in we were suggested to queue outside waiting for available table. Otherwise, you can just order for take away.

The place is small but nicely aircon and has minimalist design. All white and clean. Quite comfortable for chitchatting, but not for large group.

Matcha tastes quite good, i order Kaze, tastes a but grassy but still has good matcha flavour. I give a thumbs ub for the cheesecake for its softness and richness. The matcha ganache also suits perfectly with the cake.

Overall good. Great place to taste matcha. Beware of crowds if visiting in weekends.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: KeruPug
text:
Went there on Ramadhan holiday. Waiting time 40min. Drinking come 30min. Cakes order is pretty fast.

The quality  of matcha is superb.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Jessica Faustina
text:
The place is small but nicely aircon and has minimalist design. All white and clean. Quite comfortable for chitchatting, but not for large group.

I really enjoy all the variants they offer, but i think the place is a bit overhyped.

The original cheesecake basque is too sweet for my taste. The green one is just right 💚

I ordered Ikigai (the small cup) with oat latte, it was good, not bitter, they used oat milk for my ikigai so i can taste the oat too. a bit pricey imo. If i have to order again probably will go with kaze or sora.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Tiara Fariza
text:
Visited twice.
My first time visiting Uki was on May 2024, where it was very very crowded. I didn’t get any place to sit inside. Decided to enjoy the drinks outside. Weather was windy so it was acceptable.

Ordered Iced Usucha Kaze & Hot Akemi Latte. Immediately fell in love. Full bodied matcha with strong umami & lingering taste. Reminds me of my trip to Japan. Although the temperature for the latte was not hot at all. The portion was small.

Price-wise is pretty expensive. Other places also use Niko Neko with cheaper price :).

My second visit was on early October. It was Sunday morning and very empty. I sat down and enjoyed the matcha-making view in front of me while waiting for my Kaze Dirty Matcha & Genmaicha Mochi Danish.

Personally I think the Kaze one’s more suitable for usucha rather than dirty matcha like what I ordered. It’s a little bit hard to taste the pistachio hint from Kaze.

The Genmaicha Mochi Danish was out of the world. Crunchy, creamy, chewy. It was a perfect dessert to start the Sunday morning.

I saw many people complained about the service in Uki Matcha. I have never encountered any bad experience visiting this place. But I agree with the price range, it was pretty expensive, although the taste is very delicious. Overall still a pleasant visit. Will go back again.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Laiqa Wita
text:
post running treats!!!♥️♥️i would say that for a high quality matcha, this place is affordable. the staff were nice and informative, very knowledgeable about the menu. 🌹🌹 pro tip: come in the morning or on a weekday so you dont need to queue. oopssss, by the way, its walk in only, or you can try ordering from go shop!
in this picture are Tenka, Strawberry Matcha, and Miwa (special edition) 🩲🩲🩲🥚🥚🥚

title: Matchaman
name: Eliza Farestiani
text: First time trying matcha latte here. As for the first impression, the atmosphere is nice, aesthetic, cozy and chill to have a chit chat with friends or a date. But as a matcha lover, I feel it is not as bold and as authentic as I expected.

title: Matchaman
name: Anthoinetta Paulina
text:
Came to this place after seeing it went viral on TikTok and it didn’t dissapoint!
The service was pretty fast considering the long queue.
Tried the signature matchaman latte without sugar, and it wasn’t too bitter.
If you like creamy matcha, this one deserves a try!

title: Matchaman
name: Nes Nes
text: A very good matcha served here. No option for matcha grade, all use the same matcha powder. But the menu is vary. Really like the roasted aftertaste from the matcha. Better to grab and go, enjoy blok M area while sipping at the refreshing drink.

title: Matchaman
name: Laras H
text: Favorite matcha spot in Jkt so far! I tried their signature matcha latte, oat matcha latte and strawberry matcha latte.. I’d say go for the signature one 👍 worth queueing if the line is still inside the cafe

title: Matchaman
name: Lela Vista
text: I visited this place on a Saturday night and waited in line for about 25 minutes. The matcha was prepared at the bar table, which was quite a spectacle. However, I’ve previously found a more authentic matcha spot, so this one was just average in taste.

title: Matchaman
name: Anisa
text: Loved the cold-whisked oat milk latte. It was quite thick and creamy with barely any bitterness. I didn’t really like the jasmine matcha latte as it was too strong, almost like black tea, and the jasmine clashed too much with the matcha as a result.

title: Matchaman
name: Jay Munthe
text:
In my opinion, the drinks taste pretty average and not worth the long trip. They taste similar to other matcha brands—I tried three different drinks, and honestly, they were just okay.

The place is only popular because it went viral, but there’s no staff cleaning up after customers. We even had to approach the barista to ask for an extra seat because someone had placed their drink on a chair.

title: Matchaman
name: Geyyaratana Wirasutta
text: I will come back. Friendly staffs, matcha taste good! I ordered the peach matcha soda, it did not disappoint. Even my bf who does not like matcha, think its good. Maple Dorayaki is light and soft

title: Matchaman
name: Blue butterfly
text:
We ordered Matchaman Latte and Strawberry Matcha Latte and they were very good!
The matcha was thick and strong but not over powering and it was balanced with the soft hint of milk
We also tried the Creme Brule Dorayaki
It was interesting because one side of dorayaki was soft and plump and the other side was crunchy and sweet, it also has light cream inside, perfect!

title: Matchaman
name: kleo
text:
loved their matcha, its soo good. very very amazed how deep green is their matcha, i ordered their signature matcha. its a cold whisk with their houseblend milk( i choose their fresh milk) and also less sugar.

the place itself is pretty big for a drink place at blok m area, but i could see it could get crowded on the weekends. there are quite plenty of seats but definitely not a place you can hang at a longer time.

i come back the second time at the same day for a takeaway hehe. they pack my order tightly with a cup holder and a paper bag. the staff is very nice and helpful!

would definitely comeback for their yummy matcha 🍵

title: Matchaman
name: DANELLE PUTRI CINTA
text:
Not really a matcha drinker, but I went there to meet a friend — and Kak Zahwa & Kak Catherine ended up making me one with their excellent service. Every server was incredibly friendly and did their job really well.
Thank you for the warm welcome, I’ll definitely be coming back!

title: Matchaman
name: Vania Ghassani Putri
text: Tried their signature matcha latte (less sugar) and i loved it! Definitely could taste the matcha, perfect amount of bitterness and creaminess, and the price is not bad. Will come back to try other drinks on their menu.

title: Matchaman
name: Hilya tazkia
text: Bought matcha latte and pistachio one. Pistachio is really pricey but worth the price. Didn't expect it to be this good. Pro tip: If you order it online better ask to put the ice in seperate container, otherwise you'll only taste water with green color

title: Matchaman
name: Chris G
text: The place was crowded with people as expected. Had to wait for chairs to wait for our order. The matcha was ok but have tasted better ones.

title: Matchaman
name: Rina Mah
text: Amazing matcha, space design, and staff. Tasty matcha (highly recommend the honey sea salt cream matcha!) 🍵💚

title: Matchaman
name: Ripkian Jaelani
text: This a nice place to experiencing the taste of Matcha. I ordered Pure Matcha Water it is my favorite, the color is so vibrant and I can said that the umami & bitterness were in a good notes. Another one is Matcha latte, so smooth, creamy and silky texture. The place also interesting U can take a look at the Bar how their barista making the matcha.

title: Matchaman
name: Steviera Putri
text: honestly one of the best matchas ive ever had. though i would go back just for the creme brulee dorayaki alone. its very popular, so there's almost always going to be a wait, but its worth it. theres limited seating, so id suggest to have it outdoors under the park's shade

title: Matchaman
name: Hayyu Anshary
text: So, we went here after heavy lunch, in need of cold beverages. The spot outside were smoking area (lining chairs of 5) so if you're sensitive to smoke, better go inside directly like we did. Appreciate how they list the ingredients of each drink so we can choose what we want. Tried oat matcha latte (no sugar) and sea salt cream matcha latte (no sugar option is not available, so it is as it is). They have ample seatings, more than I expect for blok m cafe. Still full tho when we came. They have petite tap for water with small paper cups stacked up beside it, for you to drink (appreciate it Matchaman!). The sea salt cream matcha latte is way too sweet for my taste, but the oat matcha latte without sugar is perfect (strong but creamy). If one day they make that sea salt cream matcha latte unsweetend, we'll be very delighted. Also, props to all customers as they all throw their paper and plastic cups directly to the trashcan inside the store, thus leaving the seating place clean immediately for the next customers. You should do too when you visit 😉

title: Matchaman
name: Agnes S
text:
The matcha latte is enjoyable but for 65k , i wouldnt purchase again.
Dorayaki is fluffy but tastes and looks more like a normal pancake instead of authentic japanese dorayaki. The butter smells fishy too.
They shouldn’t call it dorayaki😅🤣

title: Matchaman
name: Hanif Anwar
text: As a casual matcha enjoyer, you could put a gun on my forehead and i wouldn’t be able to tell you the difference of this “real” matcha and other matcha you would get at other cafes. It’s a nice matcha tho. It seems that people are here mostly for the photo op, nothing wrong with that too. Price seems insane, but I guess thats just how it is

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Jessica
text: i tried their ice ikigai latte and it was so good🫶🏻 muchh luvv, despite i hve to travel 19km just to have my matcha latte, but it’s worth it. and their warabi mochi also so good la, the texture is like the mochi in japan👍🏼

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Sophia Hrytsko
text: The cafe is inside scbd park. It is small, and to get there you have to wait in line. I waited about 20 minutes, but it was worth it!😍 I chose Strawberry matcha (62k), and Original basque cheesecake + add tenka matcha ganache (75k). Their cheesecake is perfect😍🤤 This is definitely the best dessert I've ever had❤️‍🔥🫶🏻

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Felicia Agatha
text: Honestly it is quite expensive, but they have the best matcha in Jakarta so far. Like even the cheapest matcha latte is already so umami. If only it is not this expensive, I would have bought it everyday 😔 I usually only order takeout, since the waiting list for dine in is always so long. I don’t recommend the place as a place to hangout at, as this branch is very crowded and the place is small to begin with. I would recommend their branch at Eco8 Puri instead, there they have more relaxing ambience, with high-ceiling glasshouse vibe (not as claustrophobic)

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Bramanto Wijaya
text:
this place sells matcha based drinks and foods. for the drink, you pick your matcha type first. they have different options, with different prices. if you have no idea what to order, you can start with the akemi one. after that you choose the drink between the regular green tea, or latte.

i like the design of the interior. it's all white with a splash of green. it's beautiful. i'm not a fan of green tea, because i find it has a strong flavor of grass. surprisingly, i really like their flavor of matcha. it doesn't have any hint of grass. sadly, it's quite expensive for a drink. i know they use a high quality of matcha here.

the price shown on the menu is before 5 percent service charge, and 10 percent tax. also, it takes a while for the drinks to be ready, at least 30 minutes. they prepare each drink by hand. one trick to handle this is i would order and pay, then i leave to grab a snack, food, or just looking around the area. after that i go back to have my drink.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Agista Jung
text:
Didn’t expect the matcha will be that good. Creamy and yet balance. We chose the cheapest Matcha Powder Tenka which has nutty flavour. I think everything we got to try there were all delicious. The matcha basque cheesecake was also good, the flavour between cheese and matcha are balanced. I also love its warm, soft, and chewy Zenzai. The highlight of the Zenzai is its red-bean paste.

The place is quite small so you might expect waiting list. The service is good, staffs are friendly and helpful. Price is Jakartans average affordability, yes I am talking about SCBD are price. So, it might be quite expensive for some. But for this matcha grade taste, it is definitely worth it

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Angelina Setiawan
text:
The place was quite aesthetic, white themed interior. I tried Sora Latte Iced and Akemi Usucha, the matcha was okay (you could taste the difference) but the portion was meh. Too pricey for a few sips and it's done. I got there before the long queue but I saw people got their ordered for quite a long time. The cheesecake was good and creamy, the matcha ganache was too sweet (I think it's white chocolate mixed with matcha) but again too pricey.

I spent a total of Rp234.500, I don't think I'll comeback here again.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: V
text:
Let me tell you about this gem of a matcha place! Seriously, their matcha is on another level, not just your average matcha bar. And oh, the desserts? I couldn’t resist trying the Basque, and boy, was it good!

What I love most is the vibe here. It’s the perfect spot to chill out after a hectic day of meetings or office madness. Nestled in the heart of the office area, it’s like a little oasis where you can recharge your batteries, even if it’s just for a short while.

Now, I’ll be honest, the prices are a bit steep, but when you taste the quality, you’ll understand why. I don’t mind splurging a bit for something this good. Definitely worth every penny! So if you’re looking for a cozy spot to unwind and indulge in some top-notch matcha treats, look no further.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: yuuchan ichiigo
text:
Nutty, creamy, umamii
Perfect pokonyaaa, satisfied banget
The cheesecake also gooodddd
Love it

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Tunnamayoo
text: So far the best matcha bar in Jakarta, although the price is a bit expensive, it is very worth it. If you want to try the real matcha, you should try this. The deserrt is also delicious😋😋

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Irving
text:
Nice place to chill at when you're in SCBD Park. They have a lot of Matcha and green tea selections.
We tried the Ikigai which was balanced and very pleasant, and Kaze which was a bit more toasty and astringent.

If you're on a budget, their basic options are tasty as well! Sora oatmilk latte with no sugar was my pick and it was great.

I really recommend getting the Original Cheesecake with Matcha Ganache.

Pricing is a bit high, but considering the ingredients and location, I can say it's just right.

The lady coordinating the waiting list and table arrangements did a very good job, being helpful, friendly and positive at all times

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Emilia
text: The matcha taste good. I like the matcha ice cream the most. The mochi waffle really chewy and kinda bitter but blend well with matcha ice cream. Their basque cheesecake also tasty. Every menu worth to try.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Natasha Diva
text:
