Ordered sora latte (less sugar) and akemi latte (normal). Both tasted good. Matcha flavor is strong, nutty and creamy. Milk doesn’t overpower the matcha. The matcha gelato was ok, nothing special. However it’s overpriced for matcha cafe. But worth to give it a try. The place is very small hence why it’s always packed during weekend/national holidays.

Little tip: go during the weekday to avoid long lines

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Nathania Callista
text:
Well, Just normal. I order Sora matcha & Hira tea. A bit expensive and and the size is too small.

Matcha :
- Too creamy

Hira tea :
- Really strong taste

Both overall good and have unique taste. Worth to try!

Just the things not comfortable is less seating area.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Lee Wan Aun
text: Matcha specialist. Match Basque Cheesecake is so powerful 😋

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: etoile ling
text: If u a matcha lover u will like this place. I couldn't really differentiate the matcha itself for the drinks part but i do love the cheesecake. Pricewise is pricey enough maybe the ingredients are imported from japan. Service was fast considering they stir the matcha manually by hands but noone really pay attention for the tables as there were still some empty tables with the dirty glasses from previous customers n i cleaned them myself to get my table. The place design is good but somehow the architecture makes any sounds inside become super loud. Couldnt really enjoy a me time here coz of the noise and the seat itself is not comfy to sit

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Gerry
text:
One of the best matcha in Jakarta in my opinion, i just dont like the waiting and parking here cost 100.000 rupiah, tho’ you can park on a nearby parking lot outside, it took you some time to walk to SCBD Park.

For those who love milk and sugar in their matcha, you should come here

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: N Liang
text:
Best matcha in town! Very similar to the one we tried at Kyoto, near Kyomizudera: Gokago. The art of whisking the matcha was a real expert. Their Hojicha has a hint of peppermint, it tasted different from regular Hojicha. Their cheesecake was awesome with the matcha ganache on top.
The sitting place was not too spacious, but clean, cozy and nice. You have to be patient to wait for your drinks as they are all hand processed one by one.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Aurora Valentina
text:
Came here on sunday around 3pm, crowded inside bcs the place is quite small. Line up then order our beverage, i ordered the kaze it has nutty aftertaste. Also got their matcha opera, overall was good.

The staff informed us that we had to wait around 20mins to get our beverage. U can see the staff making the beverage on the counter while you wait.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Monica Marianes
text: Got the matcha gelato. It was creamy and very soft. Can really taste the matcha and it didn’t have any unpleasant aftertaste. Portion was okay, price was okay. Space is way too crammed. Can’t even wait for takeout orders inside the premise. The latte was half a cup, with ice lol.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Raven Moiras
text: You can taste the differences in each matcha so you can try each one and honestly every matcha is good (i tried the dirty matcha and i think there’s not really a difference w/ the original one beside the coffee flavour which i dont really like 😬) depending on what you are looking for in a matcha and the cashier/barista will help you pick whats best for you 😁. Just try the original basque cheesecake with the matcha ganache (you will regret it if you dont). Place is just really packed and the place is too small to contain this matcha goodness so if you see that it’s packed and no one is leaving anytime soon, just order for takeaway 😅.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: unevenplan
text: Such a nice Matcha break in the middle of Jakarta’s buzz. Good atmosphere, quiet long lines, but worth the wait!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Deryan Thimoti
text: For someone who didn’t really fancy Matcha, I felt that their matcha drink was nothing special. However, their cheesecake with matcha is the standout performer. For an additional IDR 15000, your cheesecake will be drenched with matcha sauce that tasted sweet and it complement perfectly with the cheesecake. I love the ambience, however this place will always be crowded on weekends and you will definitely wait for a long time just to order their dishes.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Rex Taylor
text:
Finally I got the chance to try the new Japanese cafe named UKI Matcha. This was my 2nd attempt. 1st time, I was not that patient to wait 😅. The hot ikigai latte was the highlight, with a nice mix of sweet and bitter flavors that made it enjoyable. The matcha basque cheesecake was also delicious, offering a rich & creamy taste. However in my opinion, the matcha flavor is not as strong as I expected.

However, the hojicha shiratama zenzai didn't quite meet my expectations. The flavor was a bit too mild and the texture didn't appeal to me. Despite this, the cafe was busy and it took around 18-20 minutes for my order to be ready, which seemed reasonable given the crowd.

Overall, the visit was mostly pleasant with some great flavors, especially if you like Japanese-inspired desserts and drinks.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: H S
text: The Ikigai Matcha, advertised as "the finest matcha from Japan," is overpriced but delicious. Although I was initially shocked by the tiny portion, the taste made up for it. The matcha was creamy, dense, and not too sweet, perfectly blended with fresh milk. However, the service was disappointing, as the servers were not friendly when giving us our drinks. Spending 175,000 rupiah on such small drinks felt excessive, and the unfriendly service detracted from the overall experience, making it hard to justify the expense despite the excellent taste.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: D F
text:
On weekdays the cafe was full but we got a nice seat at the end of the room. Host was really friendly and accomodative. Ikigai matcha was superbly nice witha hint of earthy note and nuttyness balances, and this is holy grail dessert place. Basque matcha was superbly smooth hits all the right notes. Matcha cookie was freshly made frm scratch is warm and sooo worth the wait. Soft cookie with ooey gooey matcha salt center. Worh the wait bangeet!!

Overall very happy. And for those of you that complains about how long it takes to prep the drinks mind you they are ALL MANUALLY hand whisked and yes good things takes some time y’all. So just sit back relax, knowing that your patience will be rewarded tenfolds…

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Panca Rahmi
text: ordered kaze matcha latte, hira latte, and cheesecake with matcha ganache. super duper nyam! the best matcha in town eventho somepeople said it was overpriced BUT go for it and try the difference😗

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: rana khalida
text:
The Original Basque Cheesecake with Matcha Sauce was absolutely delicious! 💯 I also tried the strawberry matcha, usucha sora, and the akemi latte. For dine-in, there’s a time limit of about 1 hour (if I’m not mistaken) since the seating area is limited. There’s also a toilet inside the outlet, so you don’t have to walk far to the public toilets in SCBD Park.

It’s better to park at Electronic City since it’s a shorter walk!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Aisyah Gina Fawziyah
text: Best matcha cafe in jakarta i've tried so far! They have varieties of ceremonial grade matcha and other japanese tea powder to choose from, depending on your preference, and you can choose your preferred brewing method. They also have good selections of pastries and dessert to pair with your drink too. Some of the bevs i've tried and liked so far are tenka latte, akemi latte, hira latte and wakoucha latte. If you're originally not a matcha lover, i would recommend tenka, because it has a mass-pleasing taste in my opinion; it doesn't showcase the floral and vegetal notes too much but more of that nutty sweetness of matcha. Hira is good if you want to try something other than matcha; it's not overly nutty and you can get a bit of that chocolate-y aftertaste. Having said that, i think it's safe to say uki matcha lattes are the best because they use the cold whisk method with the right amount of milk, so it's perfectly creamy while still highlighting the taste of the tea powder. I definitely will come back here to try more of their drinks!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Danny Wongso
text:
Must try their delicious matcha

They need time to make it, so just enjoy the waiting time

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Joycerlyn Chancellor
text:
Pretty legit ceremonial matcha cafe with proper serving technique, I think there’s so much hate here because people mostly come as matcha newbies and treat this place as coffee shop when it’s not.

As someone who has experimented with various ceremonial matcha, the matcha here is actually impressive. I had Kaze variant and the umami really delivers, I can tell it’s a very good ceremonial grade because the umami stands out so well on my taste buds, it doesn’t taste like generic matcha you find on stores. And the techniques are all correct, staffs are very well trained in proper matcha whisking technique and they take their time to ensure the matcha products are well whisked before they are served so they don’t leave any clumps.

What makes them slow is people over ordering like it’s a coffee shop - one order can contain 2-4 matcha beverages, and preparing matcha takes a minimum 2-4 minutes. This is where people get impatient because they expect service pace of starbucks, when it’s actually normal to prepare matcha at their speed rate. For size, yes it’s petite but I’m glad I can customize the sweetness and milk choice.

Generally it’s a good matcha cafe, pricey but for me I understand why because ceremonial matcha is indeed expensive. Before you come and bash with negative review, it’s best to understand ceremonial matcha concept first. Place can be crowded, but working station is very clean, I’m impressed with the staffs’ standards.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: KA Wiryohatmojo
text:
Today is my 15th visit I think, been here since week one. Semua flavour matcha-nya nggak pahit padahal aku selalu 0% sugar, I love it and it signifies a higher quality. Even when served as a straight tea / usucha tetep nyaman dan ga pahit. My trick is to drink each flavour in different situations. Ikigai and Ren for special meditative quiet weekday mornings/evenings, Akemi for a lovely floral touch, Kaze kalo pengen rasa kacang dikittt kayak pistachio kalo dikasih susu, Sora buat nyantai2 ringan nyaman, Tenka juga enak aja tetep.

Today I ordered 2 Sora and 2 Akemi matcha for takeaway, I like them as hot lattes while my friends love the cold latte version or usucha. Their to-go packaging is proper. I kinda felt that we could’ve dined in karena tadi sepi menjelang early evening, but we had other plans so to-go it is.

Dessertsnya rasanya comforting dan lumayan banyak pilihan for different preferences. Matcha yang dipake bukan cooking grade jadi tetep kental dan strong rasanya, bukan kayak most matcha desserts yang asal ada rasanya. I find the cheesecake and pain au matcha delicious, the opera cake enjoyable, and the bola bola mochi dessert as well as the mochi waffle super lovely and hearty.

Also some of the employees remember you and your order! Amazing service and hosting by the staff members dari kasir sampai manager 💚

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Amanda Kitamura
text: Been craving for matcha basque, and found it here! Super happy cause it was good! Their matcha latte was also super good tho.. the matcha was thick, and you can actually taste the umami from it too~ they have other dessert that looks tasty too, will definitely give this place another visit to try the other!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Agatha Fajarani
text: a very great place for anyone who loves everything matcha. their service is excellent, since It was my first time the cashier was so kind to explain to me in detail what they serve and all their menus.. oh! their matcha latte also taste good✨ you can requeste for no sugar

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Angeline Gloria
text: Second time trying their iced matcha latte, and I opted for their Kaze selection with fresh milk and less sugar, it was so yummy! The stronger, nuttier flavor was amazing, I might even go sugar-free next time. Not to mention the super chill vibes - went there around 10am on holiday, and it was perfect! Less crowds, calming music and a delicious drink.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Andi Marsali
text:
Visit on Jun 16, 2024
We arrived on 12.15, only 2 person queue, the maitre d are very friendly and helpful.
Lots of matcha varieties and menu, must try their cheese cake, amazing taste, not so overpowering cheese flavour

Suggestion to add 1 more person to explained about varieties of matcha and menu, thus customer know what to order in front of the cashier

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Dexter
text: the basque cheesecake with TENKA Matcha Ganache is really good

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Cavin Agustanto
text:
Tried the Ikigai, let me tell you.. there is no ikigai at all..

So overhyped, u better off drinking the normal one.. taste like es teh tawar for 150K

Also tried the shiratama zenzai, lebih enak ronde bandung deh. Yah i give it is chewy texture, but not worth 65K lah!

Can try one time, after that move on. Not worth the wait for a table. Just order takeaway, faster and more “ikigai”

Place is nice. Service is nice.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Joanna
text:
Great place to get matcha!
Definitely recommend if you’re trying to find match-flavored drinks & cakes. The only thing I found slightly negative is the fact that there’s not many seats inside, especially because it can be crowded in the afternoon through early evening time. Other than that, I think it’s a nice place!🍵

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: michelle
text:
matcha is good BUT you have to queue for 30 mins minimum and you'll only get very little portion of the drink. they also don't know what they're selling because i bought 3 different variations of iced matcha lattes and they ended up tasting very similar :/ upselling  too much? real matcha lovers would recommend matcha without milk but since milk is + 12k then i guess ofc they'll take that advantage.

first and last time trying and i would definitely go to other matcha places.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: I C
text:
The queue was crazy, even for takeout. Waited around 1 hour for the queue, another 30mins for the drinks.
Price a bit steep for the portion. I ordered with normal amount of ice and yet it doesnt even fill the whole tiny cup, imagine if it was no ice. Taste was ok but not sure its worth all the time spent queueing and waiting in line. And frankly, the staff's services a bit on slower side.

title: Matchaman
name: Aryo Indarto
text: Great place to taste a ceremonial grade Matcha and if you must a selection of soft with filling Dorayaki. An umami explosion in your mouth.

title: Matchaman
name: ALVIRA LIMBAI KANCHAI
text:
while walking along Blok M, i stumbled upon this Matcha Cafe..i remember it was raved on socmed, so i guess it is about time..When we arrived, the café is quite packed as it was saturday...Tho many orders were made,the preparing process was made quite fast!!love it..got my matcha in about 10 mins?it sokay..i dont mind..

i opted for strawberry matcha..verdict?omg i love love love it!!!a subtle hint of the strawberry jame (handmade) but didnt overshadow the originality of the matcha itself ❤️‍🔥 it tasted so good!!

while waiting for my order, it came to my attention where the chasen & chawan were placed on the shelves..i guess they were for sale?

if only i were to go back to the alley, i deffo stop and buy again..haha unfortunately..i didnt..
till next time Matchaman..please open more branches...at the malls maybe? ❤️
love,
ur fan from 🇲🇾

title: Matchaman
name: Fina Sukmawati
text:
💯% pure authentic matcha in Jakarta
My fav was strawberry matcha latte 🍃🍓🍵
So good 🤤

title: Matchaman
name: namelessjune
text: I'd absolutely give Matchaman a 10/10! I was really craving a creamy matcha, and their Honey Sea Salt Cream Matcha hit the spot perfectly. It was exactly what I wanted.

title: Matchaman
name: Kevin Bryan
text: Ambience of the place is really dope, something very different, but can get really crowded. Place is really good to chill and get refreshed as their AC is really solid. Great ceremonial matcha, a bit on the pricy side doe.

title: Matchaman
name: Junita
text: Really good matcha place in Blok M!! It’s not too sweet and surprisingly the coconut one is very good and refreshing. The only thing that I don’t like is the pantry where they make the matcha, because it was very open and not very hygienic. Maybe it’s better to put some glass divider in the pantry. Other than that the matcha is good and the cafe have a really nice interior design :)

title: Matchaman
name: Nio D. S.
text:
Overpriced for mid quality matcha.

Yes the matcha hype is such a money maker, but this matcha is basically worth half the price.

title: Matchaman
name: Radeleon Othniel Simarmata
text:
Came here after hearing about all the hype of this matcha store named "Matchaman".

When I came, I didn't try the Matcha, but my mother did, I'm not sure if it was good or not so I can't rate that.

For the dorayaki, I did try it, I think it was the chocolate flavored one, it is good, but I ate it cold at home, I think it would be better to eat when it's hot.

For the place itself, it's really great, great design, free water (I was thirsty when I came there so it's a plus for me!), cold air conditioning (not that cold to the point you start freezing).

Prices are pretty expensive, as I remember the prices are about 50k+ for the drinks, for the food it is pretty high in price too, but I forgot how much.

I hope they maintain their services, store, etc. As I have seen how many hyped up stores that couldn't maintain its qualities.

title: Matchaman
name: Euginia Stephany
text:
One of the best and proper matcha drinks in town! Ordered the matcha signature one and it instantly became my favorite!

However, the place was too crowded and hard to get some seats. Great as grab and go place to have your matcha dose

title: Matchaman
name: Evina Puspita
text: The Honey Seasalt Cream Matcha Latte is a perfectly creamy drink with a subtle sweetness. The matcha flavor shines through, balanced nicely by the rich, smooth cream and a touch of honey. The seasalt cream on top adds a savory note, giving the drink a unique and satisfying finish without being overly sweet.

title: Matchaman
name: Eleonora Natasya
text: Tried the best seller matchaman latte, and it's definitely on the creamier side. It was very smooth as well. I'm not a matcha expert, so for me the price was a bit high tbh, on par (or even higher?) to the other well known brand (u**). But if you're a matcha lover, definitely give this a try ;) the place was packed but for 2 people we still managed to squeeze in. Comfy and full air con blasting in the midst of blok m heat :)

title: Matchaman
name: Tripnya Afi
text:
Really love the taste of matcha.It's original matcha from Uji. Pure, the taste is exactly like i drank in Uji, Japan.
Good service, friendly employees.
Definitely love to purchase in other time.

title: Matchaman
name: Vidusha Tewari
text: Incredible Strawberry Matcha Latte!! The taste was authentic as the matcha drink wasn’t overly sweet. Definitely worth the price! Eager to try more drinks in the future 😊

title: Matchaman
name: Ida Taylor
text: Excellent place for matcha. Very exciting store!

title: Matchaman
name: Laura Angela
text:
I tried their matchaman latte (no sugar), it's creammy, slight bitter, and sweetness. Also, their matcha latte tasted more light because the differences of milk.
They dont have any cultivar option and it became a minus point as a "matcha cafe"

title: Matchaman
name: Evan Tan
text: great matcha, quick service, and cheaper than UKI. blok M on the weekend is absolutely insane tho, parking was tough to find, but thankfully the line wasn't too long here and they made the orders quite fast.

title: Matchaman
name: Daffa khairy
text:
The matcha is the best i've ever tasted so far not too pricey either, the dorayaki was a suprise, it was soft and melt into my mouth

Plus bonus point for playing toro y moi

title: Matchaman
name: Nadhira Haifa
text:
Newest matcha shop in blok M area. Ordered the signature one: iced matchaman latte with no sugar. I can taste a slight of roasted nut, the taste of umami followed by the bitterness. The bitter aftertaste stays a little longer.
And ordered another one: matcha white chocolate frappe that surprisingly not as sweet as I imagined. Since not a fan of too much sweet of my drinks, this one is good! The bittersweet is mixed well. But if you really like yours really sweet, you might add another sugar.
I came here the evening on the 1st day of ramadhan, since most of people are fasting I guess, the area is not crowded. No line. It was all very fast.
But if you come in the peak time, you especially om the weekend, just do not let your hopes hight by not getting any queue. You know, it’s blok M😂
So for this experience , 5 stars then 🌟
Oh, not to forget to mention about the price, more pricey than B*e but less pricey than U*I. It’s just so so.

title: Matchaman
name: ananda putri
text: Their matcha is good, but unexpectedly their dorayaki is exceptional!! Especially the maple butter and chocolate one, i suggest you should try it too

title: Matchaman
name: ntshbng
text: You have to try their Matchaman Signature! Tastes even better with less sugar. It’s really good, and even though the place is usually packed, service is super quick and efficient. The double choco and crème brûlée dorayaki are pretty good too! The place is always busy, but the staff handle customers really well, everything flows nicely! By the time I’m writing this review, I’ve already come back about three times to re-order.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Affandy Murad
text: The most cozy place to enjoy any various Matcha (Japan Green Tea) both drink and cake. The interior itself dominated by broken white that spread clean and calmness vibe. It is located in lot 6 SCBD park, behind of its enterance gate. Truly recommended for people who loves matcha so much. The carpark also available here. The toilet also very clean.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Steven Prasetya
text: Overrated. Queue was horrible. It’s just the hype I guess. I was so eager to try because lots of tiktok videos mentioned this place as award-winning, authentic, and such. I expected too much I guess. I tried it and didn’t even remind me of the ones I tried in Kyoto 🥲🥲

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Sagitta Ahimsha
text:
Good place to experience variety of macha beverage and cakes.

Neat and clean preparation in front of you.

Japanese style ...

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Stella Marselie
text: Their basque cheesecake is so good. I prefer without the matcha ganache cause for me it’s too sweet. Their matcha is ceremonial grade for sure. My friend and I ordered akemi & kaze. Both drinks are delicious. Kaze is like akemi on steroids, it has this big punch of umami ✨ (forgot to take a picture of the matcha 😂)

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Sharon
text: I ordered their Kaze icelatte it was nutty and creamy use normal milk, and basque cheesecake so good also but very little portion for 65K . ada harga ada barang lah ya hhe not to mention that the parking space (zero gas support they said) you have to pay 100k if u use normal car & electric 0 K. l o l so better park ur car in a across the building

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Dee 777
text: Seiu matcha best! Lady in Pink good and Basque cheese cake (too sweat)

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Carrieann Caroline
text:
One of the best matcha I’ve ever tasted in jakarta 🍵
Not pet friendly, but you get oat milks for your pet as special treats.
Drinks are a bit pricey with small portions, but still worth it. ☺️

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Shella
text:
went here at 10am and gladly there was still no queue at all eventhough it was long weekend.

for the drink i tried ikigai without milk. i actually have no idea how should matcha taste / is this umami or not, so i can only describe it tastes kinda bitter. maybe i should ordered another drink with different matcha powder so i could make a comparation.

for the dessert i tried original basque cheesecake + matcha ganache. the cheesecake was so melted, soft, cheesy but not too salt. unfortunately the matcha ganache was too sweet for me.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ben Pamuntjak
text:
Came with nephews, because of fomo, 🙂.

Tried their regular matcha tea... good.

Matcha chesse cake... not for me

Matcha ice cream and waffle... good.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Ajeng K Ratri
text: Good matcha, good place but it is often crowded.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Levina Ranisari
text:
The first time I went here when it had just opened for 2 weeks, it was great. My friend and I ordered strawberry matcha and ice matcha latte, and both tasted good and the serving portion was decent (filled the plastic cup full). The matcha basque cheesecake was also delicious. So I thought it was worth the high price. And because we went early in the morning, there was no queue. After that, I tried going again, but the queue was too long; one time I put my name before dinner, and after I finished my dinner, my name was still quite far from the next-in-line.

Then finally I managed to get in around 2 weeks ago. Brought my friend from overseas and ordered usual ice matcha latte. But what we got was so embarassing. They only served half full of the (already much smaller size than average) plastic cup, and it tasted DILUTED with ice/water. Like after 1 hour+ queue and for 65k...? What happened, dude?? My friend even said that this would the first and last encounter with Uki Matcha.

Overall it was horrible and was not worth our time and money at all. I don't know what went wrong after my first visit. But if this is their standard now, disappointment is an understatement

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Clara Tan
text:
Located in SCBD Park, UKI Matcha is one of place that give you many selections of matcha type. Also, you can choose how do you want your drink to make, straight tea or latte. They have the desserts too. A famous one is their basque cheesecake.
The place itself is small, seats are limited. You need to queue, different line between take away and dine in. It could get long queue, since it's still new and hype.
As myself waits half an hour until my take away order ready. Since i don't really know "the matcha world", i choose depend on my insting lol and gets akemi matcha latte iced and original basque (add tenka matcha ganache). For the drink, i think it's nice, sweet and creamy. The price is bit higher, the portion is quite small. For the cheesecake, the taste is balanced. Add matcha ganache for more sweetness.
If you love matcha, i think it's worth to try.
For me, if there's a next time, i like to try their straight tea.
Gracias ~

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Nabila Asrie
text:
Tried the Tenka & Seiu Matcha and Koga Hojicha.
Surprisingly i liked the hojicha more!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Michelle
text: Visited UKI on a weekday afternoon and place was pretty packed (we managed to grab a seat while we waited in line thankfully!). Ordered the infamous basque cheesecake with matcha ganache and sora latte hot with fresh milk. Latte was really good and smooth, no complaints! The cheesecake itself was really good as well - creamy and not too sweet but tbh I wouldn’t recommend the ganache add on unless you have a sweet tooth! Couldn’t really taste the matcha and for me it straight up tasted like white choco :( Nonetheless, will def revisit to try their other menu!

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Devina Amanda
text: Host was impolite, kept talking with other staff while taking my order. Had to ask me the same question twice eventhough i already answered. Ended up ordering only one drink because of the bad attitude. Strawberry matcha is just so-so, nothing special, and 86k for that. Place is actually nice, but parking is 100k. Will not go back

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Aurel
text:
Matcha nya enakkk
Memang pricey but its worth the quality
But the queue was too long and had to wait for 30 mins until the order arrived.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Kimboy Jempol
text:
I queued for 1 hour to try this matcha.
Please be patient, because they made tye beverages one by one. I tried the mochi with matcha as well.

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Arisona Billah
text: the original basque cheesecake is heaven. melted to just my liking. often times the queue is unbearable. but quality of food is great

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: mona felicia
text:
Awesome! All drinks and desserts is too nice…
Para penggemar matcha wajib datang kesini…
Too good to be true banget… definitely will come back !

title: UKI Matcha - SCBD Park
name: Syaraufa Zahra
text: Might be the hottest and my favorite matcha spot in jkt! The price range after tax is relatively high— but their matcha options and quality are top notch you should at least try it once. The seating space is very limited, clean, pretty chilly and they only allow for walk-ins. Honestly i could sip their matcha all-day that’s all i want to say🫠🤍

title: Matchaman
name: Lim Shien Yew
text: the matcha here is truly amazing. while there will be a queue and you gotta wait for your drinks (since they are eternally always preparing), but it’s well worth the wait!

title: Matchaman
name: Stella Margaret Immanuella
text:
The place is easy to find, near MRT Blok M. They offer a variety of premium matcha drinks. The place is not very spacious.
I ordered a signature matchaman latte: delicious and creamy. Rating: 8/10.

title: Matchaman
name: Dkn
text: Just a regular matcha drink at least for my taste buds

title: Matchaman
name: Tia Pratiwi
text: Best matcha I've had so far. Definitely a must go if you love matcha. It is however kinda pricey but it's worth it

title: Matchaman
name: tengku marini
text: A little bit queing but not long, the drink was nice, you can taste the premium of the green tea taste, the drink wasnt sweet so i like it, it wasnt that crowded so me and my friends can seat as we enjoy our matcha drink, overall, it was great

title: Matchaman

text: one of my favorite matcha in 2025!!💗🍵

title: Matchaman
name: Jennifer Natalia
text: One of the best matcha in Jakarta. The ceremonial matcha (less sugar) was creamy and you can taste the matcha’s bitterness. Worth to try on the weekdays.

title: Matchaman
name: SAHRUL SIDIKY
text: Good morning, the Barista has greeted me passionately, I asked their reccomendation menu since this was my first time, and it was memorable taste, and completly will coming back.

title: Matchaman

text: Kinda crowded, but the vibes is 10/10. Luvin' their signature matchaman latte

title: Matchaman

text: A little bit pricy, but the taste is good. The service and ambience is worth it…

title: Matchaman

title: Matchaman

text: Always comeback once/twice a week for a cup of matchaman latte!! Best matcha so far, not too pricey, not too bitter, just perfect!!

text: very crowded but still comfy, long queue but fast service. the signature matcha is pretty mid tho, i dont really taste the matcha, just the mix of dairy and oat milk taste too overpowering

title: Matchaman
name: Jane T
text: Taste-wise, hype worthy. For the signature latte, balanced taste between milk and matcha. Very crowded even on weekday noon, seatings are limited, so better grab the drink and go.

title: Matchaman

text: So so the taste.. too expensive. Pistachio matcha nice.

title: Matchaman
name: Ananda Aditya
text: Service is not that good, the waiter and cashier is little bit grumpy

title: Matchaman
name: Devi Loren (devlourent)
text: Came here because many people said it was delicious and I was curious about the taste. And it turned out to be delicious. But when I came here, there was quite a queue, but it wasn't too long to queue. There isn't much seating area, but it's okay.

title: Matchaman
name: Panji Laksmana
text:
The taste is quite good, seems like it's original cause it taste like grass.
But honestly the place is not cozy enough to dine in or just to hangout.

title: Matchaman
name: Ilham Suryo
text: I order espresso matcha latte with less sugar option and it taste refreshing. I personally like how the earthy-taste matcha goes really well with the coffee, so that they're not overpowering each other. I would like to try their dorayaki on my next visit.

title: Matchaman
name: Dina Andriani
text: Great matcha and nice place to hang out, even though they don't have a lot of seats and it's always crowded!

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Plush Enjoyer
text: It took me a bit to realize the cafe was this building. The atmosphere is great. But if i could put .5 on food and atmosphere, I'd do so. Their recommendations were pretty good with what was the recommendations. I dont remember my exact order, but it might be the Dori matcha, and i definitely rememebr it having the Signature Milk Blend. Overall creamier than I thought, but overall a good cup nonetheless. The outside roads were a bit filled with gravel and whatnot, but it's not really their fault as it might be from the construction trucks.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Najib H
text:
Ordered their Kyo variant + signature whisk milk + 25% sweetness. It was creamy and oaty. The sweetness came as an after taste for me.

The place was not to crowded on the weekday night. Plenty of seats left for hangout.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: M *
text:
This small place from the outside may seem small, but it’s surprisingly spacious and minimalist inside. The matcha burnt cheesecake is well-made, with a harmonious blend of cheese and matcha that creates a delightful combination.

The matcha selection is impressive, featuring a variety of ceremonial-grade matchas with distinct flavors. The matcha drinks here are excellent, expertly balanced and offering the convenience of choosing the desired sweetness level and milk type.

Whether you’re a matcha enthusiast or simply looking to indulge in a delightful treat, this place is a must-visit when you’re in PIK

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Johanes Chandra
text: The matcha is good—pleasant and balanced, though not quite exceptional. It feels slightly overpriced for what it offers. The place itself is cozy and clean, creating a comfortable atmosphere. However, it could benefit from warmer lighting to enhance the ambiance, and the background music selection doesn’t quite match the vibe of the space.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Anisa
text:
Good quality matcha. The tama cold whisk was thick and creamy, not bitter even with only 25% sugar, and probably the most concentrated compared to the other types of matcha. The moro macchiato was good as well, a little grassy but not in a bad way; it was rather refreshing. The kyo latte signature whisk had a very slight bitterness with smooth mild tones, and was okay even for someone lactose intolerant.

Their best desserts are the basque cheesecake and parfait. Wouldn’t really recommend the banana pudding or the matcha pudding; they’re not bad but not so good I’d get them again either.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Beatrice Zhang
text:
A bit crowded on public holiday. No long queue, just 1 person ahead of us. Tried Tama Cold Whisk with fresh milk and 75% sugar because I prefer Umami Matcha which neither too creamy nor overpowering the matcha itself. It has a light texture, nice flavour, the umami is good but if you’re a sweet tooth you might prefer to make it 100%.

New updates as of Sep 7th 2025:
They have new menu, Tama is no longer available. I tried Mika Cold Whisk (fresh milk, 75% sugar). It tastes very good, bolder matcha taste. The place had more crowd than my previous visit.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Johanna Tania
text: Been hearing good reviews about Matcha Bae and finally got the chance to try it out. The taste was great but the experience was disappointing.  How come a matcha specialized cafe fails at whisking the matcha correctly, the most basic thing? 🫠 There were clumps of matcha powder all over my drink. When I talked to the server, their reply was “Should we whisk it again?” 😐Yes, they meant whisking the whole drink… That’s just gonna make the drink super frothy 😭 The server also seemed a bit clueless when asked about the menu… Anyway, overall a disappointing experience. Please improve the service 😞

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Evan Tan
text:
overall one of the best matchas i've tried so far. i had the TAMA with their signature whisk, which is apparently maxed out on umami. i asked the server about the signature whisk and they won't tell me what that is cuz they said it's a top secret concoction. well it was really good, so maybe they have good reason to keep anyone from trying to recreate it themselves. hahah.

the line was long, the place was kinda warm cuz of the crowd, but the matcha signature whisk i got was amazing.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Firda Aurellia Darmawan
text:
Easy access for cars since it’s located in a pretty huge shophouse complex. Even though I visited during lunchtime on a weekday, the queue was quite long.

The service was good, we need to select some of the matcha types and the bases in a various prices in order to get your drinks served.

I forgot what matcha type I ordered but Signature Whisk is my drink base. Suits me well since I like milky drinks. Sweetness level was 50% sweet enough without losing the bitterness of the matcha itself.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Alena Mailin
text: I went to Matchabae on Sunday dinner. There wasn't any queue however the place was dirty like there was an earthquake. Many tissue and plastic straw on the ground. Chair and table was really messy. But their matcha was great. I tried Mizu cold whisk and Tama macchiato. I prefer the Tama one which more creamy. If I have time to go back, I would definitely try Tama Cold Whisk

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Sean Tanu
text:
Specialty matcha drinks and dessert cafe in PIK Golf area. Located behind Pantjoran PIK area. The matcha menu is quite extensive and variety of matcha are honestly amazing, they have various taste profile available and the staff explains it quite clearly while offering recommendation for first timer.

I have the hojicha latte (chocolate profile) and cold brew matcha (seaweed profile) and it is absolutely refreshing in the barren heat of PIK afternoon sun.

Unfortunately the seating space is very limited and some of the dessert menu isn’t available for takeaway (ex: parfait matcha). I suggest visiting during weekday instead if possible.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Maria S
text:
A nice pleasant find for authentic, delicious matcha in PIK area. I just tried this place, it’s definitely gonna be my go-to hangout soon 😁

Yummy bitter matcha, with a nice clean surrounding, and a helpful, informative cashier make this a great place! Love it! I look forward to trying the food next time.

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Natasya Susanto
text: The best matcha place in PIK! Their matcha is legit with a great quality. Also such an aesthetic and comfy place with the greatest service. Would always come back here🫶🏻

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Hendra Pangawan
text:
Matcha was great, 15-30 minutes wait on the weekend, place was quite pack on the weekend. They have severall matcha selection, price fair, they have hojicha & several dessert option too.

Service was nice, they take time to explain the menu to us (especially if you're a first timer)

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Chintya Laurencia
text: Great matcha with varieties of ceremonial matcha powder. Ordered the earl grey matcha and matcha yuzu soda — decent, but still a bit too sweet for my liking. Would definitely come back but maybe not during the weekends of public holidays, because when it’s crowded, the queue can take up to 30 minutes.

title: Matcha Bae PIK

text: A cozy place to chill. I ordered their umi matcha with signature whisk. Tasted good. But the price is quite high for a cup of matcha.

from it. Picked up Kashi cold whisk oat milk with 0% sugar and it felt nice, almond nutty flavor. Probably shouldve used around 25-30% sugar but its still nice.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Jordan J
text:
Titik Koma Tanjung Duren

Stumbled upon this gem tucked away in a residential area in Tanjung Duren.

The vibe is inviting, features a modern, cozy design with warm wooden elements, lush plants, and abundant of natural light.

Tried their Es Kopi Susu Titik Koma and it was spot on, creamy with a good coffee kick. Also their Southern Cross Blend for the long black was incredibly clean with a bright acidity. Hands down best long black in the area.

They also have a large outdoor area which is perfect for those who want to smoke.

Service was friendly, the baristas are welcoming and clearly care about good coffee and creating a good experience.

Sometimes you just need that pause from Jakarta's chaos, and Titik Koma delivers :)

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: lissa sugeng
text: Best in Town!! What’s life without coffee! Or if you prefer Tea at Titik Koma. We frequent this place,.. shamefully every day when we are in Jakarta so peaceful, uplifting with positive messages on their cups. Friendly service always! Would recommend absolutely hands down. Message to owners: just need a new stereo system to enjoy some music 🥰. A little Laufey in the background would be perfect. For those who want some “fresh air” you can sit in the garden area.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Benny
text: Perfect place to hang out, to chill, to WFC. Very homey, friendly staff as well. Coffee (kopi susu) is also great. Will come back 4 sure. Parking space not that abundant, so they provided valet service for 10k IDR. BTW, wifi extremely fast, see my last 2 photos below 🤠.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Steven Suprantio
text:
☕ Kopi Titik Koma – Tanjung Duren
I ordered a Hot Magic Coffee and really enjoyed it. The coffee had a strong flavor, nicely balanced with milk. The price is standard for a café.

They provide a good internet connection, and payment is easy with QRIS or credit card. The place has both indoor and outdoor seating, and the ambiance is very comfortable—perfect for working or chatting with friends. The café is set in a big house with a lovely small outdoor park, which adds a relaxing atmosphere.

A cozy spot to enjoy good coffee and spend your time comfortably. 🌿✨

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: ChrisX Profile
text: Inspiring coffee experience. Good coffee. Great atmosphere. Moderate pricing. Great design creates a solemn nuances with plenty seats and places. Will be back for more.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Alvian Winarta
text: kind service, cozy atmosphere, great drinks, imo caramel macchiato (less sugar) was the best one i had out of all the menu, tiger was too sweet (normal sugar), coconut was not my cup of tea, and aren doppio was alright (normal sugar). If i had to choose tiger again i would do half sugar but that could just be me. This place would be great for WFC, hanging out with friends, and just chilling by yourself though seats are limited and the wifi is not as reliable as i would like it to be, comparing it to a certain other coffee place. but overall cant hate on it, 5/5

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Art Art4
text: I often come here after breakfast and have tried several types of coffee and their bread. Today, I decided to try the matcha. The café’s quiet and warm atmosphere always gives a very comfortable feeling, especially with the open area at the back that looks like a garden. The toilets are clean and well-maintained, which is a big plus. This spot is highly recommended if you want a cozy place to work without getting bored of coming here again and again.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Miranti Vidyasari
text: I'm in awe of the hexagon shape of their building. The inside is spacious and has full AC, which is super comfy, especially on a sunny day. We came for their matcha latte, long black, kopi susu tiger, and Flora Lemona with coffee. All of the drinks were acceptably good. The wait time for takeout was moderate.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Evelyn L
text:
The café offers a nice and comfortable atmosphere for remote work. Unfortunately, when we visited, the indoor Wi-Fi connection was quite weak.

Indoor area aside, it has outdoor seating area which is pretty spacious with plenty of tables. Their standout drink seems to be the Kopi Susu Tiger, but they also have a strong focus on matcha, offering a range of ceremonial matcha options on the menu.

Definitely will be one of my go-to favorite spots to work from café!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Tracy Inggriani
text:
Hands down, this is the best coffee in town—especially among cafés that serve kopi susu. I ordered the OG Kopi Susu Tiger as usual, and it literally lifted me to heaven. I thought visiting a different branch of Titik Koma would give me a slightly different taste, but surprisingly, it was just as delicious!

I also absolutely love the vibes there. The interior design gives off a cozy yet modern, jazzy café feel. They also have an outdoor area—perfect for those who want to smoke and still snap some Instagram-worthy pics.

One of the highlights was the parking guy—super friendly and even offered to take my photo in front of the café. Amazing service, amazing person.

Definitely coming back soon!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Selly Chou
text: Various legit coffee and matcha! Nice and aesthetic environment for chilling & WFC. Friendly and helpful staff. Definitely will come back

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Adlina Zahra
text:
Good vibes for study or simply chitchat with friends, good coffee good pastry and the best part is it has a nice and neat prayer room with very clean mukenah.

Very recommended will def come back!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Awang Bagas
text:
Unique place to sip a coffee with a hexagonal-shaped building. Offers various types of seating, whether you are here for work, discuss important matters like business, or just chill and enjoy the warmth of Jakarta is provided here.

The staff are nice and greet the customer warmly, they take care of the order properly and in a quick manner of time. The internet connection is very supportive for you who are a remote worker or just working on your assignment. Definitely a recommended place to visit!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: cici
text:
its really enjoy my coffee here, u should try americano iced❤️
tbh aga kecewa dr kemarin nyari ice black at coffee shop not expecting but here last day di jkt im really happy with my coffee❤️❤️

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Sri Nurcahaya Habiba
text:
The place is cozy, cool, and super comfortable to hang out. The pastries are pretty good too.

But the real highlight is definitely the coffee and matcha — the flavor is so good (esp tiger milk varian) for a price that's still very affordable. No wonder it's always packed! The only thing missing is savory snacks or heavier food options. Hopefully they'll add those to the menu soon!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Lucy Lie
text:
Ambience is good. Esp. the outdoor.

Wifi is POOR extremely :( if u wanna just chat and hang yes can, def not for WFC ppl.

Price is overrated maybe because ambience and place are extra.

Electric plugs are limited and not widely spread.

Food and coffee taste, just ok not extra.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Agnes Sekar Arum Jati
text:
Perfect spot if you’re looking to grab a coffee and enjoy a calm atmosphere. They have a spacious outdoor backyard, making it a great escape from the hustle and bustle of Jakarta🥹🚕🛵
Such a hidden gem tucked away in Tanjung Duren.
Definitely worth visiting for a relaxing hangout!

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Kenoah Kovara
text: The place has a mix of warm-toned indoor spaces with a spacious outdoor room, making the atmosphere great and flexible. I got the espresso with the southern cross modification, making my coffee sour with a strong hit, a great add-on that not many cafes have. However, I have to give this place 4 stars due to the abysmal experience using the wifi, it is probably by far the most unstable wifi I have ever used in a cafe in Jakarta, so just keep that in mind before you go here. (I got disconnected from the wifi 2 times while writing this review)

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Billy Thanwijaya
text: Very nice and spacious cafe. The indoor is well conditioned, and they have outdoor seats too. The cocoa and caramel macchiato is very good. the caramel and chocolate is very highlighted. the matcha latte is just ok.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Anthony Dicky Rustan
text:
Kopi Titik Koma is a gem for coffee lovers and those who enjoy aesthetic spaces. The cafe is designed with a clean, modern, and cozy vibe, making it a perfect spot to relax or catch up with friends. Its thoughtful interior and minimalist touches also create excellent opportunities for photography—so if you’re into snapping Instagram-worthy moments, this is definitely a place to visit.

The coffee here is top-notch, with a variety of selections from espresso-based drinks to local brews. Whether you’re into a strong Americano or a creamy latte, their coffee consistently delivers great flavor and aroma. For non-coffee drinkers, the menu also offers alternative beverages, including refreshing teas and cold drinks.

One of the standout features is the spacious layout. You don’t feel cramped, and the ample seating areas—both indoors and outdoors—make it suitable for working or studying for hours. Whether you want a quiet corner or a lively table with friends, there’s plenty of space to accommodate different moods and purposes.

Overall, Kopi Titik Koma excels with its combination of great coffee, aesthetic ambiance, and generous space. It’s a place where you can unwind, work, or hang out comfortably, all while enjoying high-quality drinks. Highly recommended!

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: Tiffany Karen
text: best matcha

title: Matcha Bae PIK
name: I Gusti Ngurah Tata Adityawarnam
text: Great matcha options and great atmosphere

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Angela Agusta
text:
Kopi Titik Koma in Tanjung Duren is a fantastic coffee shop that truly has it all. The staff are incredibly friendly and welcoming, making you feel right at home from the moment you walk in.

The coffee is great, with rich and satisfying flavors, but what really sets this place apart is the huge selection of matcha for non-coffee drinkers. The interior is superb, offering a spacious and comfortable setting that's perfect for working from a cafe (WFC).

It's not noisy, allowing you to focus without distractions. Whether you're here to work, relax, or catch up with friends, Kopi Titik Koma offers a superb, peaceful, and stylish environment.

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Clara Tan
text:
Good coffee, try iced shaken, strong and fruity one. Good matcha, too. Try iced matcha zen, since they say its less sugar.
Spacious place, indoor and outdoor. One of the cafe you can choose if you want hangout with friends, work from cafe, and even when you want a ‘me time’ with your coffee. Nice ambience.
Too bad, for the snacks, as far i can see, they only have pastry and ubi (sweet potato) brûlée.
Pretty sure i will come again next time. Since it’s already my second visit, before they re-open and renovate their indoor area.
Gracias ~

title: Titik Koma Tanjung Duren
name: Hadi Hemanda
text:
You should manage your parking staff better. They were impolite and quite rude.

I don’t mind leaving my car key since my car was parked in parallel and could disturb other vehicles. However, I also have the right to make sure the staff are able to drive my car (it’s an electric car with a side joystick for the transmission).

The staff said it was mandatory to leave the car key. Later, I found out this is not mandatory, so it seemed like he was trying to force customers to use his valet service.

The only downside is that it's more of a spot for snacking—mainly coffee and pastries—so don’t expect full meals here. But if you’re looking for a relaxing place to enjoy a good cup of coffee and some sweet treats, it’s definitely worth a visit! ✨

name: risa rinadiputri
text:
I've been here 3 times, different menu each time.
What I recommend is anything with their karaage! The chicken pieces are huge, so juicy, crispy tender, spiced wonderfully!
The flavored iced tea is my go to to cut the richness of the main dishes, tangy and fresh, good serving portions too.
Tasted their gyudon  and mentai spaghetti and it was decent, but not as memorable as their chicken karaage.

For the dessert I've only tried their chia pudding and it was good! Not too sweet (the #1 asian compliment)
The resto itself quite small, but very nice ambience and impeccable choices of japanese songs!
Will definitely come back again

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Rui
text:
Let's start from the top, atmosphere, place, music. All of them in my opinion are really lively, give off really good vibes and all.

Foods? The wafu pasta and salmon mentai are all very good. Tastewise, it isn't too over the top, and the portions the chicken and salmon comes in isn't too selfish too.

Now for the main star of the show, the coffee. I took the Bourbon coffee, and tell you what, it smells lovely. It also tastes wonderful, for the price you're paying it's nice.

Overall 100/10, would come again if given the budget and time to.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Mikaela Anderson
text: Tried their matcha latte & really liked it! The staff was all wholesome and friendly. Came Friday afternoon and didn’t get parking at the Cafe but managed to park not too far. A great place for both WFC (wifi provided on each floor) and a hangout with friends/family.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Jeffrey Alvin
text:
A hidden gem! Surprisingly great modern Japanese cuisine. Definitely outperform flashy restaurants like Okinawa.

Mentaiko pasta very tasty I love it. 10/10
The creamy rice is around 6/10
Truffle pasta 9/10
Gyudon 9/10
They serve high quality sencha tea too.

Decor is alright, first floor a Japanese small coffee shop style while second floor is a basic restaurant with a little creative design.

I'll be back for sure.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Tia Handimuljana
text:
Good place for chit chat, be alone, and or work. Warm ambience. Rather busy during lunch time. They accept group reservations, too.
When you come alone, do mind to take 2-seat table, so they can use it for group booking and you won't get moved while you are dining-in.
Good wifi and has a power plug in almost of the tables.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Joycerlyn Chancellor
text:
Very niche Japanese cafe concept and somewhat hidden gem-like.

I had Genmaicha Latte, Chicken Nanban, and Chia Pudding. All of these are very good, drinks and desserts here have very well controlled sweetness, you can request for less sugar for the lattes and the chia pudding is so lovely, perhaps the healthiest pudding with interesting texture components I’ve ever put in my mouth. Chicken Nanban is also great, doesn’t taste like frozen karage to me and the pieces are quite big considering it’s a snack portion of three pieces.

As for the service, staffs are very helpful in guiding our preferences and the cafe ambience is very suitable for chill cafe hangouts or WFC. Toilet is super clean too and this place even offers weekly catering meals for customers. Price point for some mains might be a bit high, but considering how niche this type of Japanese cafe concept is in Jakarta, this is very recommended and I will totally come back again to try more items.

title: Morning Blues
name: Carolin Li
text:
A small contemporary cafe in Royal Mediterania Apartment that sells small varieties of meals, snacks, and drinks. Looked and felt like a casual neighborhood cafe where those who live in the apartment complex love to come hang out in. There's an indoor and (modest) outdoor seating. The inside is air-conditioned quite well. When I came in, the staff greeted me and kindly notified the menu available for today.

I paid for one of the pastries displayed on the small cashier rack alongside a coffee latte. The latte tasted light, gentle and smooth. It was more of a velvety milk experience for me than it was an indulgent, fragrant coffee latte, but it's still okay. Gave me the caffeine kick I still needed. The pastry I ordered was a delicate sausage bread. Fresh, flaky, savoury. Quite fulfilling as a snack.

Service was proper and friendly. The ambience was something I loved from the cafe. It was quite crowded at the moment I came but it was still comfortable, cozy and homey. I imagine if I lived in the neighborhood, I'd probably frequent this cafe for a snack grab before going to work 👍😌

I really appreciate the actions from the staff came directly to my table to apologize and offered if I still want to proceed my request. Thank you very much for the kind gesture 🙏🏼

I will surely visit again this place for WFC since I Iive quite near.

name: Anggraeni Setiawan
text:
A hidden gem near Central Park — cozy coffee spot with comforting food options. I especially love their mendoan and Javanese-style noodles — rich, flavorful, and satisfying.

This time, I tried one of their coffee mocktails: the Peachretto — a refreshing blend of coffee, subtle citrus notes, and a touch of soda. Light, bubbly, and surprisingly addictive!

Sunday visit to try their Iftar menu - Soto Betawi Oseng, must try!

title: Morning Blues
name: Calista Lianardi
text: The atmosphere is so nice for wfc, and good choice of song also! Fav are matcha latte and mendoan, but the fried rice and rice bowl are also great! Sittin here almost everyday

title: Morning Blues
name: Eline Angeline
text: I visited Cafe Morning Blue to work from the cafe, and it turned out to be a great choice. The ambiance is really pleasant, cozy, and perfect for focusing on tasks while still enjoying the atmosphere. The seating is comfortable, and the vibe feels welcoming whether you’re there to work or just relax. Definitely a spot I’d recommend for anyone looking for a productive yet relaxing cafe experience.

title: Morning Blues
name: Doctor Neuron
text:
I stumbled upon this café while searching for a place that is WFC-friendly on Sunday.

It is in my walking distance, so I give it a go
First, I like the small yet cozy ambiance inside. Each table provides a sufficient amount of charging plugs (2 for each).

Floors are flat enough and my table was able to stay as it is.

I ordered their iced matcha latte and for me.. it is pretty okay? The matcha was not that bitter.. more on a sweeter side of drink that you commonly found on streets.
2 hours passed and I decided to order a plate of Madam Zhang fried rice. This fried rice was more on the kampung fried rice (less soy sauce) with smoked beef inside and fried chicken skin as side. I have no words. This is solid! Savory, check! Crispy skin, check! Light yet umami, check!

Overall, this place might not be on your daily list due to the price, yet feel free to try because as people said, you get what did you pay.

title: Morning Blues
name: Yudhistira Mardi
text:
After heard this place from TikTok, decided to give this coffeeshop a visit while I looked for some place to finish my works in CP area - as this coffeeshop happened to open until 23.00.

It's kinda easy to find as it's located nearby the apartment entrance. I can say, the indoor is quite nice with minimalistic style and lots of windows - these should provide great lighting and ambience during daytime.

This coffeeshop is also quite suitable for WFC setup - lots of power sockets, like almost all tables have their own dedicated socket. The wifi access is also quite fast - have no problem with it.

Ordered Iced Black Lemonade and it tastes great - quite refreshing, not too sweet and it has some fizz - especially under hot weather. The lemonade really complement the flavor of their espresso - kinda fruity with some acidity.

Would love to give this coffeeshop another visit and explore their other drink variants ❤️

title: Morning Blues
name: Gerald Jose Hendryanto
text: Morning Blues offers a cozy atmosphere, but nothing particularly remarkable—placing it on the lower end of the 5-star spectrum. It’s a decent spot to work, but not a standout. I ordered a manual brew coffee with infused local beans, and unfortunately, it was quite disappointing, lacking the depth and quality I expected. Additionally, the staff were present to serve but didn’t seem to have any in-depth knowledge and hospitality about the coffee they were preparing. Overall, it was an average experience—good enough for a visit, but nothing compelling to return for, except for its strategic location.

title: Morning Blues
name: Hanna
text: Nice coffee shop in Royal Mediterania Apartment. Cozy and comfort for coffee, meeting, breakfast, or brunch..

title: Morning Blues
name: Ernest Widi
text:
Nice and cozy ambience. The cafe is comfortable and well lit. I ordered Hainan chicken rice and it tastes good. The coffee was mid tier and nothing special. The staff is welcoming and friendly. Unfortunately the wifi's speed is a bit unstable during my visit, so I would not recommend coming here if you need a stable internet speed.

Will definitely come back here just for the vibes, or if I want to finish some light work.

title: Morning Blues

text:
"This café isn’t just a place to drink coffee—it’s a whole vibe. Every corner tells a story."

Drink is good taste n good price

title: Morning Blues
name: Febri Fristian
text:
This coffee shop is a bit new, the place is in Royal Mediteranian Residence Apartment in West Jakarta, close to the Central Park Mall, Neo Soho Mall and Pullman Hotel. So if you want to go inside, you need to go to the Royal Mediteranian Residence Area first.
Cartenz Coffee and Kitchen serve you both for beverages and foods. You can order the menu from the cashier place and you can ask them about the informations about your food or beverages. If i am not wrong they serve both light and heavy meal.
For the beverages, they serve coffee and non-coffee. For the coffee they only have a few options for manual brew because they are more focusing on the milk-based and espresso-based coffee. And if you can not drink coffee, you can order the tea or other beverages, i saw they have a good quality of tea on the cashier place. You can buy their cookies as well to eat with the coffee, it tastes good.
They have 2 area, indoor and outdoor. The indoor site have some tables and chairs, so you can sit there with some of your friends. But for the outdoor, they only have 2 tables with 4 high chairs for each tables, and you can smoke at the outside area.
The price is not too expensive but not cheap as well. For a cup of brew coffee, you need to pay around IDR 37,000. The place is so cozy, even when you just want to enjoy your time alone.

title: Morning Blues

text: This place open up to 11 pm. The food and drinks are quite variables. There are many options of sweet and savory. This place also sell some kind of spirits. Good place to hangout.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Pey
text:
Was not expecting that this place is really this good and, quite frankly, everything here amazes me. The food, the ambience, the service, all is 10/10.

This cafe offers healthy foods but still served with such flavor. The Truffle Kinoko Pasta, for me, tastes so unique yet good. The Chicken Cream Stew also tastes great, the chicken itself is tender and chewy. For the coffee, I ordered Kogume (Kopi Gula Merah), too sweet for me but still favorable.

I also love the ambience in this place, the room was actually not too big but they still manage to make it pleasant. The waitress also sterilize the previously occupied seats. Great job👌🏻

text:
i love the concept of the cafe here, they specialize in japanese coffee, tea and matcha. i see they provide many ways of serving their coffee its really interesting.

me and my family ordered their magic coffee, cappuccino and ice matcha latte. their coffee is pretty strong according to my mom but taste fine to me.

their bagel kaya sandwich was HEAVEN i love their bagel, its more of the crispy skin bagel rather than the usual smooth and chewy. but i love it 😋 my mom also order their shokupan kaya toast the bread is very soft and milky!

will be coming back here cause their menu is very large !

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe

text: Such a nice place to dine in. It's a bit crowded during lunch time but the service still fast and good. The Caesar Salad is fresh and the chicken is juicy, the Mentaiko Pasta is creamy, the Truffle Gyudon and Shirataki Yakisoba taste very delicious. I will be comeback again for sure :D

First timer and a happy customer. The food was great, love it. And for the coffee, tried a Lintong Nihuta ice Japanese coffee, love it, it's bold and smooth with a dark chocolate aftertaste. I was recommended by my friend who lives in Gading, and this time I recommend it to you guys! Enjoy!
Btw, sorry for the photos are not that fancy, I was hungry and in a bit hectic with work when took them. Haha.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Josh Allen
text: This quaint coffee shop is great for anyone seeking a cozy environment and a relaxing coffee. It is well decorated, has friendly staff and is a great place to socialize. I’ve had the waffles which are really good (could use a little more syrup), the Wild Berry Kombucha is just right, some have too much sugar, but not this one, and I’ve tried their Vietnamese coffee. While it is tasty, it should be served the local way. Vietnamese style coffee is when they put sweet milk in the bottom of the cup, and serve you the coffee while it drips from the “phin” on top. Ice is always served in a side bowl with tongs so you can add your own amount. Overall, I love this place and will come regularly.

Our americano coffee was awesome. Salmon teriyaki don and gyudon were tasty and not overpowering with sauce like many other places. Will be back! title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Victor Oscar
text:
A Perfect Blend of Taste and Ambiance

I recently had the pleasure of dining at this Japanese restaurant and was thoroughly impressed. The atmosphere was incredibly inviting and created the perfect backdrop for a relaxing meal.

I opted for the Melonsekai and Hanamichi drinks, and I must say, they were absolutely divine! The unique combination of coffee with subtle melon and rose flavors was a delightful surprise. These drinks are a must-try for any coffee lover looking to explore new taste sensations.

I'm already looking forward to returning to savor these exceptional drinks again.

good place to hangout & chill with group. the seating is very limited, so I assume it’ll be very noisy if the it is full.

Kohicha coffee: good taste, but very light (3/5)
Fried mantou: just an ordinary chinese bun with condensed milk (3/5)
Karage curry rice: tasted great, its karage was very crispy, but doesn’t have any option for spicyness, they provide chilli powder but it didn’t give any spice at all (3/5)

During my visit, i tried their stir fried shirataki noodle (made from sweet potato), chicken teriyaki rice with tea. The food taste is good. Final result, a worthy place for ur coffee or meal.

Very recommended 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe

text: Perfect spot to chill with a friend for less-rowdy meetups. The ambience is great; the decor being suitably calm and peaceful. The coffee is highly recommended with a wide selection of manual brews, and the tea equally varied and well-made as well. They have good food, both appetizers and main courses, from pasta to meat. Overall a great experience and a new favorite spot to hang around in!

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe

text: Keep coming back for the creamy chicken stew—a true definition of comfort food 🥰 they have a variety of tea options as well, tried a few of em and they’re all great 👍🏼

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: The Duchess of Culinary
text:
Kohicha Cafe is definitely a hidden gem as the location is behind the main street. They have wide parking space and it is free as well 😁
This is a paradise for coffee and tea lovers. From the first time we entered this Japanese cafe, we will smell the fragrant coffee. I love the aromatic smell. They provide plenty variant of coffee, tea, drinks, healthy foods, snacks, main dishes, and desserts.

For a coffee and tea lovers, you will need more time to choose which coffee or tea that you will have just like me 😄

Kohicha Cafe has two floors where the second floor is wider. They follow the health protocols where both of the staffs and customers have to wear mask, there's distance for each table and chair.

Here are what I tried :

Kara Miso Gyudon (IDR 65,000)
A bowl of white rice served with braised sliced beef in spicy miso sauce, onions, crunchy tempura flakes, and seaweed. The beef is tender and combined well with the sauce that isn't too spicy.

Manual Brew - V60 Dripper (IDR 35,000)
Besides black, dairy, and flavored coffee, what I like from Kohicha is they provide the choices for manual brew with different techniques. Although I didn't see the process directly because I was in the second floor (they made the coffee in the first floor), but I followed their recommendation to try V60 Dripper. This is a modern Japanese style pour-over technique. The coffee isn't that strong and bitter.

title: Kohicha Japanese Cafe
name: Michelle Ruth Natalie
text: Great coffee blends & combinations, nice chill Japanese ambience 😍

title: Tyfel Coffee
name: Foodienisme
text:
Finally, after a long time since this café moved locations, I was able to come here again. I came here for iftar with my friends, and we got a Ramadan package promotion which includes pasta and ice tea for only 50k/person.

The main menu variants are still the same as in the old location, but there are some additional menu items such as rice boxes and burgers.

The taste, quality, and ambiance are still the same. This is one of the vegan cafes with delicious and varied food menus.

The place is quiet and suitable for those who want to have some me-time or chat in a less crowded place.

title: Tyfel Coffee
name: Nadya
text:
This is one of my favorite vegetarian coffee shop. Ths place is easy to find, right next to bebek omahan. Such a nice and comfy place with homey touch. The ambience is great with relaxing music. This cafe is not too spacious, but it's clean and neat. It has 2nd floor for those who want smoking area. The staff are good, friendly, and nice. The service is fast.

They serve vegan/vegetarian foods and beverages with reasonable price. The food is great with generous portion. Ordered their fettuccine aglio olio, it served with meat substitute. the pasta is a lil bit oily but the taste is delicious. The coffee is surprisingly good! They also serve some kind of tea and vibrant-looking drink. You can make reservation via eatigo and get 50% discount.

title: Tyfel Coffee
name: Febri Fristian
text:
This coffee shop looks normal from the outside but fancy inside, really good to have a coffee time here. They have quite big parking space for car and motocycle. The staffs and barista really friendly and polite, they will greet you immidiatelly when you enter the coffee shop. They have a good coffee beans and also good technique for manual brewing. I tried their Toraja beans and also Sunda beans, they will ask you and explain about the coffee characteristic before you decide which coffee beans suits you better. And for the price, really affordable.
They have 2 floor but only can order in the first floor. And in the second floor, they split it into 2 kind of room which is smoking outside area and non-smoking inside room. They have a lot of seats for both floors. For the smoking area, they put fans there. I have to say they have a good decoration for the interior, really good to be there for a group hangout. And they receive payment using cash and minimum 50,000 for debit card.
