Peak cafe, keep it up dude, istg Imma second the person who said this is the best cafe in Kalisari bcs they're spittin FACTS 🥹🥹

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Dhini Oktavianti
text:
Honestly, this place feels like a hidden gem — not the kind you easily spot from the main road. It’s more like “yang tau, tau aja” kind of spot. But once you arrive, first impression langsung dapet banget. The security (yang juga handle parkiran) super organized. Even with limited space, they manage to fit quite a lot of motorbikes and around 4–6 cars neatly. Big plus.

Aku udah dua kali ke sini — first for brunch arisan, second time for buka bersama sama sahabat SMA. And both visits? Consistently good.

For brunch, I ordered cappuccino and cheesecake. And honestly, chef’s kiss. The cappuccino suits my taste perfectly — smooth, not too acidic, not too bold. Cheesecakenya juga light tapi tetap rich.

Second visit, I played safe — cappuccino & cheesecake again (because why not 😄). Since it was bukber, they served complimentary kurma and warm tea, which felt thoughtful. Teman-temanku order cuanki, and surprisingly… that became the star of the table. Comforting banget, apalagi pas buka puasa.

Salad Kelapa Muda juga highlight banget untuk dipesan di Kana.

One thing that stands out here: ada abaya store inside the cafe. You can browse and shop while hanging out — such a unique touch. Though pas bukber kemarin, tokonya udah tutup.

Another highlight? The mushola. Proper banget. Even though the wudhu area is outside, it’s spacious, clean, wangi, and mukena-nya neatly prepared. This kind of detail really matters.

For WFA? Highly recommended. Banyak colokan, ambience-nya super nyaman — not too noisy, not too quiet. Perfect balance.

And the focal points — the entrance is cuakep banget, very aesthetic. Plus the fish pond adds a calming vibe. Oh, and there’s literally a motor displayed in the middle of the cafe, which somehow works and becomes part of Kana’s identity.

Final thought:
Kana is not just a coffee place, it’s a whole experience. Cozy, thoughtful, and a little bit unexpected. Definitely a go-to spot when you want to chill, work, or catch up with friends.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Haura Liew
text:
We stumbled upon this coffee shop while walking around the Menteng area. From the outside, the coffee shop has a rustic, industrial feel, simple, raw & understated with a calm neighbourhood vibe. It doesn’t look flashy but it feels warm and inviting, very much like a hidden gem for coffee lovers.

Overall, it was a nice experience. The coffee was good. We ordered the sparkling americano, which was refreshing and enjoyable, especially in the heat. For snacks, we tried the fried banana, basically pisang goreng but with a more refined, cafe-style touch.

Price-wise, it is slightly on the pricier side compared to many other cafe around Jakarta, but still reasonable for the quality and experience.

Overall, a pleasant stop for good coffee in Menteng.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Mela Dewinta
text:
Giyanti Coffee is one of my all-time favorite coffee shops. I’ve been coming here for more than 10 years, and it’s still the best coffee in town for me. It’s not just a coffee shop, but also a coffee roastery, and you can really tell they take their coffee seriously.

When it comes to the coffee, I’d say it’s one of the best in Jakarta, and honestly, even among the best in Indonesia so far. You can choose between light, medium, or strong roasts. Personally, I always go for medium, it’s not too strong, but the balance is just perfect. The espresso base usually has a nutty character, which I absolutely love.

I’ve tried a lot of their menu, but I mostly order white coffee like latte, cappuccino, and especially flat white. Flat white is my favorite. They also have magic, but flat white is the one that hits the spot for me every time.

The place itself is comfortable, but it’s not really WFC-friendly. This is more of a place for sipping coffee, relaxing, and having conversations with friends. There are two areas: indoor and outdoor. The outdoor area is for smokers, and the indoor area has three floors. The reason it’s not great for working is because many of the chairs aren’t very ergonomic for long sitting, some are too high, some just aren’t that comfortable. There also aren’t many power outlets. But honestly, that makes sense, because this place isn’t meant for working, it’s meant for enjoying good coffee.

The food is good, the drinks are consistently great, but yes, it’s on the pricey side. Still, it’s totally worth it. I usually come here as a kind of self-reward, not all the time, but when I really want a great cup of coffee, this is where I go.

It’s located on Jalan Surabaya, a very quiet, calm, and peaceful area, which adds to the overall experience. For me, Giyanti Coffee will always be my favorite coffee shop in Jakarta ☕✨

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Dian O
text:
My coffee sanctuary, hands down best coffee in Jakarta.
We frequently came here since 2013 and personally got introduced to the owner who then offered us his help to our social movement. A man with a big heart.
But of course, the coffee is the talk of the town, my benchmark when comparing with hundreds others emerging in the city.
Food is great, too.
Love this place to my bones.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Arya Nugraha
text: tried their iced cappuccino and es pasar antik. both were seriously so good. i asked for the cappuccino to be strong, and it delivered without going bitter. the es pasar antik was a pleasant surprise too: thick, creamy, sweet, but you can still clearly taste the coffee beneath it. the place has a serene vibe that makes conversations easy, and the service was genuinely warm and attentive. a spot worth coming back to for both the drinks and the atmosphere.

And they serve some croissants, croffles and pastries too.
Just order Piccolo with Carrot cake, it really matches nicely.
And I also ordered some freshly ground beans too.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Art Art4
text:
I visited a coffee shop in Cikini that’s located in an old house turned into a café, and I really liked the place. It has a warm and cozy vibe, and even though it looked full, it didn’t feel too crowded—even on the weekend.

I tried the café latte and it was really good, and the cake was tasty too with a reasonable price. The toilet was clean and well-maintained, which I really appreciate.

What impressed me the most was the service. At first, I thought they didn’t have a cashier counter, but actually they provide full table service, which felt much better than most cafés I’ve visited. They even provide a remote control on each table, which is a unique touch.

They also have both indoor and outdoor seating options. Overall, I’d definitely recommend this café for anyone looking for a comfortable place to enjoy good coffee and cake.

The coffee itself is top-notch — rich, smooth, and expertly brewed. They clearly know their craft, and the staff is super friendly and passionate about what they do. Plus, the presentation is on point, making every cup feel like a little work of art.

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Disty Disya
text:
A hidden gem in Pasar Rebo area.
Pernah masuk di timeline IG. Pas banget ada perlu ke daerah sini jadi sekalian deh jadi tempat meeting point.

Overall cafenya bagus. No music. Ini penting buat WFC. Suara natural aja dari gemericik air. Cafenya sendiri cukup luas dengan meja yang nyaman untuk kerja dan meeting.

Ruangannya terbagi menjadi 3 area, area AC dekat coffee station (meja sangat terbatas), area yang lebih luas yang AC-nya nyala klo sudah mulai panas. Kebetulan sampai lokasi jam9, tanpa AC kondisinya masih aman bgt karena rada semi terbuka, dan area smoking diatas.

Kamar kecil bersih dan wangi. Terpisah cewe n cowo. Musholla juga ada. Laff banget. Muat cukup banyak orang.

Parkiran juga muat banyak mobil dan motor.

Sekedar input untuk variasi makanan. Dimenu kebanyakan masakannya digoreng. Nasi goreng, mie goreng, dory goreng sambal matah. Jadi pilihannya “healthy food” terbatas. Sekedar yang non fried kayak dimsum, soto, etc. Tadi satu2nya menu berkuah, soto betawi gak tersedia.
Harga masih masuk akal. Tadi beli ikan dory samba matah. Enakkk! Alhamdulillah.

Semoga nanti bisa kembali kesini lagi

title: KANA Coffee & Space

text:

Kalau dari segi makanan, agak kurang cocok dengan red velvet cake. Kurang cocok dengan kolaborasi cake dan krimnya. Cakenya agak terlalu manis.

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: dl dwilestari
text:
1. FIRST BGT YA... kayaknya akan jd cafe favku buat WFC di Kalisari/Pekayon.
2. Seat capacity banyaaaak. 2 lantai ada outdoornya jg banyak.
3. Beannya blend sepertinya. Aman aja enak. Asemnya masih berasa.
4. Makanannya sedep², pesan nasgor teri enak, tp mungkin akan oke kalo lebih empuk sedikit nasinya. Pisang gorengnya enak, matang pisangnya pas. Sepertinya sedep² ya, meja samping pesen soto betawi harum bgt.
5. Cocok buat wfc krn musicnya instrument doang dan gak kenceng. Sukaaaa. Vibe indoor depan kasirnya kaya sbux. Seneng bisa nemuin yg mirip vibenya selama boikot.
6. Mushollanya GEDE BANGET, dingin, bersih, nyaman banget, banget.
7. Onderan cepet dtngnya.
8. STAFF OKE BGT. Ramah, helpfull, orderan kita dicatet dgn bener. Krn aku pesen makan buat 1 jam yg akan dtng, dinconfirm dong ke kita, catetannya manteb.
9. Lt 2 ada toko gamis syari. Nyaman bgt dlmnya.
10. TOILETNYA BERSIH BGT, semoga konsumennya behave jg ya. Baru kali ini nemuin toilet kafe yg nyediain plastik buat sampah pembalut. Mau nangis.

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Nyoman Prayoga
text:
Kesan pertama saat mampir ke sini, suasananya tenang dan bikin betah. Pilihan minumannya beragam dari kopi, mocktails, sampai minuman non-kopi juga banyak. Variasi makanan juga tersedia mulai dari snack ringan, dessert, sampai hidangan berat.

Mungkin karena saya datang di hari kerja, suasananya cukup hening. Beberapa pengunjung tampak fokus dengan laptopnya masing-masing. Sepertinya banyak yg work from away atau WFA. Seingat saya tidak ada musik yang mengalun, hanya suara gemericik air menemani dari kolam koi di tengah ruangan. Elemen alam seperti tanaman pot hingga pohon di dekat kolam makin menambah kesan teduh.

Yang menarik, ada dua motor dekoratif di dalam ruang kafe, menambah aksen unik dan kontras dengan nuansa tenangnya.

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Citta
text:
Okay, here’s the review:

1. Dateng di jam siang suasananya masih okee, tapi kalo udah sore jam 3 ke atas udah mulai rame
2. Ruangan yg pertama kali masuk itu kurang berasa AC nya, kayaknya karena semi-outdoor gitu yaa. Kalo ruangan dalemnya yg jadi satu sama kasir enak berasa dan walaupun gak terlalu besar tapi cukup nyaman cuman akan agak sering orang keluar masuk aja
3. Tujuan awal kesini buat laptopan dan sangat senang ada colokan dimana2 wkwk + wifinyaa lancar jaya
4. Toilet gak terlalu besar tapii tetep oke. Sangat thoughtfull ada sabun (bare minimum tpi kadang di tempat lain suka ga ada), wewangiannya dan plastik item (mungkin buat ciwi2 bisa bungkus pembalut)
5. Ada musholla yangg nyamannn dan disediain mukena!! Langsung liat fotonya aja deh wkwk (disini colokan pun melimpah ya:D). Cuman ada notes untuk tempat wudhunya sangat kecil, gak ada penutupnya, dan gak ada tempat buat naro barang:( semoga bisa diperbaiki
6. Last but not least, aku mesen nasi goreng kana sama lemon tea. Lemon tea nya enak tapi buat nasgornya agak kurang menurutku, entah kurangnya dimana (🙏🏻) tapi seingetku rasanya terlalu asin. Semoga bisa improve lagi yaa.

Sepertinya will come back buat wfc-an dan masih penasaran mauu nyobain menu westernnya yang terlihat tempting😆

Pas dateng masih sepi paling ada bbrp orang aja dan kebetulan dapet tempat dibawah AC jadi dingin bgt. Cocok untuk wfa/wfc. Untuk harga ya So so lahhh yaaa. Rasa juga enakkk untuk menu yg ku pesen. Sebagai ibu2 yg punya toodler kekurangan tempat ini ada mini playground hahahha tp ya mungkin ini dibuat untuk WFA or nongkrong friendly kali yaa bukan family friendly. tapi TOPP salad kelapanya, denger denger owner sebelum bangun cafe ini, emang fokus jualan salad kelapa yaa? enakkk seger gurihg mantapppp mau bungkus sebaskom kalo bisa

jadi overall untuk kenyaman maxx mentok 5, untuk makanan jujurrrr sedih ga seenak itu :( keep it up ke dapur plss! biar jadi makin enakkk

text:

Makanan dan minumannya enak, tapi pesen Chicken OG mushroom gaada mushroomnya sama sekali 😁 Untuk servicenya bisa ditingkatin lagi ya. Mungkin karena kurang orang jadinya meja yang udah selesai dengan sisa2 makanannya ga langsung diberesin. Terhitung cukup lama sih, bahkan udah ada customer baru itu meja masih penuh sama piring dan gelas kotor.

Notes juga kalau hari Jumat siang ke sini belum bisa order makanan karena cook nya semua cowo dan sholat Jumat. Tapi staff nya ramah kokk.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Chlotus
text: This cafe is absolutely lovely! There are so many cozy seats in different rooms. We picked a spot in the room where they make the drinks, so we could enjoy the delightful aroma of the coffee. It was a perfect choice!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Jerry Choo
text: Nice ambience,  decorations. Food is good (triple egg Hot & Spicy), pastries so so (canele, Portuguese egg tart). Coffee is great. Although the price is high, but will come back again.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Puji Fetika
text:
Paid 130k for 2 plastic cups of ordinary coffee. When I asked why they don't serve  our coffee in the glass, they gave hilarious answer that "we run out of glass" and only said casual sorry without any regret. The cafe didn't look that busy so they run out of glass like they said. I mean it's basic decency to serve in a glass when we choose to dine in.

I didn't expect to paid over 100k only to be served by cheap plastic cup. The cofffe also not so real good, just basic. The service quite good, but still serving overpriced coffee with plastic cup is embarassing. If you wanna have a basic coffee with plastic cup just buy from someone on the road.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Agnescia Wiranto
text:
Good taste of coffee.

Definitely will back again. The barista is super friendly and helpful.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery

text: My first time here. I like the hot magic coffee.  Besides coffee, they serve some food like pasta, egg etc.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Editha Banjarnahor
text: Great spot cafe, the ambience was so good for me. The cakes really really love it, especially the Lemon cheese cake. The combination sweet sour, make me wanna come again.

Always gravitating back to Giyanti’s.
Definitely the best well balanced, smooth and strong coffee’s in Jakarta
Owner: very knowledgable and personable.
Server: Umum and all the staff: attentive and hospitable

5/5
Coffee: Flores, Hot Americano, strong , smooth and very slight acid. Notes: chocolate and nutty.
Delicious

5/5:
3 x spicy sunny side eggs serves with toasty sour dough bread . Very good combo

Below: 5/5
Coffee:
Blue Batak +Aceh Saman blend: Medium, Machine, Long Black(Strong), caramel crema, Notes: sweet, smoky and citrus. Slight acid. Smooth throughout. My new favorite

Blue Batak +Aceh Saman: Espresso(same as above), more acid and creamier.

Blue Batak only: Expresso, creamy, higher acid , slight smoky and sweet.

1/5
Dessert
Tiramisu Cake. Needs work

___________________________________________
Older review :
Exceptional and friendly staff
Great coffee with little to no sourness.
Only One selection of Single origin coffee to chose from, but a variety of beans can be purchased. Coffee served each day may differ, depending on availability. Served “Strong, Medium and light”. Depends on your preference.

Aceh Saman Meriah (Aceh)
My favorite. Medium dark, machine. Hot Americano . Rich chocolate and earthy notes with a slight bitterness. Smooth taste throughout

Siraja Biak-Biak (Sumut)
Medium/Dark. Light sweet and chocolate notes. No bitter, but very smooth.

Sourdough with garlic butter: ask for extra crisp. Delicious

Carrot cake homemade: light , low sugar and very addicting

Beautiful whimsical interior. The buildings interior is a work of art. Lots of natural lighting throughout.  Rooms are Separated in four sections: 3 x non smoking and 1 x smoking area. The smoking area does not affect the non areas, because the doors are well insulated.

Lovely place for dates, works and social gatherings

If this review was helpful, please give it a 👍

Happy Travels

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Simon
text: Very nice coffee and delicious food. It's charm comes from it's unique architectural design.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Rumi & Bambam the fun club
text:
Here for the coffee only, not the service.

Found a strand of hair on my kid’s hot chocolate.

Furthermore, they made me settle upfront like a Starbucks whereas other tables did not get the same treatment.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Robith Hadhromi
text: Good ambience and drinks. However it is not proper when you want to work here. The place not spacious and there are no lot of electricity slots. Nevertheless, this place is great for hanging out or catching up with friends.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Riski Riyan Insani
text: On my recent visit to Giyanti Coffee Roastery in Menteng, I tried their Cinnamon Latte — an aromatic, flavorful twist on a classic favorite. The Cinnamon Latte at Giyanti is refined yet inviting—a perfect blend of espresso, milk, and spice.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Syafira Permata
text:
Went here on a afternoon Sunday with my mom. It was a quite afternoon. I had an iced latter and sourdough banana bread, and hot mocha for my mom

Not my first time being here but their service is still consistently good

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Kartika Mayang
text:
A very aesthetic and comfortable coffee shop.

I ordered flat white (hot). We can choose the coffee type (light, medium, or strong). I chose the medium one and it's good.

They also have a lot of kind pastry and food.

The wifi is okay too.

The price is a little bit pricey, 50k+ (after tax) for a cup of flat white.

Fyi, they have priority seats available! A very nice service!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Andil Ong
text:
The Good:
- Very comfortable and unique atmosphere and vibes
- It’s like every corner of the coffee shop is a secret hideout
- Not hot even outdoors because of ventilation
- Coffee is pretty good, croissant is just ok
- Fast internet
- Kinda pricey
- The background music is pretty chill. Like a hippie but not distracting. It’s there when you focus on it, but then it fades into the background when you do something else

The Bad:
- Only 1 toilet for the whole place
- It looks old and outdated, like an old home.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Lina C
text: Been here four times within the last 7 weeks or so! The coffee is great - the aroma and taste blends really well. Each time I order the mocha, it’s consistently rich and never too sweet. The pastry here is really fluffy and flakey. Service is great as well, the staff are very accommodating and one of the guy speaks really good English, too! I love the atmosphere of this place, and if you sit inside, the smell of coffee just hits the spot on a weekend brunch day.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Winda
text: Giyanti Roastery is one of those legendary coffee spots in Central Jakarta that lives up to the hype. The place is a bit cramped, but that just shows how good the coffee is — people keep coming back for a reason. I had a canelé and a latte, and both were spot on. The latte was smooth and comforting, and the canelé had that perfect crisp edge with a soft, custardy center. Cozy, a little chaotic, but totally worth it.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Rakesh Inbaraj
text:
The coffee here is truly on point, which explains why this café is so popular. The space has a charming rustic vibe with plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. While the food and pastries were fairly average, the coffee more than makes up for it—even if prices are a bit on the higher side. They also sell their own beans, and I picked up a few kilos to take back home to India.

The Magik and Piccolo seem to be crowd favorites—and for good reason

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Benito Rama
text: Still one of the best places that serve good quality (best i think) coffee in Jakarta. The vibes and ambiences of Giyanti was out of the world, with various choices of coffee, non coffee beverages, and several (also delicious) pastries. You can also pick if you want your coffee light, medium, or strong (i recommend you to pick the strong one), and a bit of extra touch to the latte, mocha, and cappucino made this place a go-to place to grab a coffee!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery

text: Very good coffe, i like the light cappucino, also the vintage vibes, comfy place for being alone or chit chat with my partner

text: Great  friendly staff and a cool place to relax. Had the best hot and cold chocolate drink.

text:

I choose papua beans today, with chocolate, dried fruit and hazelnut flavor notes ❤️💚

text: the manual brew and affogato is good! ms diva services is very nice and polite. overall good places to chill after work

text: Good coffee, good service, good ambience. Was served by the barista named Bryan, super nice and skillful. Thanks!

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Ikan Laut
text: First Crack Coffee delivered an outstanding coffee experience. Bryan, the barista, showcased exceptional expertise in manual brewing, providing detailed explanations of each coffee’s flavor profile and offering thoughtful recommendations tailored to my preferences. His engaging and professional demeanor elevated the overall experience. The ambience of the cafe was also impressive, well-curated, inviting, and ideal for both focused work and casual conversation. This has become one of my go to spots when I want a truly proper cup of coffee. Highly recommended for those who appreciate quality and knowledgeable service.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Monica Febriana
text:
There is no doubt, this coffee shop is famous for its coffee and delicious food. Their service is very fast and friendly.
I always enjoy their manual brew. The coffee is very good. The snack tofu is delicious and crisp.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: monica miranti
text: The coffee is good, I order hazelnut coffee no sugar and manual brew immersion beans from papua.. Very aromatic and match to my pallate.. The barista very informative and friendly, he exclusively brewing my coffee on my table. Love this place..

title: First Crack Coffee

text: Excellent coffee shop. Been here many times. Today I tried Papua Coffee served by Bryan with friendly service and explanation. This place never disappoint. Til next time.

title: First Crack Coffee

text: I have been here multiple times. From a coffee meet up in the morning, business meeting at noon and a quick catch up with nachos after 8 pm. Never had bad food or dessert.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Eleonora Natasya
text: Cozy place, delightful coffee, unexpectedly good Nasi Goreng Kampung. I have to say the cinnamon roll can be improved though. But all in all, will definitely go back to try more main course and ofc for the coffee 🥰

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Stanley The
text: Many choice of coffee beans from around Indonesia and various ways of brewing methods. Definitely go-to place for coffee lovers..

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Riky Setiawan
text: Great ambience cafe, attentive and friendly staff, spacious space (they have 2 rooms with connecting door), suitable for family and child, spacious parking lot. All the food and drink is absolutely delicious. They bring traditional indonesian food to the next level with great plating. You should try all their food. Coffee is great, and the price is affordable. One of the best cafe and resto in town.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: nadia zpay
text: What a great coffee shop! It's so calm, cool, and perfect for getting work done. I ordered a Papua manual brew, and Barista Akbar was incredibly helpful, explaining the brewing process in a way that was easy to grasp and expanded my understanding of coffee preparation. I've never been to a coffee shop where u can ask for ur coffee to be brewed right at your table that's an unique touch! The coffee itself was fragrant n truly tasty coffee.

title: First Crack Coffee

text:
Already several times visit First Crack, but this time is special because tried their manual brew coffee

This experience is more special because of their new beans called Orange Bourbons, that taste really good both serve as hot and iced

Personally really recommend this beans for those who like manual brew

Besides the barista (Mr. Bryan) that serves me the coffee also really well experience, both servings skills and several chats about manual brew coffee

text: The coffee was great, barista were nice & the ambiance also great ✨👍🏼

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Hungry Teddy
text: Great coffee place hidden inside the office area. Food was great. They managed to incorporate Indonesian flavor into their food. Spicy and delicious. The belgium chocolate was great. The orange bali coffee was unique in a way. Not as comfy and cool as other fancy cafe, but managed to deliver it well enough that I would love to visit it again to try other food. Service needed to be improved. We got two wrong orders.

text:
Unlike First Crack Coffee in Bumi …..
This First Crack Coffee is Up scale every thing, food and drinks was excellent, all in menu is available, service
This First Cack Coffee is upscale, atmosphere was cool and inviting, the lady on cashier I think she is the supervisor she speaks good English was very professional and helpful, she take the time politely and with knowledge to Explained to me different kinds of coffee beans because I’m very picky about my coffee and she help me select coffee that I like , thank you so much.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Ariyohana Saraswati
text:
My special spot is on the corner near coffee grinding machine 😊

Place - Very well design, Quite and view with green tree

Coffee - Black Coffee (for me this is the most recommended one)
We tried some variant drinks with coffee base BUT it’s not recommended for my taste.

Food : Indonesian food is recommended but not for western food

Toilet : available, clean and the mirror

Parking Space : plenty

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Christie Ananda
text: First time coming here. Ordered the Rujak Batagor and the staff suggested I ordered their Ijen manual brew, which apparently was not on their menu as if this review being written. Coffee tasted rich and food was really good. The place had a nice and calming atmosphere with music and ambient lighting. There are a few charging outlets by the bar so WFC is possible depending on how crowded the place is. Came here on a Saturday afternoon and it was surprisingly uncrowded, though staff mentions that this is not always the case so I guess you got to try your luck. Overall, great place for anyone looking for good coffee and/or a bite to eat. Will definitely come back here again.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Roi Permadi
text:
A hidden artisan coffee shop in a corner of Sunter business complex.

The coffee is superb, especially their house blend. Perfect acidity & strength. Amazing!

Mushroom soup = best in town. You can feel its honest fresh ingredients and balance seasoning. Highly recommended!

The ambience is warm, perfect for easy chat or work place, or even better: drown in your thoughts! This place is also very interesting for portraits. Grab your camera, bring your loved ones, get some beautiful shots here!

Try the coconut gayo. It is chilled, light, refreshing, and tickles your senses with a fair coconut water and a hint of gayo coffee.

The staffs are fast and friendly. Great hospitality!

This is the place, highly recommended for you to relax and enjoy the time slowly passing by. If you are a photographer, you might regret it if you don't bring your camera here.

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Dian Febriyanti
text:
pesan matcha latte & kopi susu gula aren, rasanya just ok. tapi sangat impressed dengan tempatnya. penerangannya cantik, bikin super nyaman. no music but there's a mini pond, jadi bisa dengar suara gemercik air yang menenangkan. very suitable to wfc here, there's a lot of plugs, customernya juga behave disini. the table are big enough for you to put your laptop + mouse + drink. the seat are comfy. ada outdoornya juga di lantai 2, disana banyakan yang main game bareng/sekedar nongkrong, kalau yang di lantai 1 rata2 wfc.

toiletnya super bersih dan nyaman. lengkap ada sabun, tisu, bahkan plastik kecil. musholanya proper dan luas.

harga minuman & makanan affordabe & sudah include tax, jadi ngga akan dikagetin sama billingnya. definitely will comeback later to wfc or just chillin here!

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: sevie musri
text:
aglio olio : terlalu oily, rasa msh oke lah, tp kl dicompare m hrganya aga pricey
Crispy chicken og mushroom : nth knp ga brasa mushroom saucenya, crispy c tp aga asin, tepung n daging ayamnya krg mnyatu, mashed potatonya encer bgt, n sedikit.
Pisang goreng : ekspektasi dpt pisang goreng utuh 2 atau 3 pcs, nyatanya pisangnya dibelah dua trus dipotong kecil² mgkn sekitar seukuran jari telunjuk, n b ajah.
Minumannya enak smua (butterscotch regal n cappucino)

Kl cm utk kongkow² n ga makan berat, oke c tmptnya.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Athirah Azlan
text:
Ordered their Triple Egg Hot & Spicy and Iced Chocolate, and have never tasted anything like it! Super tasty. Their menu is quite different and niche—for you to decide whether that’s a good thing if you have an adventurous palate or not as someone who personally doesn’t. Was quite a challenge for me to decide what I wanted to get thus why going for the safest option but regardless would say their food and drinks taste delicious!

It’s a pay before you eat situation but the cashier goes to you. No parking unless you own a motorcycle. Seating situation can be a bit cramped but I came in a group of 6 and still snuggled in nicely. Would come again!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Nadrah Nizom
text:
Heard about this place since ages ago, finally i got to try the famous coffee shop. It was indeed had the best quality.

I ordered pasar antik, they said it was brownsugar latte. Since i had issues with caffeine these days i ordered the mild one. It waaaas so good!!! I love it, and i had no stomach problem later on. For the pastry i actually went here to try croissant tapai, but it was out of stocked that day. Then i chose the eggtart, i like it!

Staffs was so friendly!!! The place itself was so cozy.

Prolly there will be a next time!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Fadhriano Rakamuna
text: Homy coffee shop also the food are superb !

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Endar Nugraha
text:
Bold yet nice taste coffee.
They have indoor and semioutdoor space.
Food is grear as well.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Mark Chan
text: I was in Jakarta recently for a work trip and wanted to try Gyanti's coffee. I didn't have time to go there but ordered beans on Grab to take home as a souvenir. Just my type of coffee...a darker roast and rich with a great crema!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Afianto “Ram” R
text:
- One of the most iconic coffee in Jakarta -

Pros:
- Great great coffee and they always give three options, light, medium or heavy.
- Attentive waiter and waitress.
- Comfy and homy atmosphere.
- Separated the non-smoking and smoking area well.
- Pasta taste good as well.

Cons:
- Food price is just too expensive. It's like 100k++ for any of the pasta.
- I think a more food variety would be great.

Will I go back? Yes, for sure.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Alex Regionable
text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Giyanti Coffee Roastery is truly a hidden gem in Jakarta! From the moment you step through the striking blue entrance, you are transported into a world of artistic charm and unique flair. The interior decor is a delightful array of eclectic artwork and creative touches that make every corner Instagram-worthy. The walls are adorned with vibrant paintings and rustic elements that blend modern chic with cozy comfort, creating an inviting atmosphere for all who enter.

Their coffee is consistently exceptional, with each single-origin cup boasting a distinct and rich flavor profile. What adds to the charm is the staff’s expertise and friendliness. They are eager to share their knowledge of coffee beans and brewing techniques, making every visit both educational and enjoyable.

Whether you’re a coffee enthusiast or just stopping by, Giyanti Coffee Roastery offers a memorable experience with its superb coffee and the warm, eclectic environment. Highly recommended for anyone looking for quality coffee served in a space that feels like a curated gallery of art and culture!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Vera Pujias
text: One of the unique and iconic coffee shop in jakarta! they served Indonesian Coffee. The cafe is located opposite a stretch of antique marketplace Jl. Surabaya, Jakarta and the entrance was through a corridor. The sign at the front should lead you the way in. The ambience was so rustic and homey but so narrow inside. there is a smoking area and non smoking area. the staff was very friendly and fast.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: F R
text:
3 basic coffees in plastic cups with a mediocre taste for 190k?

very misleading rating. This place is overpriced and overrated. For the price, you can easily access more spacious and comfortable coffee shops. The coffee is a joke—they're selling basic coffee at artisan prices. Nothing justifies the cost.

Go to 'Second Floor' , not far from here, to enjoy true artisan coffee.
or Ombe for some great basics.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Carlo Saian
text:
Got myself a quick catchup-over-coffee at brunch time. Ordered an Iced Sparkling Americano, Coffee Float and a Beef Pie.

Coffee was great as they have their own reputation about it, food was also great! (Minced beef is on point and the puff pastry is flaky and crispy). Their overall food & beverage price is quite high but the portion and quality is good.

Speaking about ambience: You won’t find a place with such ambience like Giyanti Coffee Roastery; homey, lots of unique ornaments. Their baristas are also friendly! Kudos to the team! 👍🏽👍🏽

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: OZYMANDIAS
text: The food and drinks are great. So many unique options and menu. The only downside is the price. It can really expensive, especially when you spend time here before your payday 🤣

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Nauvall Juliansyah
text: Went here for coffee and dessert after lunch, it is a big coffee shop with quite limited parking space but there was valet service, 10k for parking and whichever amount you want to pay for valet service. When I got in, there was a small alley leading to a big open space with several dining areas. It is always busy and I was seated in the non-smoking area. Giyanti offers wide variety of foods and drinks, we ordered latte, iced americano, carrot cake, and cheesecake. Coffee was really great! Carrot cake was nice too, cheesecake has lemon cream on top which was not really to my liking but otherwise it was a nice cheesecake as well! The ambience was great, very artsy, very homey.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Mohd Rozairi (Agent Rozairi)
text:
Truly amazing, amazing people, amazing food and amazing place. First of all the staff are ao polite and attentive. Ojie one of them who was so friendly and helpful.

The setup is cool they had aoking and non smoking area. I could remember the food i ordered, it was recommended to me. For its awsome and the portion is generous. I couldnt finished and they offered to ke twice to pack it even before i ask them.

I asked them about the beans that they are selling, they had nunerous type of beans and its premium. It is made to order. They beans was packed and bagged nicely.

No wonder this place has so many rating. Its was beautiful experience and surely recommend to others.

Good job to the owner and the staff!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Reiza Rajasa
text:
Coffee and food were exeptional. Had the beef sandwich and magic coffee, perfect!

Their service was great, staff were very friendly and helpful.

The cafe interior was eclectical, with many small nooks making it very cozy.

Would surely come back in the future

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Edwina Utama
text: It's all about coffee. They only serve coffee which is good for coffee lovers, and some desserts to company your drink. If you are not a fan of coffee, you could ask for a weak one. The place is superb. With a thoughtful industrial style interior, it's a jungle for some people who wants to update their social media. It's very photogenic, even the door is photogenic. The staffs are very nice and friendly. Both the interior and staffs were giving a vibe just like cafes in Bali. The music  plays good enough as a background, and even it's full of people, it didn't sound annoyingly noisy. Definitely not a one time visit coffee shop.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Vvianite
text: Tempat lucu bgt!! I dont drink coffee but they have plenty option for food and they are delicious! Klo maen ke Jakarta must visit krn every corner is worth to post on your socmed 😆

text: One of the best coffeeshop to go in Jakarta if you guys want to taste the serious coffee. The food and atmosphere was excellent tho

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Avijeet Sen
text:
Visited in Jan 2024

Pretty decent coffee place. Nice decorative interiors. Relatively exhaustive coffee menu where you will find something to your liking. I tried a couple of cold coffee items and they were good. The flavour/quality of the coffee is quite strong and hence make your choice wisely when deciding the ‘strength’ of your beverage.

The place has some nice cakes/pastries on the menu too. Tried the Bolu Pandan which was quite nice.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Rahadiany Rachmawaty
text:
At last, i visited this cafe that i would like to enjoy from long time ago.

Atmosfer is excellence, feels like enter different vibes of Jakarta.
Unique, ecletic, vintage, homy become one.
Customers could enter from the left side  of the building.  And enjoy every decoration that very mesmerizing.
Parking lot is few, but don't worry they have parking valet.
The have AC and non AC rooms.
Toilet, WiFi available.
But musholla you can use in the next building, there is the sign.

I only ordered magic coffee.
Like a cappucino with fewer foam milk, like flat white i think.

They will ask light, medium, or strong level.
Medium one shot, strong 2 shots.
For newbie please order light one.

I ordered strong one, it was very good, but i feel it's too strong for me.
Next time maybe should oder medium one.

Thank you for delivering a new experience.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: A L
text:
Love the Magic coffee here. I frequently buy their coffee beans — especially Blue Batak, Aceh Saman Meriah, and Flores Bajawa. Just note that you need to pay in advance before your order is served.

The food is overpriced and honestly not that impressive. It’s also quite difficult to find an electricity plug, and the music is too loud for my liking.

But I’d still give a high score for their hospitality — the service is warm and friendly.

text:
Siapa sangka ada tempat sebagus ini di Jaktim!

Sekarang gak usah jauh-jauh ke Bogor atau Puncak kalau mau cari pemandangan hijau nan syahdu. Karena Sudut Timur punya semuanya!

Berlokasi di Condet, letaknya memang agak tersembunyi di dalam gang perumahan. Namun pas sampai sana kamu gak akan percaya akan keindahan tempatnya

Sudut Timur berdiri di samping sebuah rumah yang juga beririsan dengan sungai ciliwung. Tempatnya melandai seperti turunan bukit. Kebayang kan view sungai dan bukit seperti apa?

Untuk makanan-nya gak kalah dengan pemandangan-nya. Quesadilla cocok disantap sebagai appetizer. Fettuccine Carbonara-nya super creamy. Dan bakmi goreng sei sapi nya datang dengan porsi besar. Recommended!

Harga makanan agak sedikit mahal. Es kopinya creamy, manisnya cukup. Agak kecewa karena croissant butter nya ditaburi gula halus jadi manis.

Dan untuk sei sapinya masih agak bau prengus, kurang smoky, nasinya terlalu kering, keras juga, sayurannya pada layu. Untuk pemesanannya langsung scan barcode dimejanya masing-masing. boleh ksih saran gak sih,tempat sbagus ini,tolong dong di perhatikan juga mslah parkiran nya,,hrus nya kan customer dtang tuh di arahin parkir nya sblah mana,tpi ko ini cuma nunjukin tnpa bicara smbil duduk trus yg bikin kesel nya tuh aku mau kluar sdangkan mtor di depan abang parkir nya malah asik aja duduk smbil main hp akhirnya ngluarin sendiri sampe akhirnya betis kena step mtor laen smpe bengkak giliran udah bru si abang parkir nya berdiri nungguin byran trus playanan nya apa dong mau ngmong juga gak enak soal nya abang parkir nya lebih tua jdi sdikit nghormatin juga akunya, aku sering lho ke sudut timur dulu  enak ada 3 orang abang parkir playanan nya juga ok sampe helem aja aman di plastikin kalo ujan,sekarang cuma sndiri yg parkir nya trus playanan nya gk kaya dulu  emang sih bayar nya seiklas nya gak di taktor tapi tolong lah aku pasti bayar ko ya minimal di bantuin buat ngluarin mtor gituh..

Makanan dan minumnya enk²

title: Sudut timur
name: Chindy Nurfaradila
text:
Ku baru tau ada cafe pinggir kali ini di condet, tempatnya cukup nyaman, suasananya bikin betah lama-lama untuk bengong nikmatin moment sambil dengerin musik yang diputer oleh cafenya.

Aku kesana hari kamis 4 Sept, pertama datang langsung disambut pekerja disitu dikasih tau bahwa bisa pilih mau duduk dimana aja dan langsung order di meja bisa langsung scan barcode, pokoknya ramah ramah banget, pelayanan makanan dan minumannya cepet banget datangnya pada saat itu ya 👍. Makanan dan minuman yang aku order semuanya enak enak beneran, harganya juga masih terjangkau banget.

Ada free parkiran lumayan luas, toilet ada diatas dan dibawah, ada free Wi-Fi untuk WFC 😀

Bagus untuk foto foto dan hangout bareng temen-temen bisa ngobrol seleluasa mungkin dan ketawa sekeras mungkin gak ganggu cust lain.

title: Sudut timur

text: Tempatnya enak sejuk banget, recomended buat nugas yang anak kampus ataupun mau curhat2 sabi bangettt

title: Sudut timur
name: Marcheilla Ariesta
text:
Date myself in right place. Sayang kopinya lupa di foto karena udah haus banget. But everything here makes me definitely will come again for sure. Make sure you try the lovely sunset, a mocktail with yakult based, and the sambosa for the cheese lover. For the Nasi Ayam Truffle juga enak but nothing special.
Pelayanannya sangat sangat memuaskan. Ramah dan sopan. Top!
Bisa dibilang hidden gem di tengah semerawutnya Kramat Jati dan sekitarnya.
Sayang aja, kali yang ada di depannya warnanya coklat, padahal jadi highlight tempat ini. Kalau aja bisa lebih bersih (ngarep), i think its a perfect place for healing. Sudut Timur top!

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Bunga Yuliana
text:
Ini tempat nongkrong baru anak Kalisari dan sekitarnya. Sekilas dari depan kayak rumah orang aja. Cuma ternyata di dalam nya ada coffee shop dan butik.

Ambience nya santai bgt, private, ga terlalu ramai. Cocok untuk yg lagi WFC atau sekedar mau bengong mikirin tujuan hidup.

Minumannya didominasi kopi. Rasa kopi nya lumayan, nothing so special tapi enak.

Makananya cocok untuk sharing.

Harga medium cenderung mahal untuk ukuran Kalisari. Tapi so far, dg suasana tempatnya it worth the price sih...

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Dian Retno Aryani
text:
Hari ini WFC di Kana, mencoba beberapa menu berikut: Mie Signature, Singkong Goreng, Tahu Cabe Garam—enaak! 😁✨
Minumannya pesan lemon tea dan milk tea, cukup bisa dinikmati, tapi bukan yang spesial.

Yang spesial banget adalah musholanya. Sungguh sejauh ini mushola ternyaman kafe di Kalisari jatuh kepada Kana!!! Luas, adem, wangi, ada mukena untuk perempuan yg lupa membawa alat sholat. Yang bisa diimprove adl tempat wudhunya. Mungkin bisa diperbanyak, dan dipisah antara perempuan dan laki2.

Oh ambience kafenya juga tenang. Ada suara gemericik air dari kolam, ada terminal colokan yang banyak.

So far, so good. Baarakallahu fiikum, Kana. ☀️

title: KANA Coffee & Space

text: Pelayanan nya kurang ramah, untuk makanan kurang dan pas dateng sudah dingin, kalau untuk tempatnya sudah bagus

title: KANA Coffee & Space
name: Pina Pkm
text: Suasananya enak nyaman, makanan porsi gede dan enak, pelayanan cepat.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Julius Setiadi
text: This coffee shop has a cozy atmosphere, the coffee and food are delicious, the service is friendly, and the place is clean, very suitable for hanging out or just having a relaxing coffee! Brew on table is very easy to understand by barista Zaza.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Dwi Sukma Saputra
text:
Good place for hanging around and enjoy the multiple menus and coffee options as well.

The ornaments inside are quite unique, iconic, and interesting to observe.

Staffs are so nice and willing to help as well.

The ordered menu may not take that long to serve, even during the crowded visitors

Prices aee reasonable as well.

Have a try to visit it guys....

title: First Crack Coffee
name: vHiemPlates
text:
Back here again 😁😁😁
Gotta get freebies when you review on gMaps
.
Located on a new office area, the access is quite hidden yet easy to be found.
With the coffee trends,
This place offers an infuse of Indonesian Dishes to their menu. Mie Jawa is definitely recommended!
Various coffee selections!

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Ignatius Ivan
text:
Located inside Altria Business Park, this coffee shop is nicely decorated and provided good coffee. They have range wide of selection for coffee, and the waiters were trained well enough to explain the difference between each method and beans.

They have seasonal beans for seasonal events like Christmas and such. Always packed with people but you will always find yourself a space here.

Water is free for every purchase of coffee. Price is a bit premium but definitely worth it.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: audrey nurbaashita
text: the coffee’s good. they have various beans selections from all over indonesia and the staff are friendly. very recommended

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Ferdy MD
text:
One of my fav coffee in town 🙌🏻
Excellent coffee, delicious chocolate cake n butter croissant.
Haven't tried their food yet.
Great ambiance and good services.
Very recommended for coffee lovers!

title: First Crack Coffee
name: lai echo
text: My first time I try this brand first crack at Pluit, one year ago already closed. This time just wanna find a cafe in Sunter nearby. I have find this brand in my map. No any considering that I go there. Their coffee is really good, I like this brand I like this atmosphere. They have told me that about coffee and make it at my table. Good experience here. Hope next time can eat their cake . Look delicious but Have 2 point, they are little bit noisy and Wi-Fi not stable. Hope can improve it thanks

title: First Crack Coffee
name: dntnd
text:
This is a hidden coffee shop inside an office area. You have to enter the office area in Altira.
The location is really cool because there are many trees. The place also quite comfortable for wfc.
The coffee tastes really good.
Overall this coffee shopp is really cozy.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Gita Dwiputri
text:
A great place with great ambience as well. They played a very soothing music, suitable for chill or working. It has a strong & fast WiFi, the toilet is also clean.

I ordered :
- Nasi Goreng Rendang Sambal Hijau
The portion is very big. The food is very tasty but a lil bit too oily.
- Nasi Bakar Dori
The rice is very fragrance and very tasty as well.

Although the staff is very friendly, unfortunately it take a long time to get the food & beverage.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Jundi Alwan
text:
good atmosphere for working and hangout with friends, it’s not too noisy because it is located inside Altira business complex (far from the main road)

car park spot are plenty, outside and also basement

dish is enjoyable for brunch and snacking

they always have more than 5 variety of beans ready for manual brew to try and they have 2 kinds of espresso taste, chocolatey and fruity

recommended

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Veronica Misykah
text: I had white cold brew (with ice cubes, i prefered) and cinnamons roll, what a comfy and friendly place for enjoying your free time and have some assignments. Serviced by Gisella, Wulan and Hera. Thanks, i'll come it later

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Anita Mulyadijaya
text: Nice ambience, tasty coffee, nice service. Love the foods and desserts. Very worth the price. Overall great

title: First Crack Coffee
name: IRV
text:
Best coffee in the area, good spot, tasty and delicious food and cakes.

They are serious in coffee. Good rotations, this place is a must for coffee-goers. Their non-coffee are good as well.

Salted cheesecake is superb!!!

Place good for big group as well. Might be busy at times.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Natalia Safar
text: Ordered a manual brew and was presented by Kak Akbar / Abay. He explained and presented about the brewing process as well as the coffee origins very clearly. I also love the coffee taste, overall it was a great experience. Appreciate it

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Abe Felisa
text: I always like to go here because of the ambiance. They have a tenant discount so I get to pay cheaper every time. Price is fair for an upscale cafe, food and drink are great and they serve real coffee! I like the decoration especially at Christmas. Great place to hang out with friends and not to mention the tranquil surrounding.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Nathania Griselda
text: Been a loyal customer. Love love love their coffee! Nice ambience, great place to chill with family. Plus the service here was absolutely very friendly.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Lisye Haryanto
text: We shared the klepon cake and the taste was pretty good. I also ordered the flat white and chose arabica instead arabica robusta for the coffee beans. The staff here are knowledgeable enough to explain the difference between the different types of coffee available. The ambiance here is good. This place is definitely worth visiting.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Juan Lee
text:
One of the top tier coffee shop and roastery in Jakarta
If you’re looking for good speciality coffee, You should visit this place
And the barista is also humble, You can ask the barista to pour over the coffee in ur table

title: First Crack Coffee
name: luse loe
text: This cafe have a great variety of coffee. The sfaff are really professional and knew about the variety of the coffee and gladly explain to you. This place offer you Klepon Cake. Really delicious cake, no to sweet, pandan flavour combine with coconut and brown sugar, really recommended must try. The tuna mayo croissant with omelete is also delicious. This place great for snacking, lunch, or breakfast. Located inside Altria Business Park. Trully a hidden artisan coffee shop in North Jakarta

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Steffi Kosli
text: One of my fave meeting point! Nice ambiance, fast service, GOOD coffee & highly recommended for traditional indonesian desserts 🖤 . Came here often times but this afternoon we met Mba Nisa & she’s so friendly, pleasant & informative . She gave us try their best seller Papua beans & served in V60. Thank you, Mba Nisa. It’s nice meeting you. Definitely worth every penny.👍👍👍

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Toris Lin
text: Nice place, just the coffee tastes quite strong for me. The tiramisu tastes good and not so sweet.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Mike S
text:
Considered one of the pioneers of specialty coffee in Jakarta, Giyanti excels in their brew and consistent in their craft for more than 10 years. The sandwich here is also one of the best in town with huge portion and fresh ingredients.

The owner, Hendrik Halianto still serves his customer on the ground. Nothing changes much since my first visit during their early days!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Herin
text:
Personally, to me, this is one of the few coffee shop in Jakarta that serves a great coffee.
Came here many times, never once the taste fail my expectations.
Magic, affogato, americano, all were super good.
Beans also great.
Although price honestly can be slightly higher, but taste don’t lie.

Place has its unique character. Cozy and comfy for casual talk with friends or your love one.
But parking could be super challenging unless you pay for vallet service.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Miranti Vidyasari
text:
I’ve known giyanti for quite a while but never had a chance to visit until recently.
And i could say, this is one of the best coffee by far.
The coffee has a good bitter taste, not too acidic, it’s perfectly balance.
The place is extraordinary, quiet, colorful, fun and comfy. It’s bright and airy.
I love how they design this place.
It reminds me with some of the typical coffee shop in bali.
All the corner is a picture perfect.

Best coffee, i could not wait to come again.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Muhammad Bian Arianandhika
text:
Giyanti is an established coffee institution in Jakarta. I believe their main business is supposed to be a roastery with the cafe on the side.

We tried 3 of their drinks, mine was black coffee, the server recommended ethiopian beans for a sour taste. My wife had the hot chocolate and my son had the iced chocolate.

The coffee was honestly amazing, it is the first black coffee I tasted which had a creamy texture. It was quite a satisfying feeling drinking the coffee slowly. Tried a bit of my wife's hot chocolate, they were also great and not overly sweet. My son inhaled his drink so I did not get a chance to taste, but it probably spoke to how good the drink was for him lol.

Price-wise, it was quite expensive I have to be honest. Total for 3 drinks with taxes were 200k, please make your own judgement whether the price fits for you.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Lee Lan Kam
text:
Jan 2024:
Thank you Giyanti for listening. Appreciate it very greatly. Today you served warm drinking water in a cup. 👍😃

2023 and before experiences:
One of the better place for coffee. Real coffee I mean. One of my favourite drinks is Mocha - though is has a little sweetness. Staff are very responsive and friendly. Some pastries are really delicious and fresh. The plain croissant served with butter was good, soft in the inside and crispy outside. It’s butter was creamy and taste milky which is to our preference. Almond croissant was equally good. Carrot cake was moist, and not overly sweet. Apple pie was also good, it’s served warm. If you’re a gluten intolerant (i am one), they serves gluten-free brownies, it is delicious but it is overly sweet for my preference. Also, wished that they serves warm drinking water in a glass, instead of a plastic cup. But when the owner is in the house, they would serve drinking water in a glass cup. 😊 They do sell coffee beans too. The interior and decorations are creatively decorated. Colourful interior too. They wouldn’t accept reservations through a phone call. You’ll be asked to make your reservation via Whatsapp. And there’s an upfront payment when making a reservation. Btw, wifi is available but it is limited usage, which is alright and fair.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Benjamin Tan
text: Very strong coffee here. It feels like a double shot even if it is single (strength: medium)  according to the staff. Good for people who enjoy really strong coffee of dark roast profile. Very instagramable place but no professional photography equipment allowed. Not a place for specialty coffee.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Kai Ancheta
text: Enjoyed the coffee so much. Definitely worth the price and the visit. I get why this cafe is so popular.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Dea Florensia
text:
Every time I’m in Central Jakarta, this coffee shop is a must-visit for me. The combination of great coffee and a cozy atmosphere makes it an unbeatable spot to relax or catch up on work.

While I usually prefer unsweetened coffee, their Biscoff Latte is absolutely irresistible – rich, creamy, and perfectly balanced. It’s a drink I simply can’t skip.

The prices are a bit on the higher side, but considering the prime location in Menteng, it’s completely reasonable. For the quality and experience you get, it’s worth every penny. Can’t wait to come back!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: TTHP
text: It’s been a long time since my last visit to Giyanti, this place keep hold it’s reputation as one of the finest coffee shop in Jakarta. Beans are good. Almost all the coffee is espresso based

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Ignatius Ivan
text:
One of the best coffee shop in town. It's very australian style. They do have both indoor and outdoor setting. Too bad they close at 5.30 PM.

The parking space it quite small although they do offer valet services. Price is a bit expensive but definitely worth it.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: christina Oktaviola
text:
The coffee shop, that I happy I have ever known and found,.
It was so aesthetic, authentic, eye-catching, and good ambiance.

The Coffee was good, the dessert too. Yes, the price bit expensive, but it paid with the ambiance and views.

I don't know why they close so early, I hope could be longer.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Hannah Glathar
text: One hundred percent my favorite coffee shop and hang place in Jakarta!! The coffee is 🤩🤩🤩 and the vibes are amazing. I usually get the iced white coffee but I've had others and they're also good. My fav food here is the egg tart. And I've been super happy that they've increased their hours in the past year. Love u giyanti ❤️

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Barry Abdul
text:
Still the best coffee in Jakarta, my 1st recommendation for anyone who wants to enjoy sipping coffee...

This is a coffee-first Cafe, and it's never disappoint. They roast their own beans and I always find them to be perfectly executed. The house blend also always give me satisfying taste of perfect coffee.

Been a regular for almost 10 years, the place has been expanding as well as their food menus. Now they have 2 indoor areas and a smoking area which very nicely designed. Foods varies from only simple pastry to main course to deserts. Service always been OK, no major issue.

My coffee recommendations are Americano, Picollo and Mocca Melt (moccachino).

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Tara Ayesha (Seichro)
text:
Still one of the best coffee places I've known!
The coffee here is absolutely delicious, so are the pastries and cakes. and they even serve kids-friendly menu too. The place itself is pretty comfortable to hang out in.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: weda
text:
Oh I freaking love this place.

A perfect place to enjoy your day off or just hanging out with your friends.

Their ice coffee is good. The croissant is good. And...they give a free flow water! That's usually a sign for a good coffee alert and I was right. Spent around 100k for a coffee and a croissant.

They have indoor and outdoor areas too. (No smoking indoors)

Overall, it's a great place.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: BboyPaul 180
text:
I would have given this place five stars if it wasn't for the incredibly rude & incompetent security guard who worked the morning shift on December 29th. Giyanti was closed today which I wasn't aware of. First, the guard waves me through, so I think "great". Then runs after me and stops me at the Giyanti hallway telling me to leave because the place is closed. So I leave accordingly, but when I walk off he stares at me, then gives me the middle finger while laughing.

I love the coffee at Giyanti. I used to come here regularily. Servers have always been very polute to me & my family as well, but are these the kinds of people they hire to guard their parameters? Re-evaluation of the security guard that worked today would be appropriate.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Marlie38
text: Good ambience, nice place, excellence service.. clean environment. Good taste of coffee, nutella croissant is one of my favorite. One of the best coffee shop, comfortable place for hanging out with friends or just enjoying your day off. Instagramable almost in every spot. Yess, surely be back!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Saira Saima
text:
Nice details of ornament, so artsy❤️. There is even a Ganesh statue at the front, when you enter the building.
For the second floor of non smoking area, the space is quite small but still enjoyable. It felt warm to be here, if you need to be focus it would be better to bring your earphone since the noise kind of loud with full people.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery

Their black coffee and on the rock are to die for! Damn! We hanged out for more that two hours and tried their hot black coffee, iced black coffee,  and on the rock. All are perfect. Strong as we requested 🙌🏻
The service was top notch and I love the ambience; artsy and cozy.
I did not try the carrot cake this time but I remembered it was good.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: David Christian
text:
This place is hidden inside a ruko, and parking-wise is very convenient. From its surrounding, this looks like a rather low-key coffee shop. Once I entered, I found this place to be fairly spacious. However hardly any personal-sized tables appropriate for solo working, with proper height. I ended up sitting on a stool next to a long, shared table, which I'm not a huge fan of.

Interior design is superficially nice with black accent and some exposure to natural light. However I feel like the atmosphere's a bit too noisy even when there were not too many guests around. Idk, perhaps it's combination of the background music and people's chatters. Not a good place for solo working. More for casual hang outs and chit chats.

I ordered Flat White and a Spaghetti Matah Aglio brunch for a combined 94k, which is not that expensive. Their coffee is decent, and the way they use milk was appropriate. Their spaghetti was combined with sambal matah and aglio olio, and I felt like it was too oily for my liking. Portion was relatively small too. Still, a decent brunch companion I would say.

If you live around Kemayoran/Sunter/Klp Gading, and you're looking for a nice place to hang out, this is one alternative you should consider. But if you live outside this neighborhood and look for a quiet place to work, don't make the same mistake I did by going the distance to come here.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: CLAUDIA
text:
Interesting coffee shop, it is located inside a business park and since it's a park so there are plenty of parking spots.

The aglio olio is not so good maybe just 1/5 and it's a bit spicy but not written on the menu. No wonder it didn't get a thumbs up but there's a surprising no thumbs up good menu!!! Try the bala-bala pizza mozzarella, I'll rate it 4.5/5 (the 0.5 is because it's too deep-fried) but really this one should get a 👍
And another interesting one is the hot tea! It uses a magic mug! Haha. Also the taste is 5/5.

This is a coffee shop but I haven't tried their coffee, maybe next time.

Points on: The phone number is working. The service is nice, you can ask for a candle or maybe two :p
Hint: they have each more than one entrance and exit gates on two different roads. So, if one is closed go to the other side :D

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Nbaity Zelly
text: Good food, good service, good ambience, keep it up First Crack Coffee Sunter

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Michelle Ruth Natalie
text:
Coffee's great! The beans vary & are high-quality.
Chill & cozy ambience.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Eric Santoso
text: Hands down the best coffee in northeast Jakarta, I’ve been coming here regularly for the past 5-6 years as i used to live around the area, now that i am living out of town whenever i come back to Jakarta, i will definitely come here for my caffeine boost, to me their food is so-so but its a great place for meeting your friends or for business meetings, environment has always been lovely, I didn’t get to try their food though, however their cold-brew tasted amazing also , professional barista and waitresses.🙏🙏👍👍👍

title: First Crack Coffee
name: luzzu wang
text:
Was told that this were established in 2017. It is nicely located within office buildings area. Love the interiors there, ambience also cozy esp if not too crowded.
Tried the long black coffee, nasi bumbu bali n Taro coconut cake. The nasi was well marinated, delicious whilst the cake is fluffy n moist, paired well with the black coffee.
Staffs were helpful n having a good product knowledge.
This premise also provide packaged coffee beans,  souvenirs ie hampers, tshirt etc.
Will be back for sure...

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Sanjay L Bhojwani
text: A small quaint place in a business district - top marks for Ambience - many clients come here to discuss business or have meetings - coffee - huge menu and choices - Atmosphere - welcoming and easy and quiet DESPITE the number of clients - Food - Tasty - Staff - friendly and easy going - this is my goto when i want to have meeting with friends or clients and time just flies - :)

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Wukong
text: Sophisticated and cozy cafe in Kelapa Gading. They are pretty serious about their coffee offerings. I tried their Cappuccino with arabica / robusta mix. Its delicious, smooth strong chocolatie flavor. The ham and cheese croissants also very good. Highly recommended if you are the area.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Midnight Pass Studio
text:
Located in Altira Business Park, the entry is located after Pertamina gas station.

Place is good, spacious and nicely decorated. But it’s too dark, too many mosquitoes and lighting is awkwardly positioned. Some how hurts the eye in some position. Seating mostly for hang out, if you are cafe hopper but looking for space to work, this place is not to go to. Not that many electrical outlet position.

Menu is a little bit confusing. No pastries around (well there was one type, but looked very old), they have indonesian food menu. Very clashing for a specialty coffee shop concept, where the smell of the kitchen and food too strong.

Coffee bar was nicely decorated and equipped. Coffee brew was okay, but not my favorite. Most of the coffee stocked were Indonesian coffee.

I heard this place is mostly for coffee learning space, but the indonesian food smell from the kitchen is too strong.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Benedict Sant
text: I recently visited this cafe to unwind and enjoy a quiet moment. The place has a luxurious vibe, and the relaxing atmosphere makes it perfect for anyone looking to take a break and savor their coffee.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Meidy Vinola Widjaja
text: nice ambience and very good coffee. will definitely come back for sure.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Louis Rizaly
text:
Visited the place quite often, grew to like the ambiance and a fan of their specialty coffee. Bonus points cause they'll reward you with discounts if you come often enough.
However from what I've experienced and observed, the staff members seemingly have an easier time granting discounts to customers they have fondness toward, rather than your typical frequent visitor. But take this anecdote for what it is.
All in all, good place to chill and enjoy coffee but the staff members left a sour aftertaste in my mouth.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Alvina Joe
text: Cant say its a lovely place to cozy up with a good book (unless you have your headset bluetooth). Its crowded with people chatting and working on laptop. First impression... indonesian version of starbuck (and its quite pricey compared to other local coffee brand). But the coffee is good and it's worth it.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Eric
text:
My most recommended place to get a good cup of coffee when you are in North Jakarta.

Out of all places I’ve tried in the area, this place serves the best balance for quality of their food and coffee.

I love their decor, it is rustic and industrial looking. Place is also usually packed during lunch, suggest to visit after lunch time if you need a quiet place to work. Smoking area is available outside on their outdoor sitting area.

Parking sometimes are hard to find as they share the spaces with other businesses in the complex.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Rakha Lazuardi
text: First Crack Coffee is an absolute gem! From the moment I stepped in, the aroma of freshly roasted coffee beans enveloped me, creating an inviting and cozy ambiance. The baristas were friendly and knowledgeable, recommending the perfect coffee for my taste. I opted for their signature pour-over, and it was a delightful experience; rich, smooth, and packed with flavor. The rustic decor and comfortable seating made it an ideal spot to relax or catch up with friends. Whether you're a coffee aficionado or just looking for a great cup, First Crack Coffee is a must-visit destination for any coffee lover.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Abdulaziz S A
text: Great place for specialty coffee lovers. High quality coffee beans roasted locally, Skilled Baristas and high end equipments. I had espresso & flat white and both were great 👍

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Artika Sari
text: Good coffee, good experiences, best service 👍

title: First Crack Coffee
name: ijull panjull
text: Great place for coffee lovers... the coffee is tasty, n the snacks are ok, from cakes to traditional like singkong n bakwan goreng. Good ambience, only the place is somewhat hidden, inside the office bld.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Kevin Kegan Kusuma
text: I remember the first time I came to this cafe. It was my office subordinate that took me here for the first time. Then, I felt in love with this place. Many foods here really suit my taste. I like their spaghetti very much, and combine it with their signature coffee really will brighten my day. I used to visit this place when I met with my friend, talking about work, life or only want to chit chat.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: rehanny lusiana
text: FC introducing their new arabica blend “es teh melati”. It’s medium light, floral, well balanced. One of my favorite👍

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Andrew Yoseph
text: Nice coffee shop! It has a cozy atmosphere with plenty of tables in various locations. The staff is friendly, and my wife really enjoys the coffee. The portions are generous too. Highly recommend!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Wisnu Bramantyo
text: I ordered pasar antik coffee, it's basically a palm sugar coffee and condensed milk but it's different because you can request for the coffee level from soft to strong. The lemon cheese  cake had a balance between creamy, sour and sweet. The parking lot is quite large for cars and bikes. The wifi connection was also fine and the place is very nice for hanging out or working.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Ju Chen
text:
6pm come all food out of service, and I order drink 55k on menu , but in the end total charge 70k, and they say it’s additional tax & service fee. (Even without a cup of water)
I would say this is the worst & expensive cafe experience during all trip.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Andy Widopo
text: One of the hidden coffee shops in Jakarta with great vibes for you outdoor lovers. Green and full of shades and options for seating, it's a treat for individual or small groups to work, chat, or read a book. The coffee was quite expensive and way too hot for my taste, however the latte art was decent. I bought a 250gr of Si Raja Biak Biak coffee beans and it's quality meets the price. One more important thing is they only accept cashless payment.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Seyberan
text: One of the best looking cafe that i’ve been to here in JKT. The atmosphere is cozy and brings you to a different vibe immediately. They made use of their limited space very well, with different seating areas for different needs, like to smoke or for casual seating.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Anindya Silmi
text: This coffee shop is quite unique. I really enjoy the taste of their coffee. You can choose light/medium/strong amount of caffeine for your coffee. Their service also very nice 👌

name: Miguel L
text: Last minute decision to visit this cafe, as my original plan was to go to Common Grounds nearby. Good thing I visited. Cool staff, great coffee and pastries. I bought six bags of beans to bring home.

name: N F
text: came here on the weekend with my baby, it was full so we waited for almost 15 mins, then they let us sit on the sofa to keep the baby happy! plus point  we got to chit and chat with the owner, Mr. H. tbh, the coconut latte was good but the ambience was even better!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery

text: Advised by a local and liked it. Their roasted beans are delicious and fresh. Strong taste with different notes on each coffee.

3 years ago…….
So crowded even on the weekend, should add up more menu specially for drink because its coffee only on the list. Many hidden and instagramable spots if you are looking for content

Now…
Iam coming back here again after 3years..
Many improvements.. so i change the star from only three to five stars

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Gaius Leong
text:
Best coffee thus far in JKT.

We had an iced latte (4/5) and a flat white (4.5/5). Ordered pastries - two crombolini (croissant roll with chocolate/pistachio) and they were quite spectacular (5/5) *note that they misnamed it as matcha when in fact it was pistachio.

Also got the goreng pisang and though it was quite oily, it tasted fresh and not too batter-y (3.5/5). Still prefer the Singaporean goreng pisang types.

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Nada Salsa
text: this is probably an og coffee shop in jakarta! the coffee was so good, and the croissants is well baked, crunchy on the outside and creamy and soft inside. i take the croissants to lampung and its still good tho. was the best coffee and croissant experince, i would probably come back!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Adit Nagata
text:
Always be my fav coffee shop. Place is quite wide. Have outdoor and indoor but lack of parking space. So park on the side of the road. Which is quite safe i guess

I ordered latte with home blend. Uh lalala. Very very smells good excelent. Not acid and a bit bitter i like. After taste is light and no remaining spot on tounge

Definitely back here again

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery
name: Zahra Wijayanti
text: Convenient coffee shop in Jakarta, suitable for going with friends or family. What I like is Giyanti Coffee has a choice of coffee flavor levels: Light, Medium, or Strong. So, there are a lots of options for customers coffee types. I’ve tried Flat White, Mocchacino, Ice Camel and Ice Pasar Antik, all delicious! The desserts and pastries are also delicious, I’ve tried Cheese Brownies, Canele, Butter Croissant. However, for me the Butter Croissant was a bit dry. The atmosphere and interior are good, there are two areas for smoking and non-smoking, but I always choose the non-smoking area in the corner near the barista & coffee machine. There are priority seats provided for the elderly, pregnant women, disabled, etc. Good service!

title: Giyanti Coffee Roastery

text: The place is quite hidden, but it’s very well decorated inside. I ordered piccolo and kouign-amann and both was good, but quite pricey. If you’re planning to work from cafe, I don’t think the place quite fitting. But if you’re planning to just wind down and have a chit chat, it’s is a comfortable place to do so.

title: First Crack Coffee
name: Bernard Citra
text:
First Crack Sunter Review

Ambiance:
The atmosphere at First Crack Sunter is welcoming and cozy. The interior design, choice of lighting, and seating arrangement create a comfortable and inviting space for coffee enthusiasts and casual visitors alike

Service:
The staff at First Crack Sunter are attentive and friendly. They make you feel right at home, whether you're a regular or a first-time visitor. Their knowledge of coffee and willingness to assist in choosing the right brew is commendable. Specially mention for Ayu who served the Mentari Senja, friendly and knowledgeable.

Coffee Quality:
The highlight of First Crack Sunter is undoubtedly its coffee. The "Mentari Senja manual brew" offers a delightful, well-balanced flavor profile with bold taste light body, fruitfull and with a hint of chocolate. The dedication to quality is evident in every sip, making it a must-try for coffee aficionados.

Cappuccino:
The cappuccino at First Crack Sunter is a work of art. The espresso is expertly crafted and balanced with velvety steamed milk, resulting in a creamy and rich coffee experience. The latte art on top is not only visually appealing but also a testament to the barista's skill.

Overall Experience:
First Crack Sunter is a hidden gem for coffee lovers. From the exceptional coffee to the welcoming ambiance, it's a place that encourages you to savor each moment. Whether you're looking for a peaceful spot to work or a cozy corner to catch up with friends, this coffee shop delivers an experience worth returning for.

title: KOCIL Stonewell (Sumur Batu)
name: Endang Norma Indah
text:
Datang di hari senin & selesai treatment, iseng jalan kaki cari coffee shop terdekat dgn map akhirnya sampai disini.
Tempatnya cukup nyaman, depan coffee shop banyak tanaman.
Saya memesan hot coffee latte & nasi goreng tuna, enak ya walau kurang sedikit asin.
Penyajian rapi, peralatan makan & minum bersih juga proper.
Untuk harga sesuai menurut saya.
Ada toilet dan cukup nyaman bila sambil bekerja disini (saat tidak ramai pengunjung ya & anak2 kecil)
Point of view yg menarik buat saya juga rak bukunya, keren, sambil menunggu pesanan bisa membaca.
Staff ramah, tidak membuat customer memesan terburu-buru.
Pembayaran mudah cash / qris / debit.
Bila membawa mobil sedikit sulit untuk parkir ya saat weekend.
Sukses selalu kocil sumur batu.

title: KOCIL Stonewell (Sumur Batu)
name: Andri Tanuwijaya
text:
Minggu, 2 Februari 2025 (13.00)
Good place to hang out/ wfc :)

Waktu datang gaada antrian dan banyak kursi yang masih kosong.
Aku pesan segelas filter coffee, datang dalam +- 11 menit.
Manual brewnya konsisten, selalu enak baik hot maupun japanesenya.

Colokan tersedia hampir di setiap tempat duduk, suhu ruangannya pas cocok untuk WFC. Outdoor smokingnya juga cukup luas.
Musik ngga terlalu kenceng, cocok untuk ngobrol.
Toiletnya bersih (sangat penting), dan ketersediaan parkirnya cukup luas (+- 5 mobil dan 14 motor)
Ada beberapa buku yang cukup menarik buat bahan bacaan (ngga cuma pajangan)

*Disclaimer, penulis bukan barista, q grader, maupun chef. Semua ulasan ditulis sesuai opini penulis pada saat datang.

title: KOCIL Stonewell (Sumur Batu)
name: Rizky Agung Laksono
text: Ordered hot cafe latte, enak tapi kurang panas. Makanannya enak-enak, mac n cheesenya recommended. Tempatnya sangat nyaman terutama kursi yang ada bantalnya. Very recommended buat nugas atau WFH.

title: KOCIL Stonewell (Sumur Batu)
name: Intan Putri Permata Sari
text: Cafe paling comfy, enak ga terlalu rame dan bising. Cocok buat yang cari santai dan ketenangan. Toilet bersih dan harum cermin di wastafel cukup besar, buat ciwi2 mau mirror selfie oke banget. Ada pilihan indoor dan outdoor. Terakhir kesini waktu itu belum update menu dan terkesan boring. Tapi sekarang udah update menu baik dari segi pastry/makananan beratnya. Minuman juga sudah update. Ohiya free wifi juga. Cocok buat WFC dan lama2 disini👍🏻👍🏻
